<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999975 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00035: (2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard

- page_id: `303999975`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999975/2026x+Refresh1+Actual+Resources+Deployment+Creation+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:32.209+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['deployment', 'wizard', 'actual-resources', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This wizard is used to create the following tables:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) CV-5 Capability to Organizational Development Mapping' space=''] (DoDAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with StV-5 table' space=''] (MODAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Modifying NCV-5 table' space=''] (NAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space=''] (UAF)

To open the **Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard**

- Do one of the following :
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram, and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard opens.

If you do not wish to use this wizard next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create <diagram name>** check box.

To restore the usage of the**Diagram Creation Wizard**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show St-Rm Actual Deployment/CV-5/StV-5/NCV-5 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

The wizard consists of six steps:

truenone

##### Specifying the name and owning package

In this step, you can select the existing or create a new owner for the created diagram. You can also type the diagram name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting Actual Enterprise Phase

In this step, select the existing or create a new Actual Enterprise Phase which will be represented as a key element in the table.

You can select (or create) instances or types. If you click the **Type** radio button and choose Enterprise Phase, the Actual Enterprise Phase is created automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Enterprise Phase

1. In the Select Actual Enterprise Phase step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a resource.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Enterprise Phase, type its name.

##### Specifying start date

In this step, specify the start date for the selected Actual Enterprise Phase.

When you select the Actual Enterprise Phase with the defined Start and End dates, those dates are selected in the calendar. Although, the Actual Enterprise Phase might not have any dates defined, or defined only the Start date. If you specified the End date for the Actual Enterprise Phase, you need to define the Start date also.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Specifying end date

In this step, specify the end date for the selected Actual Enterprise Phase.

If the Actual Enterprise Phase has the End date defined, this date is selected in the calendar. The End date cannot be earlier than the Start date.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting Capabilities

In this step, select existing or create new Capabilities which will be represented as columns in the table.

Once Capabilities are added, the Phases relationships between Actual Enterprise Phases and Capabilities are created.

If the Actual Enterprise Phases already have Phases relationships with Capabilities, they will be displayed in the **Selected Elements** section.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Capability

1. In the Select Capabilities step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a Capability.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Capability, type its name.

##### Selecting Actual Responsible Resources

In this step, select existing or create new Actual Responsible Resources which will be represented as rows of the table.

You can select (or create) instances or types. If you click the **Type** radio button and choose an Organizational Resource (Organization, Person, or Post), an instance for that Organizational Resource is created automatically and added in the **Selected Elements** section (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Responsible Resource

1. In the Select Actual Responsible Resources step, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create an Actual Responsible Resource.
3. Click the Create button.
4. Select the needed Actual Responsible Resource.
5. In the Specification window of the newly created Actual Responsible Resource, type its name.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999970 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00036: (2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Deployment Wizard

- page_id: `303999970`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999970/2026x+Refresh1+Actual+Resources+Deployment+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:31.696+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['deployment-wizard', 'actual-resources-deployment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of the **Actual Resources Deployment Wizard** is to deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability.

To open the **Actual Resources Deployment Wizard** do the following

1. Open a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating CV-5 table' space=''] (DoDAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with StV-5 table' space=''] (MODAF), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating NCV-5 table' space=''] (NAF), or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space=''] (UAF) table.
2. Double-click a cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
3. Click the + button that appears on the right side of the cell. The input data for the wizard is the Capability to be deployed and the Actual Organizational Resource responsible for that Capability deployment.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Select Actual Resources

In this step, select the Actual Resourcesrequired by the Actual Responsible Resources to accomplish the Capability. You can search for existing ActualResources or create new ones.

You can select (or create) instances or types. If you click the**Type**radio button and choose an Organizational Resource (Organization, Person, or Post), an instance for that Organizational Resource is created automatically and displayed in blue in the**Selected Elements**section (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Deployment date specification

In this step, you can specify the Deployment date and time for the selected Actual Resource configuration to be deployed and used. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Setting** dialog. By default, the Today date is selected.

If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates can be reused (select a check box next to the name of the Actual Enterprise Phase) and the date selection steps are not available.

The Deployment date for the Actual Resource usage can not be later than the No Longer Used date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Actual Resources Deployment Wizard** wizard only if the start date is specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### No longer used date specification

In this step, you can specify the No Longer Used date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be no longer used. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates are reused.

The No longer Used date for the Actual Resourceusage can not be earlier than the Deployment date of the table context. You will be able to finish the **Actual Resources Deployment Wizard** wizard if the end date is not specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Elements that can beActual Resources are listed in the following table.

| Domain | Actual Resources | Types of Actual Resources |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Security | Actual ResourceActual Resource | Security EnclaveResource Mitigation |
| Resources | Actual ResourceActual ResourceActual ResourceActual ResourceFielded CapabilityActual ResourceActual Resource | Natural ResourceTechnologySoftwareResource ArtifactCapability ConfigurationSystemResource Architecture |
| Projects | Actual Project | Project |
| Personnel | Actual ResponsibilityActual PersonActual PostActual Organization | ResponsibilityPersonPostOrganization |
| Operational | Actual Resource | Known Resource |
| Actual Resources | Actual PostActual PersonActual OrganizationActual ResponsibilityFielded CapabilityActual Resource | Actual PostActual PersonActual OrganizationActual ResponsibilityFielded CapabilityActual Resource |

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating CV-5 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with StV-5 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating NCV-5 table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The purpose of the <strong>Actual Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> is to deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Actual Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> do the following</p><hr /><ol><li>Open a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating CV-5 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[CV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (DoDAF), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with StV-5 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (MODAF), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating NCV-5 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[NCV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (NAF), or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Actual Deployment]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (UAF) table.</li><li>Double-click a cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears on the right side of the cell.<br />The input data for the wizard is the Capability to be deployed and the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Actual Organizational Resource </span>responsible for that Capability deployment.</li></ol><p>The wizard consists of three steps:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="afd20b6b-1ac7-4aff-af69-d2944c091208"><ac:parameter ac:name="outline">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="style">none</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Select Actual Resources</h3><p>In this step, select the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Resources </span>required by the Actual Responsible Resources to accomplish the Capability. You can search for existing <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Resources</span></span> or create new ones.</p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can select (or create) instances or types. If you click the </span><strong>Type</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> radio button and choose an Organizational Resource (Organization, Person, or Post), an instance for that Organizational Resource is created automatically and displayed in blue in the </span><strong>Selected Elements</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> section (see the following figure).</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_resources_deployment_wizard_step1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Deployment date specification</h3><p>In this step, you can specify the Deployment date and time for the selected Actual Resource configuration to be deployed and used<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Date and Time Settings dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Date and Time Setting</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. By default, the Today date is selected.</p><p>If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">can be reused (select a check box next to the name of the Actual Enterprise Phase)</span> and the date selection steps are not available. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2aeaf087-6e32-4bfb-8357-ed8801f7246a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Deployment date for the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Resource</span> usage can not be later than the No Longer Used date of the table context. You will be able to finish the <strong>Actual Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> wizard only if the start date is specified.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_resources_deployment_wizard_step2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>No longer used date specification</h3><p>In this step, you can specify the No Longer Used date and time for the selected Resource configuration to be no longer used<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">. For the date and time specification, the wizard provides you with the Date and Time Setting dialog. If the dates are defined in the corresponding Actual Enterprise Phase, those dates are reused.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22148d28-e480-4577-83a3-a50b5eb00146"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The No longer Used date for the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Actual Resource </span>usage can not be earlier than the Deployment date of the table context. You will be able to finish the <strong>Actual Resources Deployment Wizard</strong> wizard if the end date is not specified.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="actual_resources_deployment_wizard_step3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>Elements that can be<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Actual Resources</span> are listed in the following table.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 48.6012%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 36.2712%;" /><col class="" /><col class="" style="width: 34.4633%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Domain</th><th colspan="1"><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Actual Resources</span><br /></span></th><th><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Types of Actual Resources</span></th></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Security</strong></p></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Actual Resource</li><li>Actual Resource</li></ul></td><td><ul><li>Security Enclave</li><li>Resource Mitigation</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Resources</strong></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Actual Resource</li><li>Actual Resource</li><li>Actual Resource</li><li>Actual Resource</li><li>Fielded Capability</li><li>Actual Resource</li><li>Actual Resource</li></ul></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Natural Resource</li><li>Technology</li><li>Software</li><li>Resource Artifact</li><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>System</li><li>Resource Architecture</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Projects</strong></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Actual Project</li></ul></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Project</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Personnel</strong></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Actual <span>Responsibility</span></li><li>Actual Person</li><li>Actual Post</li><li>Actual Organization</li></ul></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Responsibility</li><li>Person</li><li>Post</li><li>Organization</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Operational</strong></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Actual Resource</li></ul></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Known Resource</li></ul></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Actual Resources</strong></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Actual Post</li><li>Actual Person</li><li>Actual Organization</li><li>Actual Responsibility</li><li>Fielded Capability</li><li>Actual Resource</li></ul></td><td colspan="1"><ul><li>Actual Post</li><li>Actual Person</li><li>Actual Organization</li><li>Actual Responsibility</li><li>Fielded Capability</li><li>Actual Resource</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating CV-5 table" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page 760209065
````

760209076

760209075

**Description**

The Actual Resource Parameters (Ar-Pm) domain depicts the measurements of resources.

The intended usage of the The Actual Resource Parameters (Ar-Pm) domain includes:

- Definition of performance characteristics and measurements.
- Identification of non-functional requirements.

**Implementation**

The Actual Resource Parameters (Ar-Pm) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Typical Measurements table' space=''].
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Actual Measurements table' space=''] .
- Actual Measurements (.xlsx).

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997282 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00038: (2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Structure

- page_id: `303997282`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997282/2026x+Refresh1+Actual+Resources+Structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:59.459+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

832612334

832612336

832612335

**Description**

An Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) domain describes the analysis, e.g. evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&V on the actual resource configurations as it provides a means to capture different solution architectures. The detailed analysis (trade-off, what-if etc.) is carried out using the Resource Constraints view. It illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations required to meet an operational need.

An Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) domain is divided in two forms:

- Typical (e.g., a typical brigade command structure). It shows the possible relationships between organizational resources. The key relationship is composition, i.e., one organizational resource being part of a parent organization. In addition to this, the architect may show the roles each organizational resource has, and the interactions between those roles, i.e., the roles represent the functional aspects of organizational resources. The intended usage of the typical form of the Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) view includes:
  - Organizational analysis.
  - Definition of human roles.
  - Operational analysis.
- Actual (e.g., an organization chart for a department or agency). It shows the structure of a real organization at a particular point in time. The intended usage of the actual form of the Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) domain includes:
  - Identify architecture stakeholders.
  - Identify process owners.
  - Illustrate current or future organization structures.

**Implementation**

Actual Resources Structure (Ar-Sr) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Actual Resources Structure diagram' space=''].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Actual Resources Structure diagram

832612333

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997297 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00039: (2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources Taxonomy

- page_id: `303997297`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997297/2026x+Refresh1+Actual+Resources+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:00.872+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Actual Resources
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1476440482

1476440483

1476440483

**Description**

The Actual Resources Taxonomy (Ar-Tx) domain illustrates the actual organizational structure and relationships among actual organizations, actual posts, and actual persons filling those actual post, that are the key players in the architecture.

This domain describes the hierarchy of control within actual organizations, between actual posts and actual persons filling those actual posts that affect the architecture and how it is used. It is the instance version of the personnel structure which defines the types of organizations and post, etc.

**Implementation**

Actual Resources Taconomy (Ar-Tx) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Actual Resource Taxonomy Matrix' space=''].

1476440482

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999999 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00040: (2026x Refresh1) Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard

- page_id: `303999999`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999999/2026x+Refresh1+Actual+Strategic+Phase+Assignment+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:34.793+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['assign-actual-person', 'actual-post', 'personnel-availability', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

276394815

INLINE

276394817

INLINE

276394816

INLINE

The **Actual Strategic Phase Wizard** is used to assign an Actual Strategic Phase.

To open the **Actual Strategic Phase****Assignment Wizard**

1. Open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart' space=''] .
2. Do one of the following:
  - In the chart toolbar, click the Add New button and choose Add New Enterprise Mission , Add New Actual Enduring Task , Add New Whole Life Enterprise , Add New Actual Enterprise Phase , or Add New Value Stream .
  - In the chart toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
  - In the chart, right-click a selected Actual Strategic Phase and choose Add New Sub Actual Strategic Phase or Add Existing Sub Actual Strategic Phase .
  - The Actual Strategic Phase Assignment Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### **Strategic Phase**selection

In this step, you can select a Strategic Phase in three available ways:

- Select an existing Strategic Phase as a type from the suggested list .
- Create a new Strategic Phase as a type by clicking the Creation Mode button.
- Create an instance without a type, choose UNSPECIFIED.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create an Actual Strategic Phase

1. In the Select Strategic Phase dialog, click the Creation Mode .
2. Select the package where you want to create a Strategic Phase.
3. Click the Create button.
4. In the Specification window of the newly created Strategic Phase, type its name.

##### Start date specification

In this step, you can set the start date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the Actual Strategic Phase starts.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### End date specification

In this step, you can set the end date (or select the date from the Timeline) when the Actual Strategic Phase ends.

For more information on setting the day and time, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Date and Time Settings dialog' space='']**Date and Time Settings** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1587085761

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998674 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00042: (2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information

- page_id: `303998674`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998674/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+a+Macro+and+editing+Macro+information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:53.350+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Macro engine
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

851898320

851898333

851898317

**On this page:**

851898322

You can add a new macro and enter all necessary information about it by following the steps described in****Opening Macro Information Dialog and**** Adding a Macro and Its Information below.

##### Opening Macro Information Dialog

You can add or modify macro information such as the macro name and description, in the **Macro Information** dialog. To open the **Macro Information** dialog, you need to open the **Organize Macros** dialog first.

To open the **Organize Macros** dialog

- Click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros on the main menu. The Organize Macros dialog will open, see figures below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Macro Information** dialog

1. Click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros on the main menu to open the Organize Macros dialog, see above figure.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The Macro Information dialog will open.

##### Adding a Macro and Its Information

Use the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** or **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** button in the **Organize Macros** dialog to add or edit a macro and its information in the **Macro Information** dialog. You can also press the mnemonic keys to add or edit a macro, see****Macro Information Dialog Mnemonic Keys.

To add a macro and enter macro information in the **Macro Information** dialog

1. Open the Organize Macros dialog (click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros on the main menu).
2. Click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**. The **Macro Information** dialog will open, see figure below. The **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**, **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**, **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**, and **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** buttons in the **Organize Macros** dialog will be disabled if there is no macro in the macro table or if you do not select any macro from the table. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Type the macro name in the Name box.
4. The default macro language you have previously selected (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting a default macro language' space=''] ) will appear in the Macro Language box.
5. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='button-1.png'] button, to locate a macro file. The Open file dialog will open, see figure below.
6. Select the file and its type (there are 5 types of file filter: *.bsh , *.groovy , *.rb , *.js , or *.py ), see figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='open-dialog.png']
7. Click Open, see figure above. The selected pathname will appear in the File box in the Macro Information dialog, see figure below. [ATTACHMENT filename='fig 8.jpg'] [ATTACHMENT filename='macro-information.png']
8. Select either (i) the **Use path variables** check box or (ii) the **Add macro to model** check box. If you have specified the file or network path in the **Environment Options** dialog by clicking **Options** > **Environment** > **Path** **Variables** and selected the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box in **Macro Information** dialog, the <Path Variable name> will show in front of the file pathname. This field is the [Required] field, for example, *<mypath>/<macro_name>.js.*If you select the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box, the full pathname will not be saved.If you select the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box, your source code will be imported from the file to the model. The location to keep the model is Data::MacroEngine.You can open the **Macro Information** by using Ctrl+Alt+m as a shortcut key.
9. Type the macro description in the **Description** box, see figure above. A macro name must be unique and cannot be duplicated.
10. The function of the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box is to allow you to run the default values automatically. If you select the check box, the system will not open a dialog to prompt you to input the value. If you select the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box, you need to enter the valid default value of each parameter.
11. Click **Add** to specify the arguments of the macro. The arguments specified in the **Arguments** table will be the global variables of a specific macro. A parameter type can be a String, Integer, Long, Double, Date, or ElementPath.If the **Null** check box is selected, you cannot enter the default value of that particular parameter.An empty value in the **Default Value** column does not necessarily mean a null value, for example an empty string value is an empty string.If you input an invalid default value or you do not enter the argument name, the system will display the following error message when you run the script: **The following argument(s) are invalid: <List of the invalid argument>**.
  - Name column contains the name of a parameter
  - Type column contains the type of a parameter
  - Array check box is to identify if an array is the parameter
  - Null check box is to identify if null is the parameter value
  - Default Value column contains an initial value to run the macro
12. Type a keyboard shortcut that will be used to run the macro in the [ATTACHMENT filename='press-new-shortcut-key.png'] box and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The newly assigned keyboard shortcut will appear in the Current keys box.
13. Click **OK** . The **Organize Macros** dialog will open, showing the newly added macro name, description, and keyboard shortcut. Macro names, filenames, and languages are required.Macro description and keyboard shortcuts are optional.If any of the required fields are not entered or duplicate macro name is entered, the following message will open: **The following field(s) are invalid: <List of the problems>**.If the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box is selected and at least one value is empty or not valid, then the following error message will open: **The following value(s) are invalid. <List of the invalid value>**If the****[IMAGE alt='' src='']**** check box is selected, all variables must have valid values or are set to null.
14. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to close the Organize Macros dialog.

##### Editing Macro Information

You can see macro information such as names and descriptions, as well as the macro keyboard shortcuts in the **Organize Macros** dialog.

To edit macro information

1. Click Tools > Macros > Organize Macros . The Organize Macros dialog will open, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

2. Select a macro from the table and either click **Edit** or press **Alt + E**. The **Macro Information** dialog will open.

3. Edit the macro information.

4. Click **OK** to save it. You will see the altered information in the **Organize Macros** dialog.

5. Click **[IMAGE alt='' src='']**.

##### Macro Information Dialog Mnemonic Keys

Macro Engine provides mnemonic keys for you to perform some operations, for example, highlight a textbox and click a specific button in the **Macro Information** dialog. The table below lists the**Macro Information** dialog mnemonic keys and their function. Below is a table listing of Macro Information Dialog Mnemonic Keys.

| Mnemonic keys | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Alt + N | To place the pointer in the box. |
| Alt + M | To highlight the box. |
| Alt + F | To place the pointer in the box. |
| Alt + . | To open the File dialog. |
| Alt + D | To place the pointer in the box. |
| Alt + A | To add an argument in the Arguments box. |
| Alt + l | To delete an argument in the Arguments area. |
| Alt + U | To highlight the Current keys box. |
| Alt + G | To click the button. |
| Alt + R | To click the button. |
| Alt + E | To click the button. |
| Alt + P | To place the pointer in the box. |
| Alt + O | To click the button. |
| Alt + C | To click the button. |
| Alt + H | To click the button. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add-macro.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>Type the macro name in the <strong>Name</strong> box.</li><li>The default macro language you have previously selected (see<strong> </strong> <em> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Selecting a default macro language" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Selecting a Default Macro Language]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </em>) will appear in the <strong>Macro Language</strong> box.</li><li>Click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="button-1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>button, to locate a macro file. The <strong>Open file</strong> dialog will open, see figure below.</li><li>Select the file and its type (there are 5 types of file filter: <em>*.bsh</em>, <em>*.groovy</em>, <em>*.rb</em>, <em>*.js</em>, or <em>*.py</em>), see figure below.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Use Path Variables Checkbox" ac:alt="Use Path Variables Checkbox"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open-dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Open, </strong>see figure above. The selected pathname will appear in the <strong>File</strong> box in the <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog, see figure below.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Types of File Filter" ac:alt="Types of File Filter"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fig 8.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /><span> <br /></span> <br /><ac:image ac:title="Macro Information Dialog" ac:alt="Macro Information Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="macro-information.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p><span>Select either (i) the </span> <strong>Use path variables</strong> <span> check box or (ii) the </span> <strong>Add macro to model</strong> <span> check box.</span> <span> <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5416dd1-742d-4ab3-b82c-babe0c89e2ad"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If you have specified the file or network path in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog by clicking <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong> &gt; <strong>Path</strong> <strong>Variables</strong> and selected the <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="use-path-variables.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong> check box in <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog, the &lt;Path Variable name&gt; will show in front of the file pathname. This field is the [Required] field, for example, <em>&lt;mypath&gt;/&lt;macro_name&gt;.js.</em></li><li>If you select the <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="use-path-variables.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong> check box, the full pathname will not be saved.</li><li>If you select the <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add-macro-to-model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong> check box, your source code will be imported from the file to the model. The location to keep the model is Data::MacroEngine.</li><li>You can open the <strong>Macro Information</strong> by using Ctrl+Alt+m as a shortcut key.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span>Type the macro description in the </span> <strong>Description</strong> <span> box, see figure above.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86396671-f89d-4b01-b34b-49dc07693d2d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A macro name must be unique and cannot be duplicated.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span>The function of the </span> <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatically-run-with-defaults.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong> <span> check box is to allow you to run the default values automatically. If you select the check box, the system will not open a dialog to prompt you to input the value.</span> <br /><span> <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dddf5cb2-6c91-46b2-a606-b7bf9a4c85f4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you select the <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatically-run-with-defaults.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong> check box, you need to enter the valid default value of each parameter.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span>Click </span> <strong>Add</strong> <span> to specify the arguments of the macro. The arguments specified in the </span> <strong>Arguments</strong> <span> table will be the global variables of a specific macro.</span></p><ul><li><strong>Name</strong> column contains the name of a parameter</li><li><strong>Type</strong> column contains the type of a parameter</li><li><strong>Array</strong> check box is to identify if an array is the parameter</li><li><strong>Null</strong> check box is to identify if null is the parameter value</li><li><strong>Default Value</strong> column contains an initial value to run the macro</li></ul><p><span> <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cf0edb8-a556-4ad8-bb80-2c0f70a2dc3a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>A parameter type can be a String, Integer, Long, Double, Date, or ElementPath.</li><li>If the <strong>Null</strong> check box is selected, you cannot enter the default value of that particular parameter.</li><li>An empty value in the <strong>Default Value</strong> column does not necessarily mean a null value, for example an empty string value is an empty string.</li><li>If you input an invalid default value or you do not enter the argument name, the system will display the following error message when you run the script: <strong>The following argument(s) are invalid: &lt;List of the invalid argument&gt;</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>Type a keyboard shortcut that will be used to run the macro in the</span> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="press-new-shortcut-key.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <span> box and click </span> <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assign-btn.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong> <span>. The newly assigned keyboard shortcut will appear in the Current keys box.</span></li><li><p><span>Click </span> <strong>OK</strong> <span>. The </span> <strong>Organize Macros</strong> <span> dialog will open, showing the newly added macro name, description, and keyboard shortcut.</span> <span> <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="38da4285-f9d0-4826-8812-5566b56beb29"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Macro names, filenames, and languages are required.</li><li>Macro description and keyboard shortcuts are optional.</li><li>If any of the required fields are not entered or duplicate macro name is entered, the following message will open: <strong>The following field(s) are invalid: &lt;List of the problems&gt;</strong>.</li><li>If the <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatically-run-with-defaults.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong> check box is selected and at least one value is empty or not valid, then the following error message will open: <strong>The following value(s) are invalid. &lt;List of the invalid value&gt;</strong></li><li>If the<strong> <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatically-run-with-defaults.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong> </strong> check box is selected, all variables must have valid values or are set to null.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span>Click </span> <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="close-btn.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong> <span> to close the </span> <strong>Organize Macros</strong> <span> dialog.</span></li></ol><h3>Editing Macro Information</h3><p>You can see macro information such as names and descriptions, as well as the macro keyboard shortcuts in the <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To edit macro information</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Macros</strong> &gt; <strong>Organize Macros</strong>. The Organize Macros dialog will open, see figure below.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Editing a Macro" ac:alt="Editing a Macro"><ri:attachment ri:filename="organize-macros.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>      2.  Select a macro from the table and either click <strong>Edit</strong> or press <strong>Alt + E</strong>. The <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog will open.</p><p>      3.  Edit the macro information.</p><p>      4.  Click <strong>OK</strong> to save it. You will see the altered information in the <strong>Organize Macros</strong> dialog.</p><p>      5.  Click <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="close-btn.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong>.</p><h3>Macro Information Dialog Mnemonic Keys</h3><p>Macro Engine provides mnemonic keys for you to perform some operations, for example, highlight a textbox and click a specific button in the <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog. The table below lists the<strong> Macro Information</strong> dialog mnemonic keys and their function. Below is a table listing of Macro Information Dialog Mnemonic Keys.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup> <col /> <col /> </colgroup><thead style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th><p>Mnemonic keys</p></th><th><p> Function</p></th></tr></thead><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><strong>Alt + N</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To place the pointer in the <span>box.</span> <br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="name-box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong> </p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><strong>Alt + M</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To highlight the <span>box.</span> <br /> <strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="macro-language-box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong> </p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td><strong>Alt + F</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To place the pointer in the<strong> </strong>box.<br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="file-box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + .</strong></td><td colspan="1">To open the <strong>File</strong> dialog.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + D</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To place the pointer in the <span>box.</span> <br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="descrip-box.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + A</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To add an argument in the <strong>Arguments </strong>box.<strong> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="argument-area.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + l</strong></td><td colspan="1">To delete an argument in the <strong>Arguments</strong> area.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + U</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To highlight the <strong>Current keys </strong>box.<strong> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="current-keys-area.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + G</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To click the <span>button.</span> <br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assign-btn.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + R</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To click the <span>button.</span> <br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove-btn.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + E</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To click the <span>button.</span> <br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove-all-btn.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding a Macro and editing Macro information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1"><strong>Alt + P</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>To place the /></ri:attachment></ac:image> </strong></p></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997379 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00043: (2026x Refresh1) Adding a new Metric Suite

- page_id: `303997379`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997379/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+a+new+Metric+Suite
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:06.498+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Metric Suites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

189812560

189812562

189812561

There are two ways to add a new Metric Suite to your model:

- Create a Metric Suite using the BaseMetricSuite (recommended).
- Create a Metric Suite from scratch.

The *BaseMetricSuite* is a predefined Metric Suite stored in the *DSL Customization* profile of the *UML Standard Profile*.

You are free to choose either way, but we highly recommend the first one. It aids the creation of a new Metric Suite, which inherits the *BaseMetricSuite*attributes that are necessary for calculating metrics (e.g., the context parameter definition and the metric definition for date tracking). This way you don’t have to specify them on your own.

To create a Metric Suite using the *BaseMetricSuite*

1. Make sure that your project uses the UML Standard Profile . If not, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using other projects in a project' space=''] by going to Options > Project Usages .
2. Create a Package to store all the Metric Suites of your model.
3. In the Package, create a Profile Diagram. If you can't find it in the list of available diagrams, enable the Expert mode.
4. Press Ctrl+Alt+F and in the **Quick Find** dialog type “BMS”. Make sure you clear the **Apply Filter** check box.
5. Click the *BaseMetricSuite* in the list of search results. The *BaseMetricSuite* is selected in the Containment tree.
6. Drag the *BaseMetricSuite* from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. Its shape appears on the diagram.
7. In the diagram palette, click **MetricSuite**, and then click a free space on the diagram. A new metric suite is created and its shape appears on the diagram.
8. Create a Generalization relationship from the newly created Metric Suite to the *BaseMetricSuite.*

Now the newly created Metric Suite inherits all the parameter and metric definitions of the *BaseMetricSuite.*

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a Metric Suite from scratch

1. Create a Package to store all the Metric Suites of your model.
2. In the Package, create a Profile Diagram. If you can't find it in the list of available diagrams, enable the Expert mode.
3. On the diagram palette, click MetricSuite , and then click a free space on the diagram.

A new Metric Suite is created and its shape appears on the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To specify the description of a Metric Suite

1. Double-click the shape of a Metric Suite to open its Specification window.
2. Click Documentation/Hyperlinks on the left side of the window.
3. In the text box on the right side of the window, type the description.
4. Click Close .

189812558

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metric Suites' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specify the target for the Metric Suite' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying parameter definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Building metric definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Building validation-based metric definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specific use cases' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993823 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00044: (2026x Refresh1) Adding a scripting language to the modeling tool

- page_id: `303993823`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993823/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+a+scripting+language+to+the+modeling+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:40.216+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Macro engine > (2026x Refresh1) Supported scripting languages
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add a new scripting language that supports JSR-223 to the modeling tool by editing the Macro Engine's *plugin.xml* file.

To add a new scripting language

1. Open the plugin.xml file in <modeling tool name>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton/plugin.xml .
2. Add the following jar file tags to the runtime section and save the file.

```text
"/>]]>
```

The path to the jar file is relative to *<modeling tool name>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton* or the absolute pathname can be used, for example, if you want to add Sleep programming language, add the following tag to the runtime section:

```text
]]>
```

A slash (/) is used as a directory separator for all platforms, including Windows. The new language will be displayed in most of the Macro Engine dialogs, for example, in the Macros section in the **Environment Options** dialog, the**Create Macro** dialog, and in the**Macro Information** dialog. The language name is automatically configured from the information inside the jar file. This version of Macro Engine does not allow you to modify the language name.

##### Script Filename Extension Filter

When you open an **Open** file dialog to browse for a script file in Macro Engine, you can find the filename extension for the script file in the dialog file filter. If you have added a new scripting language to the modeling tool, for example, Sleep programming language, Macro Engine will add the filename extension (**.sl*)” in the**Open** file dialog.

The filename extensions are derived from jar files. There can be more than one filename extension for each engine. However, the current Macro Engine version only shows the first filename extension that is queried from the engine jar file.

You can remove a scripting language from Macro Engine by removing the associated library tag in the *plugin.xml* file. Once the tag has been removed, the language will be removed from all of the dialogs in Macro Engine. However, the script configured to be used with the language will not be removed from the modeling tool. The language of the script will be reset to the default language, which has been previously configured in the modeling tool**Environment Options**dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The new language will be displayed in most of the Macro Engine dialogs, for example,  in the Macros section in the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog, the<strong> Create Macro</strong> dialog, and in the<strong> Macro Information</strong> dialog. The language name is automatically configured from the information inside the jar file. This version of Macro Engine does not allow you to modify the language name.</p><h3>Script Filename Extension Filter</h3><p>When you open an <strong>Open</strong> file dialog to browse for a script file in Macro Engine, you can find the filename extension for the script file in the dialog file filter. If you have added a new scripting language to the modeling tool, for example, Sleep programming language, Macro Engine will add the filename extension (<em>*.sl</em>)” in the<strong> Open</strong> file dialog.</p><p>The filename extensions are derived from jar files. There can be more than one filename extension for each engine. However, the current Macro Engine version only shows the first filename extension that is queried from the engine jar file.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0065b927-4b15-4e6a-85a2-56407b6d0c49"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>You can remove a scripting language from Macro Engine by removing the associated library tag in the <em>plugin.xml</em> file. Once the tag has been removed, the language will be removed from all of the dialogs in Macro Engine. However, the script configured to be used with the language will not be removed from the modeling tool. The language of the script will be reset to the default language, which has been previously configured in the modeling tool </span><strong>Environment Options</strong><span> dialog.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999000 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00045: (2026x Refresh1) Adding Actual Project Milestones in Lr diagram

- page_id: `303999000`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999000/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+Actual+Project+Milestones+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:21.567+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) Lr Lines of Development > (2026x Refresh1) Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart
- labels: ['add-milestone-in-lr', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add an Actual Project Milestone in Lr diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > **Actual Project** Milestone .
  - On the selected project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - In Service
  - Deployed
  - No Longer Used
  - Out Of Service
  - Other
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='']**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add an Actual Project Milestone in Lr diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Actual Project</strong> </span><strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<br /><ul><li>In Service</li><li>Deployed</li><li>No Longer Used</li><li>Out Of Service</li><li>Other</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the<strong> </strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click the <strong>Finish</strong> button when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999006 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00046: (2026x Refresh1) Adding Actual Projects in Lr diagram

- page_id: `303999006`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999006/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+Actual+Projects+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:22.198+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Logical viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) Lr Lines of Development > (2026x Refresh1) Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart
- labels: ['add-project-in-lr', 'add-sub-project-in-lr', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new Project

1. On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.
2. Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.

To add an existing Actual Project

1. On the Lr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the Select Actual Project dialog, select a Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

To add a new Actual Sub Project

1. Select the Actual Project for which you want to create a Actual Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Lr diagra m toolbar, click **Add New** > **Actual Sub Project**.
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Project**,**choose**Add new Actual Sub Project.******
3. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.
4. Specify a time frame for the Actual Sub Project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a new Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click<strong> Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Project</strong>. An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add an existing Actual Project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Lr diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Actual</span> Project</strong> dialog, select a Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the + button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To add a new <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Actual S</span>ub Project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Actual Project for which you want to create a Actual Sub Project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Lr diagra<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">m toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Sub Project</strong>.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">From the shortcut menu of the selected Project<strong>, </strong>choose<strong> Add new Actual Sub Project.</strong><strong><br /></strong></span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">An empty line for the newly created project appears on the diagram pane.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Specify a time frame for the Actual Sub Project.</span></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000528 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00047: (2026x Refresh1) Adding actual projects to NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `304000528`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000528/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+actual+projects+to+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:24.007+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Programme Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add a new or an existing actual project to the NPV-1 diagram.

To add a new actual project

1. On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created actual project appears.
2. Specify a time frame for the actual project.

To add a new actual sub project

1. Select the actual project for which you want to create an actual sub project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Sub Project .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected actual project, click Add new Actual Sub Project .
3. Specify a time frame for the actual sub project.

To add an existing actual project

1. On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select Element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select an Actual project (or several actual projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add a new or an existing actual project to the NPV-1 diagram.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new actual project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Project</strong>. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created actual project appears.</li><li>Specify a time frame for the actual project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add a new actual sub project</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the actual project for which you want to create an actual sub project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Sub Project</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected actual project, click <strong>Add new Actual Sub Project</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Specify a time frame for the actual sub project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing actual project</p><hr /><ol><li>On the NPV-1 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select Element</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, select an Actual project (or several actual projects) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 00048: (2026x Refresh1) Adding and removing rows

- page_id: `303997571`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997571/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+and+removing+rows
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:24.835+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Basic tasks in tables > (2026x Refresh1) Working with rows
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Adding a new row

You can create a new element in the model by adding a new row in the table. Each type of table requires a different procedure to add new row(s).

To add a new row in the Generic and Glossary tables

1. On the table toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new.png'] button.

#### INFO: More information

More information

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Glossary table' space='']]]>

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generic table' space='']]]>

To add a new row in the Instance Table

1. On the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_table.png'] > [ATTACHMENT filename='button_create.png']

#### INFO: More information

More information

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Instance table' space='']]]>

To add a new row in the Metric Table

1. On the table toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='calculate_metrics.png'] > [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_metric_with_different_parameters.png']

#### INFO: More information

More information

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metric table' space='']]]>

To add an existing element as a row in the Generic, Instance, Glossary, Requirement, and Metric tables

1. In the table toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='add_existing.png'] button.
2. Select the element existing in the model and click OK .

##### Removing a row from a table

To remove a row from a table

1. Select the whole row or select one cell of the row you want to remove.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Remove From Table button on the table toolbar.
  - Press Ctrl+Delete on the keyboard.

##### Removing an element from a model

You can delete an element from the model when removing the row from a table.

To remove an element from a table

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting an entire row' space=''] or select one cell of the row you want to remove.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Delete button on the table toolbar.
  - Press Ctrl+D on the keyboard.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000014 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00049: (2026x Refresh1) Adding capabilities to NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `304000014`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000014/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+capabilities+to+NCV-3+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:36.807+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new capability

- On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability . On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.

To add a new capability provision

1. Select the capability for which you want to create a capability provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add new Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] .
4. Click Finish , when you are done.

To add an existing capability

1. On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a new capability</p><hr /><ul><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability</strong>. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To add a new capability provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to create a capability provision.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click <strong>Add new Capability Provision</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing capability</p><hr /><ol><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>. The <strong>Select element</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the<strong> +</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To edit Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Edit Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Edit the dates.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Remove Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ad34cb3-d429-4779-b639-b6814c5255ed"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

- page_id: `304000749`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:38.598+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Managing tables
- labels: ['add-custom-columns-in-tables', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add any desired column to your table by clicking**Show Columns** button.

When generating a report, only columns that are visible in your table will be included in the output.

To add a custom column

1. On the table toolbar, click Show Columns > Select Columns .
2. In the Select Columns dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the table.
3. Click OK , when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This capability is not exhibited by CV-5, StV-5, NCV-5, SV-9, NSV-9, SvcV-9, TV-2, NTV-2, and StdV-2 tables.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add any desired column to your table by clicking<strong> Show Columns</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca5d35fe-1fbe-4d0e-8d88-8ad29bae7b56"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When generating a report, only columns that are visible in your table will be included in the output.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To add a custom column</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, click <strong>Show</strong><strong> Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>Select</strong><strong> Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select</strong><strong> Columns</strong> dialog, choose the columns you want to display in the table.</li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>, when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exchange_kind_and_confirms_to_columns_added.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding custom columns in tables" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc0393f3-60e1-4ab4-a6ef-1695f9a65e27"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This capability is not exhibited by CV-5, StV-5, NCV-5, SV-9, NSV-9, SvcV-9, TV-2, NTV-2, and StdV-2 tables.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995308 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00051: (2026x Refresh1) Adding Existing Resource Exchanges to NSV-6 table

- page_id: `303995308`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995308/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+Existing+Resource+Exchanges+to+NSV-6+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:26.305+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NSV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to add existing Resource Exchanges to fill in an NSV-6 table.

To add existing Resource Exchanges

1. Click the Add Existing and select the Resource Exchange Kind:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
2. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchange identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read-only.
- You may use an NSV-1 product to create or modify a Resource Exchange.
- You can remove the Rows (Resource Exchanges) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You need to add existing Resource Exchanges to fill in an NSV-6 table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add existing Resource Exchanges</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> and select the  Resource Exchange Kind:<ul><li>Command (between Organizational Resources)</li><li>Control (between Physical Resources)</li><li>Resource Exchange</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="018e2a36-4f7f-41d9-bae8-ea1ae22a91b2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Exchange identifier, Resource <span>Exchange </span>Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read-only.</li><li>You may use an NSV-1 product to create or modify a Resource Exchange.</li><li>You can remove the Rows (Resource Exchanges) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

- Resource Exchange identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. Sending Performer, Receiving Performer, and Implements cells are read-only.
- You may use an SV-1 product to create or modify a Resource Exchange.
- You can remove the Rows (Resource Exchanges) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991587 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00053: (2026x Refresh1) Adding extension points

- page_id: `303991587`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991587/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+extension+points
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:52.607+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Functional analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Use Cases
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

19692200

19692202

19692201

INLINE

An extension point in the Use Case represents the location at which the extension can be inserted. In the Use Case diagram, the extension point is used together with the extend relationship.

To add an extension point to the Use Case

1. Draw an extend relationship from the extending Use Case to the extended Use Case.
2. Subsequent actions depend on whether the extended Use Case has at least one extension point already:
  - If there are no extension points, a dialog appears asking for confirmation to add a new extension point. Click Yes and type the name of the extension point.
  - If there is at least one extension point, the Extension Points dialog opens, displaying the list of extension points of that Use Case. Click Create , type the name of the new extension point, and click Close .

You can also add extension points via the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] of the Use Case. To do this, click **Extension Point**> [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and then type the name of the new extension point.

19692199

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MED']
