Click <strong>Yes</strong> and type the name of the extension point.</li><li>If there is at least one extension point, the <strong>Extension Points</strong> dialog opens, displaying the list of extension points of that Use Case. Click <strong>Create</strong>, type the name of the new extension point, and click <strong>Close</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ol><p>You can also add extension points via the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /></ac:link> of the Use Case. ## PAGE 00054: (2026x Refresh1) Adding JRuby and installing Gems

- page_id: `303993812`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993812/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+JRuby+and+installing+Gems
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:40.059+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Macro engine > (2026x Refresh1) Supported scripting languages
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

147042186

147042197

147042185

**On this page:**

147042196

By default, JRuby is not included in the modeling tool, however, you can add it manually. In addition, you can also install Gems for JRuby.

##### Adding JRuby to the modeling tool

To add JRuby to the modeling tool

1. Copy the JRuby engine jar file (e.g. jruby-complete-9.2.14.0.jar ) to <*tool_installation_directory*>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib.
2. Add the entry below to the file *<tool_installation_directory>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\enginedescriptor.xml.* rb
 
 JRuby
]]>
3. Append a library entry in file *<*tool_installation_directory*>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\plugin.xml.* ]]>

##### Installing Gems for JRuby

To install a gem for Ruby engine in the modeling tool

1. Open the command line.
2. Go to the folder <*tool_installation_directory*>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib.
3. Enter the following command to install a gem.

```text
.jar -S gem install [--user-install]   ...]]>
```

- <version> is the JRuby version, e.g. 9.2.14.0.
- <gem name> is the name of a gem.
- The three dots (...) mean that you can type a list of gem names there.

Once the gem has been installed, you can use it in the Macro Engine. The example below shows how to create a macro that can generate a unique ID by using a gem.

```text

```

To install a gem for the existing Ruby environment on your machine and use it in the Macro Engine

1. Add the following properties to specify the home and library of JRuby in <**tool_installation_directory**>/bin/mduml.properties under JAVA_ARGS section (assuming you have JRuby installed in C:/jruby-9.2.14.0 on your machine).

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

2. Change the JRuby library path to navigate to your *jruby.jar* in *<***tool_installation_directory***>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton/plugin.xml*.

```text
]]>
```

1. The command in this section should run in <****tool_installation_directory****>/plugins/com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton/engine .
2. If you have a whitespace in the property file path, you need to type a double quote to wrap both the property and its value, for each property, for example: “ -Djruby.home=C:/my white space/jruby-*9.2.14.0* " “ -Djruby.lib=C:/my white space/jruby-*9.2.14.0*/lib ”

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7952164a-d50b-40e1-b7e8-780e1ddcae2e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147042186</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5510481d-508a-4e41-8e15-8c1e88940b0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147042197</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60dbe2fc-87e5-41e8-8e2f-b941f68ccbfa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147042185</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page:</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="acfb9d0a-77f5-4fbb-ac3e-6150c37672dc" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1957e301-6d12-45bf-ab73-a0ef2d6693a6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">147042196</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, JRuby is not included in the modeling tool, however, you can add it manually. 			<object class="java.util.ArrayList"/>
		</void>
		<void property="name">
			<string>JRuby</string>
		</void>
	</object>
</void>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Append a library entry in file <em>&lt;<em>tool_installation_directory</em>&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\plugin.xml.</em></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a16e952e-af3e-4799-9626-f0d532a95518"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<library name="lib/jruby-complete-9.2.14.0.jar"/>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Installing Gems for JRuby</h3><p>To install a gem for Ruby engine in the modeling tool</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the command line.</li><li>Go to the folder <em>&lt;<em>tool_installation_directory</em>&gt;\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.automaton\lib.</em></li><li>Enter the following command to install a gem.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e484a77-ecae-4168-a38b-26100dd55d04"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[java -jar jruby-complete-<version>.jar -S gem install [--user-install] <gem name1> <gem name2> ...]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9533940-40a5-4b95-aebc-1f9b5eae7999"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>&lt;version&gt; is the <span>JRuby </span>version, e.g. 9.2.14.0.</li><li>&lt;gem name&gt; is the name of a gem.</li><li>The three dots (...) mean that you can type a list of gem names there.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Once the gem has been installed, you can use it in the Macro Engine. The example below shows how to create a macro that can generate a unique ID by using a gem.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77be0e79-ded9-490d-9b06-d32a81c58189"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[require 'java'

uuid = UUID.new Application.getInstance().getGUILog().log(uuid.generate);]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />To install a gem for the existing Ruby environment on your machine and <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000593 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00055: (2026x Refresh1) Adding Milestones in NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `304000593`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000593/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+Milestones+in+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:27.138+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Programme Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a milestone

1. Select an actual project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Milestone .
  - On the selected actual project shortcut menu, click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - Out Of Service Milestone
  - Increment Milestone
  - Deployed Milestone
  - No Longer Used Milestone
  - Actual Project Milestone
4. The Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard opens.
5. Specify milestone settings in the following wizard steps and click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a milestone</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Milestone</strong>.</li><li>On the selected actual project shortcut menu, click <strong>Add new Actual Project Milestone</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Select one of the available milestone kinds:<ul><li>Out Of Service Milestone</li><li>Increment Milestone</li><li>Deployed Milestone</li><li>No Longer Used Milestone</li><li>Actual Project Milestone</li></ul></li><li>The<strong> Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard</strong> opens.</li><li>Specify milestone settings in the following wizard steps and click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999199 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00056: (2026x Refresh1) Adding more report data directories

- page_id: `303999199`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999199/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+more+report+data+directories
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:40.456+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Configuring Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

267209495

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

267209497

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

267209496

The default report data directories for operating systems are as follows:

- For Windows: text\AppData\Local\.magicdraw\\data\reports]]>
- For Linux: text/./< modeling tool version number>]]>

Report Wizard provides a configuration to add more report data directories by creating a **report.properties** file in the Report Wizard plugin directory, *<modeling tool name>\plugins\com.nomagic.magicdraw.reportwizard*.

You can declare the **template_loc** parameter in the **report.properties** file to specify another report data directory as follows:

```text

```

You can also specify the **template_loc** parameter for multiple report data directories by using a semicolon (;) to separate each of the directories.

```text

```

All of templates in the default report data directory and other locations specified in the **report.properties** file are available for generating in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard dialog' space='']**Report Wizard** dialog and can be called through the **-template** parameter in command line report generation.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The template names defined in the **template.xml** file in each report data directory must be unique.

267209494

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Configuring Report Wizard' space=''] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Configuring Report Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard Environment options' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995294 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00057: (2026x Refresh1) Adding New Resource Exchanges to NSV-6 table

- page_id: `303995294`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995294/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+New+Resource+Exchanges+to+NSV-6+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:25.655+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NSV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add new Resource Exchanges

1. Click Add New . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created resource interactions are added to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add new Resource Exchanges</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Add New</strong>. <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done. The rows with created resource interactions are added to the table.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994866 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00058: (2026x Refresh1) Adding or removing element types

- page_id: `303994866`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994866/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+or+removing+element+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:50.740+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Generic table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add or remove element types

1. In the Criteria area, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] button next to the Element Type box. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting element types' space='']**Select Element Type** dialog with the element and stereotype list opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='criteria_element_type.png']
2. Do one of the following:

- To add the element types, select the appropriate check boxes in the list. Properties corresponding to the selected types will be added to the list of available columns.
- To remove the element types, click to clear the appropriate check boxes in the list. Properties corresponding to the deselected types will be removed from the list of available columns. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Select the Include subtypes check box to display the subtypes of the selected element types.

- Select the Include Custom Types check box to display the custom types extending the selected element types.

To add an element type, you can also drag a selected element to the **Element Type** box directly from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model Browser' space=''].

- To select adjacent elements, select a single element and hold down the Shift key while you click other elements.
- To select nonadjacent elements, select a single element and hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements.

The new value of the **Element Type** box replaces the old value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Selecting element types" /><ac:link-body><strong>Select Element Type</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> dialog with the element and stereotype one of the following:</li></ol><ul><li><p>To add the element types, select the appropriate check boxes in the list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000020 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00059: (2026x Refresh1) Adding sub capabilities to NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `304000020`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000020/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+sub+capabilities+to+NCV-3+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:38.011+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new sub capability

1. Select the capability for which you want to create a sub capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add new Sub Capability .
3. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.

To add an existing sub capability

1. Select the capability for which you want to add a sub capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click A dd existing Sub Capability .
3. In the opened Select Capability dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a new sub capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to create a sub capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click <strong>Add new Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing sub capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability for which you want to add a sub capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>On the NCV-3 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click A<strong>dd existing Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the opened <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da049ff8-52ce-4793-997c-e889f70f4ec3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994877 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00060: (2026x Refresh1) Adding terms in the Glossary Table

- page_id: `303994877`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994877/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+terms+in+the+Glossary+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:51.044+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables > (2026x Refresh1) Glossary table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add terms in the Glossary Table in the following ways: 
43

##### Directly from the diagram pane

To add a word or phrase to a glossary directly from the diagram pane

1. Select a word or phrase you want to be a term. Even if you mark only a part of a word, the entire word will be added to the glossary as a term.
2. Right-click it and select Add To <Glossary Table name>. The word or phrase is converted to a term and added to the Glossary Table. [ATTACHMENT filename='glossary_add.png']

##### Using the Generic Table toolbar

To add a term in the Glossary Table using a toolbar

- Click the Add New button to create a new row for a term in the table.
- Click the Add Existing button and from the Select Element dialog. Choose the element you wish to be a term.

##### By dragging any element

To drag an element to the Glossary Table

1. In the Containment tree, select an element you wish to be a term.
2. Drag it to the Glossary Table.

##### By pasting from another resource

To create terms in the table by pasting data from another resource

1. Copy a table from another resource. The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the Glossary Table.The data types of columns in the Glossary table must be compatible with copied information.
2. Open the Glossary Table where you want to paste the copied data. The cells must be editable in the Glossary Table.
3. Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). 
Each row displays one term with its description. If the term already exists in the model, the information updates.If terms have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted terms in the Glossary Table. Manage ID prefixes in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Element Numbering dialog' space='']**Element Numbering** dialog. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Adding synonyms of the terms

You can add one or more synonyms for every term. After that, the synonyms are available in the suggestion list. When you use a synonym, the original term is displayed in the description.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To add a synonym

- In the term's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space='']**Specification** window , Synonym property specification cell, type the synonyms for the term. [ATTACHMENT filename='synonyms.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add terms in the Glossary Table in the following ways:<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6684ea6d-ca59-4c0f-815c-f50bde5487b0"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Directly from the diagram pane</h3><p>To add a word or phrase to a glossary directly from the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select a word or phrase you want to be a term.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcebc2ff-42e8-463b-b08f-c5d19c16e556"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Even if you mark only a part of a word, the entire word will be added to the glossary as a term.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Right-click it and select<strong> Add To &lt;Glossary Table name&gt;.</strong> <br />The word or phrase is converted to a term and added to the Glossary Table.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="glossary_add.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding terms in the Glossary Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Using the Generic Table toolbar</h3><p>To add a term in the Glossary Table using a toolbar</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button to create a new row for a term in the table.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and from the<strong> Select Element</strong> dialog. Choose the element you wish to be a term.</li></ul><h3>By dragging any element</h3><p>To drag an element to the Glossary Table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, select an element you wish to be a term.</li><li>Drag it to the Glossary Table.</li></ol><h3>By pasting from another resource</h3><p>To create terms in the table by pasting data from another resource</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Copy a table from another resource.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5799968b-3b43-4cd1-8c33-87d572954aec"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The number of columns in that table should be the same as the number of columns in the Glossary Table.</li><li>The data types of columns in the Glossary table must be compatible with copied information.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Open the Glossary Table where you want to paste the copied data.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e77cc25f-6962-4a18-bd50-6ae2f1e0c262"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The cells must be editable in the Glossary Table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X).<br />Each row displays one term with its description.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6aa3ecfe-f5e2-4189-8710-ee308ed4e62d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the term already exists in the model, the information updates.</li><li>If terms have ID prefixes in another resource, they are automatically set after the copy-and-paste command only for pasted terms in the Glossary Table. Manage ID prefixes in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Element Numbering dialog" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Element Numbering</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="paste_table_in_glossary_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding terms in the Glossary Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><h3>Adding synonyms of the terms</h3><p>You can add one or more synonyms for every term. After that, the synonyms are available in the suggestion list. When you use a synonym, the original term is displayed in the description.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="synonym_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding terms in the Glossary Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To add a synonym</p><hr /><ul><li>In the term's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specification window" /><ac:link-body><strong>Specification</strong> window</ac:link-body></ac:link>, Synonym property specification cell, type the synonyms for the term.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="synonyms.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding terms in the Glossary Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998408 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00061: (2026x Refresh1) Adding variables into an output report filename

- page_id: `303998408`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998408/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+variables+into+an+output+report+filename
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:39.323+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the following variables and include them in an output report filename.

- Date variable
- Template variable
- Random number
- Project attribute

#### Date variable

A date variable is a variable for the current date and time. (See section **$date** in the previous chapter). The date variable consists of:

- A date variable with the default format.
- A date variable with a customized date format.

##### Date variables with default format

Using a date variable without an attribute causes it to format the current date and time into a default format.

The syntax: **${date}**

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_2011-12-21 11.55.44.html*”.

##### Date variable with custom format

Using a date variable with an attribute causes it to format the current date and time into a specific format.

The syntax: **${date.get('format')}** or **${date.get("format")}**

For example:

```text

```

The output filename would be “*index_20111221-115544.html*”.

#### Template variable

A template variable is a variable for a template that is created in Report Wizard. It allows you to add all template variables into an output report filename (see **).

##### Including template variables in an output report filename

When you include template variables created in an output report filename, the output filename which consists of the variables will be converted into value. This section uses the following template variables as examples, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Adding a value of a top-level variable

You can retrieve a top-level variable in an output report filename by using the following syntax.

The syntax: **${Parent}**

For example:

```text

```

The report output filename would be “*index_parent value.html*”.

##### Adding a value of a child variable

You can retrieve any of the following child variable values in an output report filename.

1. Getting a child variable at an index position: The syntax : ${Parent.get(0)}
2. Getting a child variable by child names: The syntax : ${Parent.get('Child')} or ${Parent.get("Child")}
3. Getting a child value by referencing its name (in this case “Child”): The syntax : ${Parent.Child}

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_child value.html*”.

#### Random number

Report Wizard supports only pseudorandom numbers in an output report filename. The random number consists of:

- A random number with the default distributed int value.
- A random number with a distributed int value between 0 (inclusive) and specified value (exclusive).

##### Random number with the default distributed integer value

You can use Report Wizard to generate pseudorandom numbers with the default distributed integer value.

**The syntax: ${random}**

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_21547.html*”.

##### Random number with a distributed integer value between 0 (inclusive) and specified value (exclusive)

You can use Report Wizard to generate pseudorandom numbers with the default distributed integer value between 0 and a specific value.

**The syntax: ${random.nextInt(n)}**

The Parameters:

“n” - the bound on the random number to be returned. The “n” value must be a positive integer.

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_457.html*”.

#### Project attribute

You can add a project attribute or information about a project into an output report filename.

The project attribute consists of:

- Project name
- Project version

##### Project name

You can retrieve the name of a project by using the following syntax.

**The syntax: ${project.name}**

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_myProject.html*”.

##### Project version

You can retrieve a project version by using the following syntax.

**The syntax: ${project.version}**

For example:

```text

```

The output report filename would be “*index_1234.html*”.

#### Exception flow

You may accidentally specify an invalid output report filename such as:

- An invalid syntax variable.
- A variable that does not exist in a template variable.
- An invalid parameter.

If this happens, Report Wizard will use this variable as a filename and a warning message will open in a log file (*\.modeling tool\version\md.log*).

For example:

```text

```

A single quote after “yyyyMMdd-hhmmss” was missing. Therefore, the output filename would be “index_${date.get('yyyyMMdd-hhmmss)}.html” and a warning message would open, see figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A Warning Message of an Invalid Variable Syntax.

For example:

```text

```

“${Parent}” was not in the report template variable. Therefore, the output filename would be "index_${Parent}.html" and a warning message would open, see figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A Warning Message of a Variable that Does not Exist in a Template Variable.

For example:

```text

```

In this example, ‘yyyy-MM-dd qq’ is an invalid parameter. Therefore, output filename would be “index_${date.get('yyyy-MM-dd qq').html” and a warning message would open, see figure below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
A Warning Message of an Invalid Parameter.

- All variables should be able to be combined together. For example: C:\OutputReport\index_${project.name}_${date}.html The output filename would be “ index_myProject_2011-12-21 11.55.44.html ”.
- The template variable is the highest priority of all other variables. For example: C:\OutputReport\index_${random}.html

If you specify a template variable whose name is at random in the Report Data, the variable value will be used as the filename.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The output filename would be “*index_myRandomNumber.html*”.

If you do not specify a template variable whose name is at random in the Report Data, the random number with the default distributed integer value will be used as the filename.

The output filename would be “*index_12345.html*”.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can use the following variables and include them in an output report filename.</p><ul><li>Date variable</li><li>Template variable</li><li>Random number</li><li>Project attribute</li></ul><h2>Date variable</h2><p>A date variable is a variable for the current date and time. (See section <strong>$date</strong> in the previous chapter). The date variable consists of:</p><ul><li>A date variable with the default format.</li><li>A date variable with a customized date format.</li></ul><h3>Date variables with default format</h3><p>Using a date variable without an attribute causes it to format the current date and time into a default format.</p><p>The syntax: <strong>${date}</strong></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4218d084-8f1e-410b-9f13-b52437369ab8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${date}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output report filename would be “<em>index_2011-12-21 11.55.44.html</em>”.</p><h3>Date variable with custom format</h3><p>Using a date variable with an attribute causes it to format the current date and time into a specific format.</p><p>The syntax: <strong>${date.get('format')}</strong> or <strong>${date.get(&quot;format&quot;)}</strong></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aecf2d93-fb72-44e3-8f95-783788c2373d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[C:\OutputReport\index_${date.get('yyyyMMdd-hhmmss')}.html]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output filename would be “<em>index_20111221-115544.html</em>”.</p><h2>Template variable</h2><p>A template variable is a variable for a template that is created in Report Wizard. It allows you to add all template variables into an output report filename (see <em><ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Including Variables in a Template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>).</p><h3>Including template variables in an output report filename</h3><p>When you include template variables created in an output report filename, the output filename which consists of the variables will be converted into value. This section uses the following template variables as examples, see figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Sample of Report Variables" ac:alt="Sample of Report /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h3>Adding a value of a top-level variable</h3><p>You can retrieve a top-level variable in an output report filename by using the following syntax.</p><p>The syntax: ## PAGE 00062: (2026x Refresh1) Adding version of configurations to NSV-8 chart

- page_id: `303995312`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995312/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+version+of+configurations+to+NSV-8+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:26.539+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NSV-8 System Evolution Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a version of configuration

1. On the NSV-8 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Version of Configuration . On the diagram pane, an empty line appears.
2. Name the created Version of Configuration.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To add a version of configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>On the NSV-8 diagram toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Version of Configuration</strong>. On the diagram pane, an empty line appears.</li><li>Name the created Version of Configuration.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997323 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00063: (2026x Refresh1) Adding, removing, and changing order of favorites

- page_id: `303997323`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997323/2026x+Refresh1+Adding+removing+and+changing+order+of+favorites
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:21.281+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Favorites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1539458257

1539458269

1539458259

There are a few ways to add an element to favorites:

- Using the Add Selected to Favorites command from the Favorites menu. This way is especially handy for quick additions to favorites.
- Using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Manage favorites' space=''] dialog. Choose this way when you need to add and then perform other manipulations with favorites.

To quickly add an element to favorites

1. Select the element in the Model Browser and click [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites.png'] on the toolbar of the open tab. [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites_quick_add.png']
2. Click Add Selected to Favorites on the menu.

To add an element to favorites in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Manage favorites' space='']** dialog

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Manage favorites' space=''] dialog.
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Manage favorites' space=''] dialog, select the element and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='add.png'] button, which is between two lists of the dialog. The element appears in the list of favorites.
3. Click OK to confirm the addition and close the dialog.

Once the element is added to favorites, the following changes occur in your project:

- The element becomes marked as a favorite in all trees and lists of the model.
- You can easily navigate to the element in the Model Browser. The element name is added to the Go To Favorites command group on the Favorites menu that opens in the Model Browser. [ATTACHMENT filename='favorites_goto_favorites.png']

To remove an element from favorites

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Manage favorites' space=''] dialog.
2. In the list of favorites (on the right side of the dialog), click the element you need to remove and then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='remove.png'] button.
3. Click OK to confirm the removal and close the dialog.

Once the element is removed from favorites, it is no longer marked. Commands for navigation to the element and for searching in it are no longer available on the Favorites menu.

The following procedure describes how to change the order of favorite elements on the Favorites menu. To change the order of favorites on the Favorites menu, you need to make appropriate order changes in the list of favorites on the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Manage favorites' space='']** dialog.

To change the order of favorites

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Manage favorites' space='']**Manage Favorites** dialog.
2. In the list of favorites (on the right side of the dialog), click an element whose order on the Favorites menu you need to change and then do either of the following:
  - If you need to move the element up, click the Up button. Click again if you need to move the element up by one more position and so on.
  - If you need to move the element down, click the Down button. Click again if you need to move the element down by one more position and so on.
3. Click OK to confirm the reorder and close the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Moving the element up in the Favorites menu.

1539458256

**Related pages**

- Favorites

To change the order of favorites on the Favorites menu, you need to make appropriate order changes in the list of favorites on the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Manage favorites" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Manage Favorites]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the order of favorites</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Manage favorites" /><ac:link-body><strong> Manage Favorites</strong> </ac:link-body></ac:link>dialog.</li><li>In the list of favorites (on the right side of the dialog), click an element whose order on the Favorites menu you need to change and then do either of the following:<ul><li>If you need to move the element up, click the <strong>Up</strong> button. Click again if you need to move the element up by one more position and so on.</li><li>If you need to move the element down, click the <strong>Down</strong> button. Click again if you need to move the element down by one more position and so on.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> to confirm the reorder and close the dialog.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="changing_favorites_order.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Adding, removing, and changing order of favorites" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Moving the element up in the Favorites menu. </h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc16ea9c-7887-48dd-8eeb-70b574a427d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1539458256</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Favorites">Favorites</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997703 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00064: (2026x Refresh1) Address tab

- page_id: `303997703`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997703/2026x+Refresh1+Address+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:43:24.167+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Manage Navigation > (2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks > (2026x Refresh1) Edit Hyperlink dialog
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Address** tab is dedicated to adding, editing, or deleteing hyperlinks to webpages and other external resources.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example of the Address tab in the Edit Hyperlink dialog. The text "units" links to the unit description in the NoMagic documentation.

All boxes and buttons of the **Address** tab are described in the table below.

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Text to display box | Shows the text that will be displayed as a hyperlink. If you select the text before opening this dialog, you will see that text in this box. If you change the displayed text in this box, it will be changed in the HTML text field too. If this field is empty, you can type a text and it will be added in the HTML text field.It is available only when adding a hyperlink on text. |
| Type the address box | Allows you to type or paste the hyperlink address. |
| Select from list box | Displays all previously used hyperlinks. You can select them to fill in the Type the address box. |
|  | Opens the hyperlink in the web browser specified in the Text to display box. |
| The Clear button | Deletes all previously used hyperlinks from the Select from list box. |
| The Active check box | Activates the hyperlink if selected. It is available only when adding a hyperlink on element or entire diagram.Active check box ruleOnly one hyperlink can be active. By default the last added hyperlink is active. |

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Defining hyperlinks' space='']

- Managing hyperlinks
- Linking model elements to OSLC resources

## PAGE 00065: (2026x Refresh1) AdHoc SubProcess

- page_id: `303999133`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999133/2026x+Refresh1+AdHoc+SubProcess
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:34.384+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Activities > (2026x Refresh1) SubProcesses
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1487248268

1487248269

1487248269

**Description**

An AdHoc SubProcess is a specialized type of SubProcess, which is a group of activities that have no required sequence relationships. A set of activities can be defined for the process, but the sequence and number of performances for the activities are determined by the performers of the activities.

**Notation**

- Expanded [ATTACHMENT filename='expanded_adhoc.png']
- Collapsed [ATTACHMENT filename='suppressed_adhoc.png']

1487248267

**Related elements**

- Activities
- SubProcess
- Event SubProcess
- Transaction

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using SubProcesses

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997341 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00066: (2026x Refresh1) Advanced HTML Editor dialog

- page_id: `303997341`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997341/2026x+Refresh1+Advanced+HTML+Editor+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:03.997+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) HTML editor
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Advanced HTML Editor allows you to change text style, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Inserting images into HTML text' space=''], insert symbols and tables, and perform other formatting actions.

To open the **Advanced HTML Editor** dialog

- Click the Advanced HTML Editor [ATTACHMENT filename='advanced_html_editor_button.png'] button on the HTML editor toolbar. [ATTACHMENT filename='html_editor_toolbar.png']

For more information, please see how to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) HTML editor' space=''] in a dialog.

Click the**HTML source** tab to view the HTML source. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Advanced HTML Editor dialog

To insert an image into HTML text

1. Click the **Insert image** button, as shown in the following picture. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. In the Picture Properties dialog, specify the image location. Do one of the following:
  - Type the path to the image location.
  - Click **Browse** and select the image you want to insert. If you are working on a server project, make sure that the path to the image location is accessible from any computer that has an Internet connection. Otherwise, the image will not display when the project opens on another computer. To insert an image as an attached file, simply paste its URL to the **Picture Source** box by pressing Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on OS X). For more information, refer to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Inserting images into HTML text' space=''].
3. Click OK when you are done.

The image is inserted into HTML text.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Image in HTML text.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000143 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00068: (2026x Refresh1) Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors

- page_id: `304000143`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000143/2026x+Refresh1+Allocate+Activities+to+State+Internal+Behaviors
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:52.019+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['activities-allocation', 'states', 'internal-behavior', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

890462394

890462398

890462397

You can allocate Activities to States in all state diagrams. Activities allocation in the UAF profile works in a similar way as in SysML v1.

To allocate an Activity to a State

1. Select an Activity, drag and hold it over the State shape in the diagram.
2. When the tool-tip appears, release the mouse button. The context menu appears.
3. Select one of the following:
  - Entry
  - Do Activity
  - Exit

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Activities allocation in the UAF profile works in a similar way as in SysML v1.</p><p><br /></p><p>To allocate an Activity to a State</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an Activity, drag and hold it over the State shape in the diagram.</li><li>When the tool-tip appears, release the mouse button. The context menu appears.</li><li>Select one of the following:<ul><li>Entry</li><li>Do Activity</li><li>Exit</li></ul></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990236 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00069: (2026x Refresh1) Allocation Mode

- page_id: `303990236`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990236/2026x+Refresh1+Allocation+Mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:18.793+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Functional analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Functional Allocations
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Allocation Matrix' space=''] or new swimlane in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Activity Diagram' space=''], you can select the kind **Definition** or **Usage** allocation mode. The definition elements are Activity and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED']; usage elements are Action and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'].

When we talk about the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Allocation Matrix' space=''], the rows in the matrix represent either the Activity or Action that are the clients of the Allocate relationship. Columnsin the matrix represent either the Block or Part Property that are the suppliers of the Allocate relationship.

When creating a SysML Allocation Matrix in a SysML project for the first time, the**Create SysML Allocation Matrix** dialog appears, shown in the figure below. The usage allocation matrix is created by default when creating a new SysML Allocation Matrix in a MagicGrid project.You can change that by specifying the **Allocation Mode** option value in the **Project Options**dialog.>]]>

The **Allocation Mode** project option is global and will be applied for the SysML Allocation matrix and SysML Activity diagram (swimlanes).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The****Create SysML Allocation Matrix dialog where you can select the Definition or Usage mode.

The choice you made will be remembered and applied for the following matrices you will create in the project. The same applies to the swimlanes.

When you create a swimlane for the first time, the **Create Swimlane** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose one of the allocation modes and the choice will be remembered. The dialog will not be displayed if the Allocation Mode is already chosen when creating a SysML Allocation matrix.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Create Swimlane dialog where you can select the Definition or Usage mode.

The examples of Usage and Definition modes are illustrated below. Figures are created by using the [distiller model.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9919520/distiller%20model.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1505483436734&api=v2) sample model that comes only with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The definition and usage modes in the SysML Allocation matrices.

##### Changing the Allocation Mode

Changing the Allocation Mode in the Project Options dialog applies the change for the SysML Allocation matrix and swimlanes.

change_modeTo change the default Allocation Mode

1. From the main menu, select **Options** > **Project**.
2. In the **Project Options**dialog, expand the **General** node and select **SysML**.
3. Select the **Allocation Mode**option value: 
 - Definition (Activity allocated to Block) 
 - Usage (Action allocated to Part) 
 - Ask and Remember
4. Click **OK**. The default Allocation Mode is selected and will be applied each time you create a new SysML Allocation matrix or new swimlanes in the SysML Activity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Allocation Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Activity Diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When creating a new <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Allocation Matrix" /></ac:link> or new swimlane in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Activity Diagram" /></ac:link></span>, you can select the kind <strong>Definition</strong> or <strong>Usage</strong> allocation mode. The definition elements are <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> Activity and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link>; usage elements are Action and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link>. </span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When we talk about the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Allocation Matrix" /></ac:link>, the rows in the matrix represent either the Activity or Action that are the clients of the Allocate relationship. Columns </span>in the matrix <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">represent either the Block or Part Property that are the suppliers of the Allocate relationship. <br /></span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When creating a SysML Allocation Matrix in a SysML project for the first time, the</span><strong> Create SysML Allocation Matrix</strong> dialog <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">appears, shown in the figure below. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The usage allocation matrix is created by default when creating a new SysML Allocation Matrix in a MagicGrid project. </span>You can change that by specifying the <strong>Allocation Mode</strong> option value in the <strong>Project Options </strong>dialog. </span></span><ac:link ac:anchor="change_mode"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to change the Allocation Mode >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9455691-acab-43c9-b2ac-c3ff48a0ae17"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Allocation Mode</strong> project option is global and will be applied for the SysML Allocation matrix and SysML Activity diagram (swimlanes).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="definition_usage_mode.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Allocation Mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The<strong> </strong>Create SysML Allocation Matrix dialog where you can select the Definition or Usage mode.</span></h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The choice you made will be remembered and applied for the following matrices you will create in the project. The same applies to the swimlanes. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When you create a swimlane for the first time, the <strong>Create Swimlane</strong> dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose one of the allocation modes and the choice will be remembered. The dialog will not be displayed if the Allocation Mode is already chosen when creating a SysML Allocation matrix. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_swimlane.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Allocation Mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Create Swimlane dialog where you can select the Definition or Usage mode.</span></h6><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The examples of Usage and Definition modes are illustrated below. Figures are created by using the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9919520/distiller%20model.mdzip?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1505483436734&amp;api=v2">distiller model.mdzip</a> sample model that comes only with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="allocation_matrix_definition_and_usage.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Allocation Mode" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The definition and usage modes in the SysML Allocation matrices.</span></h6><h3><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Changing the Allocation Mode</span></h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Changing the Allocation Mode in the Project Options dialog applies the change for the SysML Allocation matrix and swimlanes.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea5d9072-a75d-4aa4-834e-62ad2a7d24c9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">change_mode</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To change the default Allocation Mode</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="0b4ca894-5b25-4abe-9877-173d5f9cf532"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</span></li><li data-uuid="d36a59b4-fbbe-405b-b5e2-f14d69dfcf74"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In the <strong>Project Options </strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">dialog, expand the <strong>General</strong> node and select <strong>SysML</strong>.</span><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="63605a35-b521-475b-b243-96d8e64d0a34"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the <strong>Allocation Mode</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> option value:<br /> - Definition (Activity allocated to Block)<br /> - Usage (Action allocated to Part)<br /> - Ask and Remember</span></span></li><li data-uuid="bb35ad43-f333-40c2-a972-b69644591006"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The default Allocation Mode is selected and will be applied each time you create a new SysML Allocation matrix or new swimlanes in the SysML Activity diagram.</span></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="b0911824-a9a5-469d-ad8a-88cb0daf6c19"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Allocation Matrix" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="d807f9e2-4bc8-41e6-b487-e62ec80ab53a"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Activity Diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Merge results are displayed in the **Merge** window. Read about analyzing and managing model merge results in

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Getting merge summary information and changes legend' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding content of the Merged Result panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Inspecting element property changes in the Specification panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Inspecting changes in the Change details panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Displaying and navigating through changes' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Quickly navigating through conflicting changes' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Accepting and rejecting changes using shortcut menu' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Viewing and analyzing changes of modified diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generating a differences report' space='']

If you do not need to perform the analysis and management of merge results, proceed to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Finishing and canceling merge' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the Merge window.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997892 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00073: (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing dependencies among elements

- page_id: `303997892`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997892/2026x+Refresh1+Analyzing+dependencies+among+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:42.016+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Identifying Package Dependencies
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before exporting or sharing a package as an independent used project, you need to identify and solve package dependencies first. More specifically, you have to make sure that the packagedoes not depend on any external elements (except other used projects). Furthermore, cyclic dependencies between several used projects are not allowed.

##### relationshipPackage dependencies by relationship

The used project depends on the external elements which have relationships with the model elements from the used project packages.

external

###### The used project depends on external elements

If a used project's element has a relationship with an external element and that relationship is contained in the used project package, an error message appears when exporting the used package.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the used project dependency on an external element

Such dependencies on external elements are displayed in the Model Browser:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Package depends on an external element

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Error in the Package Dependencies panel

In this situation, a modeling tool can suggest moving the relationship into the parent package of this external element. For example, the package *Items* is a parent of a class *Book*, so the relationship can be moved from the *Items* into the package *Library*:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Resolved package dependency on an external element

Some movements can be achieved by clicking **Solve** in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package Dependencies panel' space='']**Package Dependencies** panel.

###### The used project depends on an external element, but can be exported (with warning)

Even though the used project element has a relationship with an external element, that relationship is contained in an external package:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a “legal” used project dependency on an external element

In this case, the dependency on an external element is displayed in the Model Browser:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### “Legal” used project dependency on an external element in the Model Browser

The package can be exported as the used project because the relationship is contained in an external package.

###### The used project does not depend on an external element

If the used project element has a relationship with an external element it is irrelevant in the context of UML (for instance, the external model uses the used project, but not vice versa) and that relationship is contained in an external model, the package can be exported into an independent used project:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a relationship when the used project does not depend on an external element

##### referenceDependencies by reference

The used project depends on the external elements which have references to the model elements from the used project packages.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Example of a dependency by reference

In this case, the package *Items* cannot be exported to an independent project because this package has reference to the *Library* package.

##### dependenciesDiagram dependencies

The diagram depends on all model elements displayed within it.

If the diagram is contained in the package *Items* and depends on external elements, this package cannot be exported to a separate project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of the relationship when a diagram depends on an external element

For more information about the package dependencies on external elements, see .

In this case, if the diagram is not important to the used project, it can be moved from the used project package into any external package by dragging-and-dropping it within the Model Browser:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diagram is moved from the package *Items* to the package *Library*

**

The package*Items* can now be exported into an independent project.

Teamwork Cloud is an ideal solution for a collaborative work on the same project. For more information about using a modeling tool in the collaborative environment, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation' space='CM'].

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Identifying Package Dependencies' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package Dependencies panel' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Unresolved dependencies' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Before exporting or sharing a package as an independent used project, you need to identify and solve package dependencies first. More specifically, you have to make sure that the package</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> does not depend on any external elements (except other used projects). Furthermore, cyclic dependencies between several used projects are not allowed.</span></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The used project depends on the external elements which have relationships with the model elements from the used project packages.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9fd7b6c9-b31b-4f39-8fe9-e740e1ce9852"><ac:parameter ac:name="">external</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></span></p><h4>The used project depends on external elements</h4><p>If a used project's element has a relationship with an external element and that relationship is contained in the used project package, an error message appears when exporting the used package.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment center;">Error in the Package Dependencies panel</h6><p><br /></p><p>In this situation, a modeling tool can suggest moving the relationship into the parent package of this external element. For example, the package <em>Items</em> is a parent of a class <em>Book</em>, so the relationship can be moved from the <em>Items</em> into the package <em>Library</em>:</p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="resolved_package_dependency_on_external_element.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing dependencies among elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Resolved package dependency on an external element</h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p>Some movements can be achieved by clicking <strong>Solve</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Package Dependencies panel" /><ac:link-body><strong>Package Dependencies</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><h4><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The used project depends on an external element, but can be exported (with warning)</span><br /></span></h4><p>Even though the used project element has a relationship with an external element, that relationship is contained in an external package:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_legal_used_project_dependency_on_external_element.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing dependencies among elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a “legal” used project dependency on an external element</h6><p><br /></p><p>In this case, the dependency on an external element is displayed in the Model Browser:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="legal_used_project_dependency_on_external_element.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing dependencies among elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">“Legal” used project dependency on an external element in the Model Browser</h6><p>The package can be exported as the used project because the relationship is contained in an external package.</p><h4><br />The used project does not depend on an external element</h4><p>If the used project element has a relationship with an external element it is irrelevant in the context of UML (for instance, the external model uses the used project, but not vice versa) and that relationship is contained in an external model, the package can be exported into an independent used project:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_relationship_when_used_project_does_not_depend_on_external_element.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing dependencies among elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a relationship when the used project does not depend on an external element</h6><p><br /></p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="852ea357-1736-4818-b49a-18da28122c01"><ac:parameter ac:name="">reference</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Dependencies by reference</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">The used project depends on the external elements which have references to the model elements from the used project packages.</span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_dependency_by_reference.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing dependencies among elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Example of a dependency by reference</span></h6><p><br /></p><p>In this case, the package <em>Items</em> cannot be exported to an independent project because this package has reference to the <em>Library</em> package.</p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47cb74b9-9192-4cd8-9305-2b670b1dc1d9"><ac:parameter ac:name="">dependencies</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Diagram dependencies</h3><p>The diagram depends on all model elements displayed within it.</p><p>If the diagram is contained in the package <em>Items</em> and depends on external elements, this package cannot be exported to a separate project.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_relationship_when_diagram_depends_on_external_element.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing dependencies among elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of the relationship when a diagram depends on an external element</h6><p><br /></p><p>For more information about the package dependencies on external elements, see <ac:link ac:anchor="external"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[The used project depends on external elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>In this case, if the diagram is not important to the used project, it can be moved from the used project package into any external package by dragging-and-dropping it within the Model Browser:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_is_moved_from_package_Items_to_package_Library.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing dependencies among elements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Diagram is moved from the package <em>Items</em> to the package <em>Library</em></h6><p><em> </em></p><p>The package<em> Items</em> can now be exported into an independent project.</p><p>Teamwork Cloud is an ideal solution for a collaborative work on the same project. For more information about using a modeling tool in the collaborative environment, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CM" ri:content-title="Client-Side Collaborative Modeling Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Collaborative modeling]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="03aacdf1-2b4b-4437-81e9-96175e9ea5cf"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Identifying Package Dependencies" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="bf1d98ed-e74c-4051-9c73-2e1b0a80040d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Package Dependencies panel" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="1b47419c-d8e6-4b9c-96ea-d5d8f13fd179"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Unresolved dependencies" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991136 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00074: (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing dependencies in dependency matrix

- page_id: `303991136`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991136/2026x+Refresh1+Analyzing+dependencies+in+dependency+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:23.162+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements management > (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing Requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

830972946

830972948

830972947

You can create, analyze, and modify requirement relations in the matrix, which provides a compact visual view for analysis. Using a dependency matrix, you can also edit relations between a selected requirement and model element (i.e., add or remove a desired relation).

The Dependency Matrix enables:

- The visualization of more than one element relations from different diagrams.
- The capability to create domain-specific matrices and their templates.
- Ready-to-use matrix templates: traceability of elements and behavior diagrams, traceability of requirements, traceability of requirements and elements refining, satisfying, verifying them, and others.
- Faster creation of traceability links between elements (for example, between requirements and the architecture or requirements and test cases). This improvement saves a huge amount of time in comparison to linking elements in diagrams.

To create a dependency matrix

1. From a requirements specification package shortcut menu in the Containment tree, select **Create****Diagram** and then select one of the following:
  - Derive Requirement Matrix to create a Dependency Matrix to analyze, create, and modify derivation relations between a derived requirement and a source requirement.
  - Refine Requirement Matrix to create a Dependency Matrix to analyze, create, and modify refined relations between a requirement and a model element or a set of elements refining the requirement.
  - Satisfy Requirement Matrix to create a Dependency Matrix to analyze, create, and modify satisfaction relations between requirements and model elements fulfilling the requirements.
  - **Verify Requirement Matrix** to create a Dependency Matrix to analyze, create, or modify verification relations between requirements and named elements that can determine whether the systems fulfill the requirements.
2. Type a diagram name.
3. Select criteria and a scope to be represented in the matrix. You can specify requirements to be represented as rows and class elements to be represented as columns.
4. Click the **Refresh** button. 
The following figure shows how particular requirements satisfy particular model class elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a dependency matrix.

You can also remove redundant or create missing dependencies using a dependency matrix. For detailed instructions on how to use dependency matrixes, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Dependency Matrix' space=''].

830972945

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Dependency Matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Requirement matrices' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991133 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00075: (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing dependencies in relation map

- page_id: `303991133`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991133/2026x+Refresh1+Analyzing+dependencies+in+relation+map
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:22.915+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements management > (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing Requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

831044545

831044548

831044547

A relation map allows visual analysis among multiple levels of abstraction, such as how requirements are satisfied by other model elements. The relation map enables you to show existing relationships, visualize multilevel relationships, observe traceability from requirements to implementation, or any other analysis you need.

The relation map is useful for:

- Making fast analysis and overview of a model.
- Observing traceability from requirements to implementation all the way through different levels of abstraction (such as analysis, design, etc.).
- Discovering the requirements structure.

You are not allowed to edit relations in the relation map diagram.

To create a relation map

1. From a package or requirement shortcut menu in the Containment tree, select **Create Diagram** and then select one of the following:
  - Requirement Containment Map .
  - Requirement Derivation Map .
2. Type a diagram name.
3. Specify a relation criterion, element type, and scope.
4. Click the **Refresh** button. 
The following figure shows how particular requirements satisfy particular model class elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of relation map

For detailed instructions on how to use relation maps, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Relation Map' space=''].

831044543

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Relation Map' space='']
