<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989664 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00076: (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing Requirements

- page_id: `303989664`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989664/2026x+Refresh1+Analyzing+Requirements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:17:45.745+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements management
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The requirements analysis encompasses those tasks that determine the needs or conditions a new or altered product or project must meet. It considers the possibly conflicting requirements of the various stakeholders by analyzing, documenting, validating and managing software or system requirements. Requirements analysis is critical to the success or failure of a systems or software project.

You can analyze requirements using the following tools:

- Usages and Dependencies . Discovers the Requirements usage and dependencies on other model parts: traceability, change impact analysis.
- Dependency Matrix . Checks the Gap analysis, Change Impact analysis, Requirements completeness and correctness.
- Model validation . Checks Requirements completeness and correctness based on build in or custom validation suites.
- Traceability . Tracks, visualizes, navigates, and analyzes the elements involved in traceability relations.
- Relation Map . Visualizes the relations of multilevel requirements: traceability, coverage analysis.
- Generic and Requirements Tables . Makes the coverage analysis in a compact format.
- Report Wizard . Analysis (e.g. coverage) and estimation (e.g. functional points method) based on built in or custom documentation generation templates.

The following sections describe the procedures for analyzing requirements:

It considers the possibly conflicting requirements of the various stakeholders by analyzing, documenting, validating and managing software or system requirements. Requirements analysis is critical to the success or failure of a systems or software project.</p><p>You can analyze requirements using the following tools:</p><ul><li><strong>Usages and Dependencies</strong>. Discovers the Requirements usage and dependencies on other model parts: traceability, change impact analysis.</li><li><strong>Dependency Matrix</strong>. Checks the Gap analysis, Change Impact analysis, Requirements completeness and correctness.</li><li><strong>Model validation</strong>. Checks Requirements completeness and correctness based on build in or custom validation suites.</li><li><strong>Traceability</strong>. Tracks, visualizes, navigates, and analyzes the elements involved in traceability relations.</li><li><strong>Relation Map</strong>. Visualizes the relations of multilevel requirements: traceability, coverage analysis.</li><li><strong>Generic and Requirements Tables</strong>. Makes the coverage analysis in a compact format.</li><li><strong>Report Wizard</strong>. Analysis (e.g. coverage) and estimation (e.g. functional points method) based on built in or custom documentation generation templates.</li></ul><p>The following sections describe the procedures for analyzing requirements:</p><p class="atl-forced-newline"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="32f67e56-5911-42ce-9969-8b7c7a5e3994" /><br /><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997735 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00077: (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing traceability relations

- page_id: `303997735`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997735/2026x+Refresh1+Analyzing+traceability+relations
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:46.096+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Traceability
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1977687758

1977687761

1977687760

You can perform the impact and/or gap analysis in your project using the Dependency Matrix feature, which is a powerful way for representing traceability relations between multiple elements from different packages, levels of abstraction, views, or other relations that cannot be represented on diagrams, for example, relations through UML tags.You can create your own dependency matrices or even custom dependency matrix types for visualization of various traceability relations. You only need to define a relevant traceability property as **dependency criteria** for this.

The following figure depicts an example of traceability relations between several model elements.

###### Example of traceability relations

These relations can be represented by the following traceability properties:

- Realizing relations (use cases classes):
  - Realizing Class
  - Realizing Element
  - All Realizing Elements
- Specifying relations (classes use cases):
  - Specifying Use Case
  - Specifying Element
  - All Specifying Elements

You can create a dependency matrix to visualize these traceability relations.The following steps will show you how to create a dependency matrix for the Realizing Class predefined property.

To create a dependency matrix for the Realizing Class predefined property:

1. Create a Dependency Matrix diagram.
2. Define row and column type element types as follows:
  1. For the Row Element Type, select UseCase
  2. For the Column Element Type, select Class
3. Define row and column scopes as follows:
  1. For the Row Scope, click [ATTACHMENT filename='expand_button.png'] > Requirements > MagicTest and select Administration .
  2. For the column scope, click [ATTACHMENT filename='expand_button.png'] > Design > MagicTest and select UI .
4. Define the Realizing Class property as Dependency Criteria.
5. Rebuild the matrix (click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh_button.png'] in the diagram toolbar). [IMAGE alt='' src='']Defining criteria for visualization of traceability relationships that can be represented by Realizing Class predefined property

The following figure depicts the created Dependency Matrix showing the traceability relationships between use cases and design classes they realize:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Visualization of Realizing Class traceability relations on Dependency Matrix

1977687757

**Related Pages**

- Traceability

- 
  - Creating traceability relations
  - Traceability relations representation
  - Navigating between different levels of abstraction
  - Analyzing traceability relations
  - Predefined traceability rules
  - Custom traceability rules

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9df33ceb-76cb-4c44-a903-24b25ad1f31d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1977687758</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0d829793-d49b-4997-89d2-e94c10da076f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1977687761</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4276c2e-67c7-4aea-a2fb-79b9c1257825"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1977687760</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><br /></p><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>You can perform the impact and/or gap analysis in your project using the Dependency Matrix feature, which is a powerful way for representing traceability relations between multiple elements from different packages, levels of abstraction, views, or other relations that cannot be represented on diagrams, for example, relations through UML tags. </span>You can create your own dependency matrices or even custom dependency matrix types for visualization of various traceability relations. You only need to define a relevant traceability property as <strong>dependency criteria</strong> for this. </p><p><br /></p><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><span>The following figure depicts an example of traceability relations between several model elements.</span></p><p><span><br /></span></p></div><div class="layoutArea" style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_traceability_relations.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing traceability relations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></div><div class="layoutArea" style="text-align: center;"><div class="column"><h6><span>Example of traceability relations </span></h6></div></div><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p style="text-align: center;"><span> <br /> </span></p><p><span>These relations can be represented by the following traceability properties: </span></p><ul><li><span>Realizing relations (use cases </span> <span> </span> <span>classes):</span><ul><li><span>Realizing Class </span></li><li><span>Realizing Element</span></li><li>All Realizing Elements</li></ul></li><li>Specifying relations (classes use cases):<ul><li>Specifying Use Case</li><li>Specifying Element</li><li><span>All Specifying Elements </span></li></ul></li></ul><p><span> <br /> </span></p><p><span>You can create a dependency matrix to visualize these traceability relations. </span>The following steps will show you how to create a dependency matrix <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7c4f0005-310a-4f1a-80ad-54767974bb53">for</ac:inline-comment-marker> the Realizing Class predefined property.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a dependency matrix for the Realizing Class predefined property:</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Dependency Matrix diagram.</li><li>Define row and column type element types as follows:<ol><li>For the Row Element Type, select <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2eead9be-2581-4e08-812a-8a275589cf61">UseCase</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></li><li>For the Column Element Type, select <strong>Class</strong></li></ol></li><li>Define row and column scopes as follows:<ol><li>For the Row Scope, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing traceability relations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong> &gt; Requirements &gt; MagicTest</strong> and select <strong>Administration</strong>.</li><li>For the column scope, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expand_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing traceability relations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><strong>&gt; Design &gt; MagicTest</strong> and select <strong>UI</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>Define the <strong>Realizing Class</strong> property as Dependency Criteria.</li><li>Rebuild the matrix (click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing traceability relations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the diagram toolbar).<br /><br /><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dependency_matrix_criteria.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing traceability relations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">Defining criteria for visualization of traceability relationships that can be represented by Realizing Class predefined property</span></h6></li></ol><p>The following figure depicts the created Dependency Matrix showing the traceability relationships between use cases and design classes they realize:</p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><br /></span></p><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realizing_class_dependency_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Analyzing traceability relations" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></div></div><div class="layoutArea"><div class="column"><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span>Visualization of Realizing Class traceability relations on Dependency Matrix </span></h6></div></div></div></div></div></div></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d061180-cda7-4811-9cc9-59830c6876a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1977687757</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages</strong></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Traceability">Traceability</a></li></ul><ul class="list-children not-expandable"><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul class="list-children not-expandable"><li> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Creating+traceability+relations">Creating traceability relations</a></li><li> <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Traceability+relations+representation">Traceability relations representation</a></li><li> <a rules</a></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995534 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00078: (2026x Refresh1) Analyzing usages and dependencies

- page_id: `303995534`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995534/2026x+Refresh1+Analyzing+usages+and+dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:43.656+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

525949422

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

525949425

525960400

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

525949424

No Magic modeling tools provide a way to track and analyze element usages and dependencies in models, understand the relationships between used and dependent elements, and find the diagrams where these elements are represented. You can do all of this using the commands **Used By** and **Depends On**, as described below.

##### Used By command

The **Used By** command allows you to find all the elements that use a selected element (e.g., as property, tag etc.). This also includes usage in diagrams.

#### TIP: Example

Example

In the figure below, the *Computer* Propertyhas its type set to the *WorkStation* Class.**In other words, the *Computer* Property uses the *WorkStation* Class as its type. If you execute the **Used By** command for the *WorkStation* Class, you will get the*Computer* Property as one of the results.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window showing that the *WorkStation* Class is used by the *Computer* Property.

Note that containing (owning) other elements is not considered usage. For example, when a Package contains a Class, this does not mean that the Package uses the Class. The Package is only a container of the Class.

##### Depends On command

Use the **Depends On** command to find all elements used by a selected element (e.g., properties, tags etc.).

#### TIP: Example

Example

Let's say you have a model with the content tree shown in the figure below. Execute the **Depends On** command for the*Workplace Infrastructure*Package to see how its content depends on the outer elements (not owned by the Package) of the model. One of the search results shows that the*Workplace Infrastructure*Package depends on the *Power Socket* Class, which is the type of several Ports owned by this package.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The content tree of a sample model with the *Workplace Infrastructure*Package having dependencies to the outer elements of the model.

Note that****in this particular situation you must select the **Search recursively** check box in the **Usages/Dependencies Search Options** dialog. This allows you to search for dependencies of the Package content rather than the Package itself. Also, select the **Ignore inner interrelations** checkbox to eliminate the dependencies between the elements contained by the package from the search results.

##### Searching for usages and dependencies

You can find usages and dependencies of a particular model element by opening its shortcut menu from the model browser or a diagram.

To search for usages/dependencies of a model element

1. In the model browser or a diagram right-click the element, the usages or dependencies of which you want to find.
2. In the shortcut menu of the element click Related Elements , and select Used By or Depends On (depending on what you want to find).
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Usages and Dependencies Search Options dialog' space='']**Usages/Dependencies Search Options** dialog , select the search options you want to use.
4. Click OK to start the search.

The search results are listed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Usages and dependencies search results window' space=''] at the bottom of the modeling tool. The search results window also provides additional information, such as element type and how a particular element is used by/in the element for which you perform the search.

525949420

**Related pages**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Model analysis' space='']**

The search results window also provides additional information, such as element type and how a particular element is used by/in the element for which you perform the search.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="07e1ba14-ca1f-4b39-ab88-405f62c9ceeb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">525949420</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong class="confluence-link">Related pages</strong></p><p><strong class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b1f7fc5f-ff6e-420c-939d-92549e7c25e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Model analysis" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989942 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00080: (2026x Refresh1) Annotation

- page_id: `303989942`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989942/2026x+Refresh1+Annotation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:18:42.181+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Comment with or without the stereotype XSDannotation. The documentation content maps to the UML Comment body (name).

“documentation” maps as a UML comment, as outlined below:

- the “content” value - the comment name.

- the “xml:lang” value – the tag “xml:lang” value.

- the source value – the tag ”source” value.

“appinfo” maps as a tag value with the name “appInfoSource”:

- the “source” value will be the tag value.
- the “content” will be documentation for the tagged value.

When several annotation nodes appear on one element node, mapping shall be done as follows:

- the “documentation” text shall be merged into one UML comment with merged content, but the “content” and “xml:lang” tag values shall represent only the first matched values.
- “appInfo” shall have “content” merged into one tag “appInfoSource” comment, but the tag value shall represent the “appinfo” matched first.

#### PANEL: annotation XML representation summary

annotation XML representation summary

```text
<annotation
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace …}>
```

```text
Content: (anninfn I dorumpnrarioWl.
```

```text
</annotation>
```

```text
<appinfo
```

```text
source = anyURI>
```

```text
Content: ({any})*
```

```text
</appinfo>
```

```text
<documentation
```

```text
source = anyURI
```

```text
xml:lang = language>
```

```text
Content: ({any})*
```

```text
</documentation>
```

#### PANEL: XML representations of three kinds of annotation

XML representations of three kinds of annotation

```text
<xs:simpleType fn:note="special">
```

```text
<xs:annotation>
```

```text
<xs:documentation>A type for experts only</xs:documentation>		<xs:appinfo>
```

```text
<fn:specialHandling>checkForPrimes</fn:specialHandling>		</xs:appinfo>
```

```text
</xs:annotation>
```

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### annotation UML model example.

#### PANEL: annotation XML code sample

annotation XML code sample

```text
<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "http://nomagic.com" xmlns:xs = "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" targetNamespace = "http://nomagic.com">	<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:appinfo source = "infoSource">infoContent</xs:appinfo>		<xs:documentation source = "documentation source" xml:lang = "EN">the documentation for this schema</xs:documentation>	</xs:annotation>
```

```text
</xs:schema>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

## PAGE 00081: (2026x Refresh1) Any and anyAttribute

- page_id: `303990906`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990906/2026x+Refresh1+Any+and+anyAttribute
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:10.295+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Attribute with the stereotype XSDany or XSDanyAttribute.

maxOccurs - to multiplicity upper range. The unbounded value maps to an asterisk in UML.

minOccurs – to multiplicity lower range.

annotation maps to Attribute documentation.

Other properties map to TaggedValues.

#### PANEL: any and anyAttribute XML representation summary

any and anyAttribute XML representation summary

```text
<any
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
maxOccurs = (nonNegativeInteger | unbounded) : 1
```

```text
minOccurs = nonNegativeInteger : 1
```

```text
namespace = ((##any | ##other)| List of (anyURI | (##targetNamespace |
```

```text
##local)) ) : ##any 	processContents = (lax | skip | strict) : strict
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</any>
```

```text
<anyAttribute
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
namespace = ((##any | ##other) | List of (anyURI | (##targetNamespace |
```

```text
##local)) ) : ##any
```

```text
processContents = (lax | skip | strict) : strict
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)</anyAttribute>
```

#### PANEL: XML representations of the four basic types of wildcard

XML representations of the four basic types of wildcard

```text
<xs:any processContents = "skip"/>
```

```text
<xs:any namespace = "##other" processContents="lax"/>
```

```text
<xs:any namespace = "http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform"/>
```

```text
<xs:any namespace = "##targetNamespace"/>
```

```text
<xs:anyAttribute namespace = "http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace"/>
```

```

```

```

```

```

```

```

```

```

```text
<xs:annotation>
```

```text

```

```text
</xs:annotation>
```

```

</xs:schema>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990555 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00082: (2026x Refresh1) Appendix A: report extensions

- page_id: `303990555`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990555/2026x+Refresh1+Appendix+A+report+extensions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:46.169+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1352447286

1352447286

1352447286

Report Wizard has been enhanced with an extension that provides additional functionalities (for example, a Custom Tool that encapsulates Java functions in context). The report extensions are Java class and related resources collected into JAR archives, which are merely ZIP files that contain a specially formatted “manifest” file describing the contents of the archive.

To install the extension, copy the *.jar* file into the extensions directory under the Report Wizard plugin directory or template directory. The archives stored within subdirectories will not be loaded in Report Wizard, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Report Wizard Plug-in Directory Structure.

The extension deployed under the global extensions directory will affect all templates, which means you can use the deployed Custom Tool in all templates. Extensions deployed under a template package will affect only the specific template.

1352447285

**Related pages**

The report extensions are Java class and related resources collected into JAR archives, which are merely ZIP files that contain a specially formatted “manifest” file describing the contents of the archive.</p><p>To install the extension, copy the <em>.jar</em> file into the extensions directory under the Report Wizard plugin directory or template directory. The archives stored within subdirectories will not be loaded in Report Wizard, see figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Report Wizard Plug-in Directory Structure" ac:alt="Report Wizard Plug-in Directory Structure"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Extensions Deployment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Appendix A: report extensions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">Report Wizard Plug-in Directory Structure.</h6><p>The extension deployed under the global extensions directory will affect all templates, which means you can use the deployed Custom Tool in all templates. Extensions deployed under a template package will affect only the specific template.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d608ca77-9395-42b0-8788-59f20f350bfa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1352447285</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="5e747ab4-111f-402e-a497-b2836dc5f39c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996354 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00083: (2026x Refresh1) Appendix B: office open XML format template

- page_id: `303996354`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996354/2026x+Refresh1+Appendix+B+office+open+XML+format+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:38.191+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1353187275

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1353187276

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1353187276

Office Open XML (also referred to as OOXML or Open XML) refers to the standardized file formats of spreadsheets, charts, presentations, and word processing documents for Microsoft Office. These standardized file formats become free and open as ECMA-376 Office Open XML File Formats - 2nd edition (December 2008) and ISO/IEC 29500:2008.

The most common Open XML file formats that Report Wizard supports include:

- DOCX - for word processing (text) documents
- XLSX - for spreadsheets
- PPTX - for presentations

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Support Office Open XML (DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX) and Microsoft Office 2007 and newer versions.

1353187274

**Related pages**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996369 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00084: (2026x Refresh1) Appendix C: OpenDocument format template

- page_id: `303996369`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996369/2026x+Refresh1+Appendix+C+OpenDocument+format+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:41.204+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1355361738

1355361739

1355361739

The OpenDocument format (*ODF*) is an open file format for office documents, such as spreadsheets, presentations, and word processing documents. The standard was developed by the Open Office *XML* technical committee of the Organization for the Advancement of Structured Information Standards (OASIS) consortium.

The version 1.0 manifestation was published as an ISO/IEC International Standard, ISO/IEC 26300:2006 Open Document Format for Office Applications (OpenDocument) v1.0.

The most common files supported by Report Wizard are:

- ODT for word processing (text) documents
- ODS for spreadsheets
- ODP for presentations

1355361738

**Related pages**

The standard was developed by the Open Office <em>XML</em> technical committee of the Organization for the Advancement of Structured Information Standards (OASIS) consortium.</p><p>The version 1.0 manifestation was published as an ISO/IEC International Standard, ISO/IEC 26300:2006 Open Document Format for Office Applications (OpenDocument) v1.0.</p><p>The most common files supported by Report Wizard are:</p><ul><li><em>ODT</em> <br />for word processing (text) documents</li><li><em>ODS</em> <br />for spreadsheets</li><li><em>ODP</em> <br />for presentations</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="019e42de-51d9-4046-a350-f1f7095ec904"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1355361738</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bdc85593-179d-4d8d-8bc5-7696d304540a" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00085: (2026x Refresh1) Appendix D: HTML tag support

- page_id: `303996366`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996366/2026x+Refresh1+Appendix+D+HTML+tag+support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:26:40.260+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard supports *HTML* code conversion to *RTF, ODF,* and *OOXML* file formats. The *HTML* code is created from the Element Documentation pane, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Documentation Pane.

The element documentation can also be retrieved by the following *VTL* code:

```text

```

Whenever the report engine encounters *HTML* content, it will automatically convert the content into a valid output format style.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <em>HTML</em> code is created from the Element Documentation pane, see figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="MagicDraw Documentation Pane" ac:alt="MagicDraw Documentation Pane"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MD doc panel.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Appendix D: HTML tag support" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">The Documentation Pane.</h6><p>The element documentation can also be retrieved by the following <em>VTL</em> code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05929382-6822-42a5-943c-1eea4ff279ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($e in $elements)

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990250 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00086: (2026x Refresh1) Appendix E: DocBook Support

- page_id: `303990250`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990250/2026x+Refresh1+Appendix+E+DocBook+Support
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:19.548+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1357340520

1357340520

1357340520

DocBook was built as an application to write technical documents related to computer hardware and software. It is a semantic markup language that was extended from XML language. As an open source application, DocBook has been adopted as a standard to write other types of documentation as well. The current version of DocBook, published by O'Reilly Media and XML Press, is 5.1.

The Report Wizard’s DocBook engine enables you to input the *VTL* codes inside DocBook’s document content. It also allows you to convert HTML into DocBook tags, rearrange tags, and escape special characters. Report Wizard uses “*dbk*” and “*docbook*” file extensions to save DocBook files. The figure below shows the DocBook’s document content.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1357340519

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1879d7d-73b1-46ad-983f-20b4c8b023f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357340520</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f2a02b0-82d7-4b87-85a4-142f445e5220"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357340520</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e93512d9-88a3-462b-b3d2-e3fbbf50029d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357340520</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>DocBook was built as an application to write technical documents related to computer hardware and software. It is a semantic markup language that was extended from XML language. As an open source application, DocBook has been adopted as a standard to write other types of documentation as well. The current version of DocBook, published by O'Reilly Media and XML Press, is 5.1.</p><p>The Report Wizard’s DocBook engine enables you to input the <em>VTL</em> codes inside DocBook’s document content. It also allows you to convert HTML into DocBook tags, rearrange tags, and escape special characters. Report Wizard uses “<em>dbk</em>” and “<em>docbook</em>” file extensions to save DocBook files. The figure below shows the DocBook’s document content.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="A DocBook Template" ac:alt="A DocBook Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="DocBook template.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Appendix E: DocBook Support" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e7b495d-dd8b-494d-94cd-c5243c79db1b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357340519</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="9d0420e6-393b-4ce7-ad50-d65d4b6d04ae" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993578 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00087: (2026x Refresh1) Applying Actual Measurements

- page_id: `303993578`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993578/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+Actual+Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:18.897+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures > (2026x Refresh1) Measurements > (2026x Refresh1) Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['make-element-measurable', 'set-default-value-for-actual-property-set', 'dv', 'apply-actual-measurement', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Actual Measurements can be applied to Measurable Elements and Property Sets. 
 
To make a Measurable Element measurable

1. Create a Measurement Set with one Measurement at least. For a Property Set, it is sufficient to define measurements as an owned property.
2. Select the element you want to measure and open its Specification window.
3. Add the created Measurement Set as a value for the Measurement Set property.

You can apply the Measurement Set by dragging it from the Model Browser onto the element.

As soon as you make the element measurable, define actual values for all measurements contained within the Measurement Set.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Define the default values in the Specification window of the Measurement Set, and you can use them when creating Actual Property Sets. You can use the default values with just one click of the **Default Value** button in the element's Specification window. 
 
To set the default value for the Actual Property Set

1. Select the element for which you want to specify the Actual Property Set and open its Specification window.
2. In the Actual <measurement type name> property group, click the **dv** button to assign the default value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- The default values are set automatically when the measurement’s multiplicity is set to 1 and more.
- When you remove all values of the Actual Measurement Set, the Actual Property Set is removed from the model as well.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can use the default values with just one click of the <strong>Default Value</strong> button in the element's Specification window. </span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> To set the default value for the Actual Property Set<br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Select the element for which you want to specify the Actual Property Set and open its Specification window.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the Actual &lt;measurement type name&gt; property group, click the <strong>dv</strong> button to assign the default value.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Actual Measurements" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="208651de-8398-4fae-8b88-43d30065bbd0"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The default values are set automatically when the measurement’s multiplicity is set to 1 and more.</li><li>When you remove all values of the Actual Measurement Set, the Actual Property Set is removed from the model as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990748 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00088: (2026x Refresh1) Applying actual project status in NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `303990748`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990748/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+actual+project+status+in+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:55.821+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Programme Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To apply the actual project status to a new milestone

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Apply Status .
2. Specify the actual project status by following steps of the Project Status Application Wizard .

To edit the actual project status

1. From the milestone shortcut menu, select Edit Status .
2. Edit the actual project status by following the steps of the Project Status Application Wizard .

Specify Project Status

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992298 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00089: (2026x Refresh1) Applying automated post-processing scripts after ReqIF file import

- page_id: `303992298`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992298/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+automated+post-processing+scripts+after+ReqIF+file+import
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:51.071+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements management > (2026x Refresh1) Importing requirements > (2026x Refresh1) Importing ReqIF files
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the ReqIF file import, you can apply an automated post-processing script to your project in order to modify requirements and create custom mapping upon requirements interchange through ReqIF.

In order to see the scripts and execute them, you must be using ReqIF Profile. To set the profile, go to **File** > **Use Project**, then scroll to select *ReqIF Profile.mdzip* and click **Finish**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To apply the post-processing rules

1. From the Options menu, select Project .
2. In the options group list, select General , then scroll down to the ReqIF options group.
3. Under the ReqIF Import category, find Post-processing rules and select the one you want to apply.
4. Click OK .

| Post-processing Rule | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Fill Empty Requirement Name | When applied to a requirement with an unspecified Name property, the rule picks the data from Element ID and uses it as the element name. |
| Set CustomID as ID | When applied to a requirement with an empty Element ID, the rule sets the specified CustomID as an element ID. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>After the ReqIF file import, you can apply an automated post-processing script to your project in order to modify requirements and create custom mapping upon requirements interchange through ReqIF.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d942ed88-5d32-4e2f-ac22-050f019816a6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In order to see the scripts and execute them, you must be using ReqIF Profile. To set the profile, go to <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Use Project</strong>, then scroll to select <em>ReqIF Profile.mdzip</em> and click <strong>Finish</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="140" ac:width="581"><ri:attachment ri:filename="reqif_profile_used.jpg"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying automated pre-processing scripts before ReqIF file export" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p>To apply the post-processing rules</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the options group list, select <strong>General</strong>, then scroll down to the <strong>ReqIF</strong> options group.</li><li>Under the<strong> ReqIF Import</strong> category, find <strong>Post-processing rules</strong> and select the one you want to apply.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><table><tbody><tr><th style="text-align: left;">Post-processing Rule</th><th style="text-align: center;">Description</th></tr><tr><td>Fill Empty Requirement Name</td><td>When applied to a requirement with an unspecified <strong>Name</strong> property, the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0e6ad2d6-0dac-458f-b39e-ef25257112c2">r</ac:inline-comment-marker>ule picks the data from <strong>Element ID</strong> and uses it as the element name.</td></tr><tr><td>Set CustomID as ID</td><td>When applied to a requirement with an empty <strong>Element ID</strong>, the rule sets the specified <strong>CustomID</strong> as an element ID.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993800 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00090: (2026x Refresh1) Applying automated pre-processing scripts before ReqIF file export

- page_id: `303993800`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993800/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+automated+pre-processing+scripts+before+ReqIF+file+export
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:37.940+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Requirements management > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting requirements
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Prior to the ReqIF file export, you can apply an automated pre-processing script to your project in order to modify requirements and create custom mapping upon requirements interchange through ReqIF.

In order to see the scripts and execute them, you must be using ReqIF Profile. To set the profile, go to**File**>**Use Project**, then scroll to select *ReqIF Profile.mdzip* and click **Finish**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To apply the pre-processing rule

1. From the Options menu, select Project .
2. In the options group list, select General , then scroll down to the ReqIF options group.
3. Under **ReqIF Export**, find **Pre-processing rules** and select the one you want to apply.
4. Click OK .

| Pre-processing Rule | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Restore Empty Requirement Name | When applied to a requirement with a specified Name property, the rule removes the element name upon export. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995071 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00091: (2026x Refresh1) Applying image

- page_id: `303995071`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995071/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+image
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:14.013+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Formatting symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Ways to apply image on a symbol

###### The Image Library tab

To apply an image on a symbol using the**Image Library** tab

1. From the Window menu, select Image Library. The Image Library tab opens on the right side of the window.
2. Select the image you want to apply from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Image Library tab' space=''] tree.
3. Drag the image directly on the symbol. The image is applied on a symbol. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Select Image dialog

To apply an image on a symbol using **Select Image** dialog

1. Open the **Select Image** dialog by doing one of the following: Only mentioned elements support the **Image Library** button on the smart manipulator by default. You can customize the smart manipulator of any other element.
  - Open the Specification window of any element, select **Image**(**Icon**for Stereotype) property cell and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
  - Select Class, Actor, Port, Block, Part, Node, Performer, Concept Role, Stereotype, or Text Box ((HTML Text) on the diagram pane and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] on the smart manipulator toolbar.
2. Select the image you want to apply.
3. Click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The image is applied on a symbol.

###### The external resources

To apply an image on a symbol from the external resources

1. Select an image from any external resource (e.g. web browser, email). Image formatsYou can apply images of the following formats: .gif, .jpg, .jpeg, .svg, .png, .wmf.
2. Drag it directly on a symbol. 
The image is applied on a symbol. If the the image that you applied on a symbol is large, you can resize that symbol to a preferred size automatically.To resize a symbol to a preferred sizea. Select the symbol with an image applied. 
b. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] smart manipulator that appears at the right bottom side of the shape’s horizontal border.

##### Applying image to Property or its type

When applying image to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part' space='MED']shape (which type is specified) by draggingor selecting an image from the[Image Library](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Applying+image#Applyingimage-TheImageLibrarytab) or [other resources](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Applying+image#Applyingimage-Theexternalresources), and the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting port and part types' space='']**Type Selection Mode** is switched on,the image is applied to the Property type and the notification message is shown by default. For example, if you drag an image on the *workplace* Property shape that type is *Workplace* Class, the image will be applied to the Class but not to the Property.

#### WARNING: Exceptions

Exceptions

- If the Property already has the image applied, the new image replaces it and is set to the Property but not to it's type.
- If the Property type already has an image applied, you cannot replace it with the new one by dragging it on Property shape.

To apply image to Property type

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space=''], switch on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Selecting port and part types' space='']**Type Selection Mode**.
2. Select an image from the [Image Library](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Applying+image#Applyingimage-TheImageLibrarytab) or [other resources](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Applying+image#Applyingimage-Theexternalresources).
3. Drag selected image on the Property shape which: 
 - Type is specified. 
 - The Property is without image. 
 - The Property type is without image. 
The image is applied to the Property type and the notification message is shown by default. You can specify to apply image on Property by changing the **Apply Image To** property value.

#### NOTE: Symbol display mode

Symbol display mode

You can change the symbol display mode. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MED']>]]>

To specify the **Apply Image To** property value

1. On the main menu, select the **Options** > **Project**.
2. In the project Options dialog, find **Apply Image To** property.
3. Select one of the following value: 
 - **Type and Notify** (default) - to apply image to Property type and after that show notification message on the right corner of the screen. 
 - **Type** - to apply image to Property type. 
 - **Property** - to apply image to Property.
4. Click **OK**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

ac:macro-id="f5756551-3db2-42e3-a3e6-553ce6d098e6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Only mentioned elements support the <strong>Image Library</strong> button on the smart manipulator by default. You can customize the smart manipulator of any other element.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span> </span></p></li><li data-uuid="9b0e3492-f27d-4e03-aedd-ae1577eadfcd"><span>Select the image you want to apply.</span></li><li data-uuid="076d91c7-c04f-46e3-9329-d09ee60aea32"><span><span class="confluence-link">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span><br class="confluence-link" /></span></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Select Image dialog" ac:alt="Select Image dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_image_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying image" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><span class="confluence-link">The image is applied on a symbol.</span><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><h4>The external resources</h4><p>To apply an image on a symbol from the external resources</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="01035ed5-c634-456e-b1e8-fa8abf9d888f"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select an image from any external resource (e.g. web browser, email).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e934d2a1-e42d-4e81-85de-988abbb68835"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Image formats</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can apply images of the following formats: .gif, .jpg, .jpeg, .svg, .png, .wmf.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="abcdb34b-5c68-422b-be41-4cd9ad3a22a2"><p>Drag it directly on a symbol.<span> </span><br /><span class="confluence-link">The image is applied on a symbol.</span><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e69884e0-1129-415c-ae39-3ec2d1884d5e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the the image that you applied on a symbol is large, you can resize that symbol to a preferred size automatically.</p><p>To resize a symbol to a preferred size</p><hr /><p>a. Select the symbol with an image applied.<br />b. Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="make_preferred_size.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying image" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> smart manipulator that appears at the right bottom side of the shape’s horizontal border.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3><span class="confluence-link">Applying image to Property or its type<br /></span></h3><p><span class="confluence-link">When applying image to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Property ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>shape (which type is specified) by dragging<span class="confluence-link"> or selecting an image from the<a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Applying+image#Applyingimage-TheImageLibrarytab"> Image Library</a> or <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Applying+image#Applyingimage-Theexternalresources">other resources</a></span>, <span class="confluence-link">and the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Selecting port and part types" /><ac:link-body><strong>Type Selection Mode</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link> is switched on, </span>the image is applied to the Property type and the notification message is shown by default. <span class="confluence-link"> For example, if you drag an image on the <em>workplace</em> Property shape that type is <em>Workplace</em> Class, the image will be applied to the Class but not to the Property.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c65f7c27-0e83-4d88-a53b-8bb00e7527ef"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Exceptions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="69e10214-c682-4d9d-9504-21d7922d732e"><span class="confluence-link">If the Property already has the image applied, the new image replaces it and is set to the Property but not to it's type.</span></li><li data-uuid="9b53ebf7-796b-4cd1-b98e-9d1149360c46"><span class="confluence-link">If the Property type already has an image applied, you cannot replace it with the new one by dragging it on Property shape.<br /></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span class="confluence-link"> </span></p><p><span class="confluence-link">To apply image to Property type</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="36db4051-68cf-45dd-812a-b6d9b62ec488"><span class="confluence-link">In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, switch on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Selecting port and part types" /><ac:link-body><strong>Type Selection Mode</strong></ac:link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="36ffa062-cce1-4244-9145-a072f140ae8b"><span class="confluence-link">Select an image from the <span class="confluence-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Applying+image#Applyingimage-TheImageLibrarytab"> Image Library</a> or <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Applying+image#Applyingimage-Theexternalresources">other resources</a></span>.</span></li><li data-uuid="5aa8381e-69d1-46b8-9afa-8a6b6ab17e19"><span class="confluence-link">Drag selected image on the Property shape which:<br />    - Type is specified.<br />    - The Property is without image.<br />    - The Property type <span class="confluence-link">is without image.</span><br />The image is applied to the Property type and the notification message is shown by default.</span><span class="confluence-link"> You can specify to apply image on Property by changing the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Apply Image To</strong> property</span> value. <br /><br /></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd0300b9-7b48-436c-aa10-bda05dd153f1"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Symbol display mode</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can change the symbol display mode. <ac:link ac:anchor="Changing the stereotype display mode"><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to change the stereotype name and its icon visibility on an element's shape >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><span class="confluence-link">To specify the <strong>Apply Image To</strong> property value<br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="07d7f337-9e10-4c4e-beef-dbd88912ae55"><span class="confluence-link">On the main menu, select the <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</span></li><li data-uuid="dce9f749-5be2-429c-961a-3e4c0e68f9c9"><span class="confluence-link">In the project Options dialog, find <strong>Apply Image To</strong> property.</span></li><li data-uuid="3cde480e-a233-4112-8d28-f5bdaff5aa69"><span class="confluence-link">Select one of the following value:<br /> - <strong>Type and Notify</strong> (default) - to apply image to Property type and after that show notification message on the right corner of the screen.<br /> - <strong>Type</strong> - to apply image to Property type.<br /> - <strong>Property</strong> - to apply image to Property.<br class="confluence-link" /></span></li><li data-uuid="1b6408b1-99ca-4903-b7d9-d12323a9cdba"><span class="confluence-link">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000140 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00092: (2026x Refresh1) Applying industrial symbols

- page_id: `304000140`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000140/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+industrial+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:51.946+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Industrial symbols can be applied to the nodes, performers, and concept roles in the OV-1 and NOV-1 diagrams.

If you want to use industrial symbols in your model, you can

- create Performer with Image or Concept Role with Image from the toolbar (1)
- click the Image Library item on the element smart manipulator (2)

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Two ways of using Image Library

Use the **Select Image** dialog to browse the hierarchy of the industrial symbols. The dialog provides the Simple Search. In the search field, type what you want to find in the hierarchy.

Recently used

You can also apply an industrial symbol to any UAF element by using the element’s specification window, image property.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Industrial symbols can be applied to the nodes, performers, and concept roles in the OV-1 and NOV-1 diagrams.</p><p>If you want to use industrial symbols in your model, you can</p><ul><li>create <strong>Performer with Image</strong> or <strong>Concept Role with Image</strong> from the toolbar (1)</li><li>click the<strong> Image Library</strong> item on the element smart manipulator (2)</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="industrial_symbols.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying industrial symbols" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Two ways of using Image Library</h6><p>Use the <strong>Select Image</strong> dialog to browse the hierarchy of the industrial symbols. The dialog provides the Simple Search. In the search field, type what you want to find in the hierarchy.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5835c62e-a8be-49d7-9f88-05f425b41c87"><ac:rich-text-body>You can easily find recently used symbols by opening the <strong>Recently used</strong> tab.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />You can also apply an industrial symbol to any UAF element by using the element’s specification window, image property.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990992 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00093: (2026x Refresh1) Applying layout template

- page_id: `303990992`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990992/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+layout+template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:14.886+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Layout templates
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you want to use the layout template in another diagram (usually the Composite Structure diagram), the following conditions must be met beforehand:

- The Part must be typed by the Class or subtype of Class in the Composite Structure diagram.
- The appearance of the Part type Class or subtype of Class must be defined in the Class diagram.
- That Class diagram must be set as the layout template.

You can apply layout templates directly from the diagram toolbar or shape's shortcut menu by using the **Apply Template** command. This command provides different suggestions in the following situations:

- Apply Template without drop-down arrow appears when only one layout template is available for the selected element.
- Apply Template with drop-down arrow appears when multiple layout templates are available for the selected element. The multiple layout templates are listed according to the following rules:

If you set the default layout template, it is always the first on the list, and other templates are listed alphabetically. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Setting layout template as default' space=''].If you have not set the default layout template, all layout templates are listed alphabetically.

The procedures below explain how to apply the layout template to:

- 
- Class and all classifiers
- 

###### Applying a layout template to the Part

There are several different ways you can apply a layout template to a Part. Use one of the workflows below depending on your situation.

To apply a layout template to the Part Property displayed in the Composite Structure Diagram

- Do one of the following:
  - Right click the Part Property shape to which you want to apply the layout template and select Display > Layout Template > Apply Template . Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_icon.png'] and select Layout Template > Apply Template . Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.

To apply a layout template when creating a new Part Property in the Composite Structure diagram

- 
  - Drag the Class that is the type of the Part Property you want to create on the diagram pane and do one of the following:
    - Drop the Class.
    - Hold down the mouse button until the tooltip appears (long drag), then release the mouse button and select the Create Property Symbol and Apply Default Template command.

###### Applying a layout template to the Class and all classifiers

You can apply a layout template for the Class and all classifiers that are set as the layout template, but are represented in other Class diagrams. To do this, follow the procedure below.

To apply a layout template to the Class and all classifiers

1. Select the Class shape or another classifier shape that has the layout template defined.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right click the shape and select Display > Layout Template > **Apply Template**. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_icon.png'] and select Layout Template > Apply Template . Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.

###### Applying a layout template to the diagram frame

When creating the internal structure for the Class that has a layout template defined, you can keep the same layout and style of its Ports on the Composite Structure Diagram frame.

To apply a layout template to the diagram frame

1. Select the diagram frame.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right click the diagram and select Display > Layout Template > **Apply Template**. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.
  - In the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_icon.png'] and select Layout Template > Apply Template . Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.

The example below illustrates how the appearance of Ports defined in*the Air Transfer System* layout template is applied to the Internal Block Diagram of the *Air Transfer System* Block.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The layout template applied on the *Air Transfer System* Internal Block Diagram frame.

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figure of this page is **Modem Cable** which you can download here: *[Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1481817717837&api=v2).*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>If you want to use the layout template in another diagram (usually the Composite Structure diagram), the following conditions must be met beforehand:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2cb52f42-38ff-4324-9698-f8587564a8f6">The Part must be typed by the Class or subtype of Class in the Composite Structure diagram.</li><li data-uuid="92c49f43-9d7c-40dc-8311-b102fdef86e8">The appearance of the Part type Class or subtype of Class must be defined in the Class diagram.</li><li data-uuid="207eb294-d349-46b4-b100-c0d33bf32f22">That Class diagram must be set as the layout template.</li></ul><p>You can apply layout templates directly from the diagram toolbar or shape's shortcut menu by using the <strong>Apply Template</strong> command. This command provides different suggestions in the following situations:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e124ad7f-c24c-4546-893d-a329c99a4d6d"><strong>Apply Template</strong> without drop-down arrow appears when only one layout template is available for the selected element.</li><li data-uuid="e645855a-eb07-4036-85ae-3655c039e2fd"><strong>Apply Template</strong> with drop-down arrow appears when multiple layout templates are available for the selected element. The multiple layout templates are listed according to the following rules:</li></ul><p>If you set the default layout template, it is always the first on the list, and other templates are listed alphabetically. <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Setting layout template as default" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Click to learn how to set the default layout template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>If you have not set the default layout template, all layout templates are listed alphabetically.</p><p>The procedures below explain how to apply the layout template to:</p><ul><li class="confluence-link" data-uuid="bd495392-d8e9-4288-b0d9-9cd9a633b780"><ac:link ac:anchor="Applying a layout template to the Part"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="f299de60-cdc0-4929-9411-2fac33b258a4"><ac:link ac:anchor="Applying a layout template to the Class and all classifiers"><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Class and all classifiers</span></ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="2fd5f4bb-6f92-4aa0-b3f0-e8cdf97ae789"><ac:link ac:anchor="Applying a layout template to the diagram frame"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Diagram frame]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><h4><br />Applying a layout template to the Part</h4><p>There are several different ways you can apply a layout template to a Part. Use one of the workflows below depending on your situation.</p><p><br /></p><p>To apply a layout template to the Part Property displayed in the Composite Structure Diagram</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="3c4bbfc9-018c-49ce-a1b7-2b166881a110">Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right click the Part Property shape to which you want to apply the layout template and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt;<strong> Apply Template</strong>. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.</li><li>In the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying layout template" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt;<strong> Apply Template</strong>. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To apply a layout template when creating a new Part Property in the Composite Structure diagram</p><hr /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="7d84cc32-08b9-4b92-99d4-04d5353de364"><ul><li>Drag the Class that is the type of the Part Property you want to create on the diagram pane and do one of the following:<ul><li>Drop the Class.</li><li>Hold down the mouse button until the tooltip appears (long drag), then release the mouse button and select the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Apply Default Template </strong>command.<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><h4>Applying a layout template to the Class and all classifiers</h4><p>You can apply a layout template for the Class and all classifiers that are set as the layout template, but are represented in other Class diagrams. To do this, follow the procedure below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To apply a layout template to the Class and all classifiers</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="cf22a671-0518-4db0-8eeb-ec1a6bbae13c">Select the Class shape or another classifier shape that has the layout template defined.</li><li data-uuid="54033c18-2af0-4406-83b4-fa1530945cca">Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right click the shape and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt;<span><strong> Apply Template</strong>. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.</span></li><li>In the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying layout template" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt; <strong>Apply Template</strong>. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.<br /><br /></li></ul></li></ol><h4>Applying a layout template to the diagram frame</h4><p>When creating the internal structure for the Class that has a layout template defined, you can keep the same layout and style of its Ports on the Composite Structure Diagram frame.</p><p><br /></p><p>To apply a layout template to the diagram frame</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="aea90c63-440d-4574-84a3-0ac0e9d6596f">Select the diagram frame.</li><li data-uuid="0b322b5f-059e-4dd6-a2c2-a2c78904cc49">Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right click the diagram and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt;<span><strong> Apply Template</strong>. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.</span></li><li>In the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying layout template" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt; <strong>Apply Template</strong>. Then select the name of the desired layout template if you can apply more than one template.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br />The example below illustrates how the appearance of Ports defined in<em> the Air Transfer System</em> layout template is applied to the Internal Block Diagram of the <em>Air Transfer System</em> Block.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="layout_on_diagram_frame.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying layout template" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The layout template applied on the <em>Air Transfer System</em> Internal Block Diagram frame.</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The sample model used in the figure of this page is <strong>Modem Cable</strong> which you can download here: <span class="confluence-link"><em> <a Templates.mdzip</a>.</em></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
