<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997720 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00094: (2026x Refresh1) Applying Legend Items

- page_id: `303997720`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997720/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+Legend+Items
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:00.215+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Legends > (2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Items
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Adorned Properties

1283264726

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Adorned Properties

1283264728

1283264725

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Adorned Properties

1283264727

When you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends' space=''] a Legend in a diagram or table, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Item adornment' space=''] of its Legend Items isappliedto the symbols or table rows of corresponding elements. You can define the elements to which a Legend Item should be applied by specifying one of its properties in the **Apply To** property group. Legend Items can be applied either to**Specific Elements** or to **Elements By Condition**. If you apply a Legend to a table, you can also specify the **Adorned Properties** property, which defines what cells of the relevant table rows should be adorned.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

You can specify either the **Specific Elements**or the **Elements by Condition** property of a Legend Item, but not both.

##### Applying Legend Items to specific elements

You can apply a Legend Item to one or multiple specific elements as described below.

To apply a Legend Item to specific elements

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend Item you want to apply.
2. Select the property specification cell of the Specific Elements property, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_short.png'] .
3. On the left side of the Select Elements dialog, select the elements to which you want to apply the Legend Item.
4. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_button_long.png'] to add them to the Selected elements area, and click OK .
5. Close the Specification window.

You can also apply one or multiple Legend Items to an element from its symbol.

To apply Legend Items to an element from its symbol

1. In a diagram, right-click the shape of the element to which you want to apply Legend Items, and in the menu, select Legend Item .
2. In the open dialog, select the Legend Items you want to apply, and click the Apply button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example how to apply Legend Items from an element shape.

##### Applying Legend Items to elements by conditionApplying Legend Items to elements by condition

Applying a Legend Item to **Elements by Condition** ensures that it is consistently applied to all the elements that meet the defined condition, even after you change your model.

**Example**

Let's say that you want to apply a Legend Item to all requirements that have a high-risk level. The quickest way to do it is to create a Property Test operation. Follow the steps below to analyze this specific use case and define the **Elements by Condition** property for the Legend Item:

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend Item.
2. Select the specification cell of the **Elements by Condition** property, and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. In the **Element by Condition** dialog, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] next to the **Element Type** box.
4. In the Property Test operation specification area of the **Elements by Condition** dialog, select the **risk** property and set its value to **High**. Default optionsBy default, the **Language** is set to **StructuredExpression,** and the Property Test operation is selected in the **Elements by Condition** dialog.
5. Click OK , and close the Specification window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Elements by Condition** dialog with the Property Test operation defined to test if Requirements have a high-risk level.

After following the above steps, the Legend Item is applied to all Requirements that have the **Risk** property set to **high**. If this use case does not meet your needs, you may define advanced Property Test operation properties. You may also[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating new operations' space='']of a different type, or create several operations for a single condition, as long as it returns a Boolean data type value (*true*or*false*). In addition, you can define a condition using a different**Language**setting.

##### Applying Legend Items to specific cells of a table

When a Legend is used in a table, you can specify not only the elements to which specific Legend Items should apply (the **Specific Elements** or **Elements By Condition** properties) but also the desired properties of these elements. In other words, you can adorn specific cells of a table instead of adorning entire table rows. To do this, use the **Adorned Properties** property of a Legend Item.

#### NOTE: Adorned Properties

Adorned Properties

The **Adorned Properties** property of a Legend Item is only effective if you use the Legend in a table.

**Example**

Let's say you have a Requirements Table with three columns: *Name,* *Text,* and *Risk*., and you want to apply two legends in that table: *Risk* and *Status*. When you create or use a Legend in a table, Legend Item adornments are normally applied to enitre table rows. So in this case, you also have to specify certain element properties (table columns) to which Legend Items of each Legend should be applied. To reproduce this spesific use case, follow these steps:

1. Create a Requirement Table with the Name , Text and Risk columns.
2. In the table, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends' space=''] to indicate the risk level of Requirements and name the Legend Risk .
3. Create and define the Legend Items for the Risk Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''] indicating three possible Requirement risk levels and name them High , Medium , and Low .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired Fill Color .
  4. Specify the **Elements by Condition** property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the Risk property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Specify **Adorned Properties** property by selecting the **Risk** property as its value.
  6. Repeat the steps from b. to e. with the rest of the Legend Items.
4. In the same table, create a legend intended to show the status of Requirements and name the Legend Status .
5. Create and define the Legend Items for the Status Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. Create four Legend Items indicating four possible Requirement status values and name them Closed , Approved , Disapproved and In Progress .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired Fill Color .
  4. Specify the Elements by Condition property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the Status property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Specify the Adorned Properties property by selecting the Name and Text properties as its values.
  6. Repeat the steps from b. to e. with the rest of the Legend Items.

After following the above steps, you have a Requirements Table with the*Status*and*Risk*Legends applied to it. The Legend Items of both Legends do not conflict because they are applied to different columns of the table, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The figure shows a Requirement Table with the *Risk* and *Status* Legends applied to it. 
The Legend Items of each Legend are applied to different columns of a table.

##### Selecting adorned symbols

You can select the adorned symbols in the open diagram directly from the shortcut menu or the smart manipulator toolbar of the selected Legend item.

To select adorned symbols in the diagram

Do one of the following:

1. 
  - Right-click the Legend item in the Legend symbol on the diagram pane and then click Select Adorned Symbols . [ATTACHMENT filename='select_adorned_symbols.png']
  - Select the Legend item in the Legend symbol on the diagram pane and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_adorned_symbols_button.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='select_adorned_symbols_from_smart_manipulator.png']

If the diagram is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Filtering by Legend items' space=''], the **Select Adorned Symbols** menu item is greyed out (inactive).

582727279

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Items' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Item adornment' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994454 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00095: (2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends

- page_id: `303994454`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994454/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+Legends
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:15.691+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Legends
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Keeping Legend adornments

580023290

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Keeping Legend adornments

580023292

580023289

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Keeping Legend adornments

580023291

You can manage the Legends by choosing to either apply, unapply, suspend, or disable them in the diagrams and/or tables of your project. You can also adorn a Legend in a diagram or table without displaying the Legend itself, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Requirement Table displaying only Legend adornments

##### Applying a Legend in an open diagram or table

To apply a Legend in an open diagram or table

Do one of the following:

- Create a Legend in a diagram or table.
- Drag an existing Legend from the model browser to a diagram.
- Display a Legend via the Legend [ATTACHMENT filename='Legends_button.png'] button:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] in the diagram or table toolbar.
  2. In the drop-down menu, select Apply Legends .
  3. Select the Legend(s) you want to display.
  4. Click the OK button.

Completing one of the actions described above displays the Legend in an open diagram or table, and applies its Legend Item adornments to the relevant diagram shapes or table rows.

#### NOTE: Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock

Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock

You can also temporarily suspend or activate a Legend via the **Suspended/Active** toggle button available next to the Legend name in the **Apply Legends**option. This functionality can be useful for demonstration purposes, working with read-only diagrams/tables, or if you want to preview a diagram/table with or without adornments of a Legend without making permanent changes. The changes made via the **Suspended/Active** toggle button are only temporary, i.e., they are not saved upon the saving of a project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Unapplying a Legend from an open diagram or table

To unapply a Legend from an open diagram or table

Do one of the following:

- Delete the Legend shape from a diagram.
- Hover the mouse pointer over a legend in a table, click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_edit_button.png'] and select Hide .
- Remove a Legend via the Legend [ATTACHMENT filename='Legends_button.png'] button:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] in the diagram or table toolbar.
  2. In the drop-down menu, select Apply Legends .
  3. Clear the check mark of the Legend(s) you want to remove.
  4. Click the OK button.

The actions described above remove a Legend from an open diagram or table, and Legend Item adornments are no longer applied to the relevant diagram shapes or table rows.

#### NOTE: Keeping Legend adornments

Keeping Legend adornments

When you delete a Legend shape in a diagram or select the **Hide** command for a Legend in a table, the question message appears, allowing you to keep Legend Item adornments after the Legend is removed.

##### Displaying an applied Legend in an open diagram or table

You can display an applied Legend that was previously removed from a diagram or table.

To display an applied Legend in an open diagram or table

1. In a table or diagram toolbar, click the Legend [ATTACHMENT filename='legend_button.png'] button.
2. In the drop-down menu, select Display Applied Legends .
3. Select the Legend(s) you want to display.
4. Click the Display button.

##### Disabling/enabling Legends

To disable/enable Legends

1. In the main toolbar, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the General options group, select Legends .
3. To disable/enable selected Legends:
  1. For the **Ignored Legends** option, click the three dots [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The **Select Legend** dialog opens.
  2. Click the **Multiple Selection** button and do one of the following:
    1. To disable Legends: on the left side of the dialog, select the Legend you want to disable, click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button and click **OK**.
    2. To enable Legends: on the right side of the dialog, select the Legend you want to enable, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_button_3.png'] button and click OK .
4. To disable/enable all Legends:
  1. Set the Use Legends option to true to enable or false to disable the Legends of the project. Click OK .
5. Click OK .

Once the Legend is disabled, even if it is displayed on the diagram, the Legend cannot be used to filter the diagram, and the diagram symbols are not colored by Legend adornments. Once all the Legends are disabled, even if they are displayed on the diagram, the Legends cannot be used to filter the diagram, and the diagram symbols are not colored by Legend adornments.

##### display_adornmentsApplying Legends by Condition

You can apply Legend adornments in a diagram or table without displaying the Legend. To achieve this, define the condition that diagrams should meet for the Legend to be applied (the **Diagrams by Condition** property). Legends with the specified **Diagrams by Condition** property are considered Global Legends, as the Legends' adornments are automatically applied to existing and newly created diagrams that meet the defined condition. Such Legends are marked in the **Apply Legends** and **Display Legends** menus with the *(global)* indication:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Example**

Let's say that you want to apply the adornments of a Legend to all Requirement Diagrams and one specific Requirement Table of your model. To do this, follow these steps:

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend.
2. Select the Diagrams by Condition property specification cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
3. In the Diagrams by Condition dialog, select the Condition radio button.
4. In the Property Test operation specification area, select the Type property and enter the Requirement Diagram as its value. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_diagrams_by_condition.png']
5. Click OK .
6. Select the Specific Diagrams property specification cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
7. In the Select Diagram dialog, select the desired Requirement Table.
8. Click OK .

After completing the above steps, the Legend adornments are applied to all Requirement Diagrams and the selected Requirement Table. By specifying the **Diagrams by Condition** property, you also ensure that the Legend adornments will be automatically applied to newly created Requirement Diagrams as well because they meet the defined condition.

580083820

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Legends' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends automatically' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating and managing Legend Items' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Items' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e018f8db-f711-4a2a-91fa-b3d332138916"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">580023290</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4aad01dc-3880-4863-94f1-4986ccc87f4e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">580023292</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5ce4407-fc22-4e37-8a5e-4c81f6a1542a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">580023289</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f51355a-0584-41d0-8896-7ad8ccc5658a"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe843ff0-97f3-4464-85a1-8dc5b69d79cc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">580023291</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can manage the Legends by choosing to either apply, unapply, suspend, or disable them in the diagrams and/or tables of your project. You can also adorn a Legend in a diagram or table without displaying the Legend itself, as shown below.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="displaying_adornment_without_legend.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">A Requirement Table displaying only Legend adornments</span></h6><h3>Applying a Legend in an open diagram or table</h3><p>To apply a Legend in an open diagram or table</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Create a Legend in a diagram or table.</li><li>Drag an existing Legend from the model browser to a diagram.</li><li>Display a Legend via the Legend <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Legends_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button:<ol><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="12"><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the diagram or table toolbar.</li><li>In the drop-down menu, select <strong>Apply Legends</strong>.</li><li>Select the Legend(s) you want to display.</li><li>Click the <strong>OK</strong> button.</li></ol></li></ul><p>Completing one of the actions described above displays the Legend in an open diagram or table, and applies its Legend Item adornments to the relevant diagram shapes or table rows.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2dae9ada-0e03-4f1d-a06f-2b9e9bcf3fef"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Manipulate Legends without Diagram Lock</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also temporarily suspend or activate a Legend via the <strong>Suspended/Active</strong> toggle button available next to the Legend name in the <strong>Apply Legends </strong>option. This functionality can be useful for demonstration purposes, working with read-only diagrams/tables, or if you want to preview a diagram/table with or without adornments of a Legend without making permanent changes. The changes made via the <strong>Suspended/Active</strong> toggle button are only temporary, i.e., they are not saved upon the saving of a project.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="active_suspended_legends.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Unapplying a Legend from an open diagram or table</h3><p>To unapply a Legend from an open diagram or table</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Delete the Legend shape from a diagram.</li><li>Hover the mouse pointer over a legend in a table, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="15"><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend_edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> and select <strong>Hide</strong>.</li><li>Remove a Legend via the Legend <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Legends_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button:<ol><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="12"><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the diagram or table toolbar.</li><li>In the drop-down menu, select <strong>Apply Legends</strong>.</li><li>Clear the check mark of the Legend(s) you want to remove.</li><li>Click the <strong>OK</strong> button.</li></ol></li></ul><p>The actions described above remove a Legend from an open diagram or table, and Legend Item adornments are no longer applied to the relevant diagram shapes or table rows.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="230aa9d1-ca9a-4ca2-9a0d-f965eb74de96"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Keeping Legend adornments</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you delete a Legend shape in a diagram or select the <strong>Hide</strong> command for a Legend in a table, the question message appears, allowing you to keep Legend Item adornments after the Legend is removed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Displaying an applied Legend in an open diagram or table</span></h3><p><span>You can display an applied Legend that was previously removed from a diagram or table.</span></p><p><span>To display an applied Legend in an open diagram or table</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In a table or diagram toolbar, click the Legend <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="12"><ri:attachment ri:filename="legend_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button.</li><li>In the drop-down menu, select <strong>Display Applied Legends</strong>.</li><li>Select the Legend(s) you want to display.</li><li>Click the <strong>Display</strong> button.</li></ol><h3>Disabling/enabling Legends</h3><p>To disable/enable Legends</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main toolbar, click <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> options group, select <strong>Legends</strong>.</li><li>To disable/enable selected Legends:<ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">For the <strong>Ignored Legends</strong> option, click the three dots <ac:image ac:alt="edit.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/26676999/edit.png?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1643385379535&amp;api=v2" /></ac:image> button. The <strong>Select Legend</strong> dialog opens.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button and do one of the following:</p><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To disable Legends: on the left side of the dialog, select the Legend you want to disable, click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button_3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">OK</strong>.</p></li><li>To enable Legends: on the right side of the dialog, select the Legend you want to enable, click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="23"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_button_3.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button and click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">OK</strong>.</li></ol></li></ol></li><li>To disable/enable all Legends:<ol><li>Set the <strong>Use Legends</strong> option to <em>true</em> to enable or <em>false </em>to disable the Legends of the project. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>Once the Legend is disabled, even if it is displayed on the diagram, the Legend cannot be used to filter the diagram, and the diagram symbols are not colored by Legend adornments. Once all the Legends are disabled, even if they are displayed on the diagram, the Legends cannot be used to filter the diagram, and the diagram symbols are not colored by Legend adornments.</p><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc75b0b5-766d-4749-9bbb-925046786a4f"><ac:parameter ac:name="">display_adornments</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Applying Legends by Condition</h3><p>You can apply Legend adornments in a diagram or table without displaying the Legend. To achieve this, define the condition that diagrams should meet for the Legend to be applied (the <strong>Diagrams by Condition</strong> property). Legends with the specified <strong>Diagrams by Condition</strong> property are considered Global Legends, as the Legends' adornments are automatically applied to existing and newly created diagrams that meet the defined condition. Such Legends are marked in the <strong>Apply Legends</strong> and <strong>Display Legends</strong> menus with the <em>(global)</em> indication:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="global_legends.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><strong><br />Example</strong></p><p>Let's say that you want to apply the adornments of a Legend to all Requirement Diagrams and one specific Requirement Table of your model. To do this, follow these steps:</p><ol><li>Open the Specification window of a Legend.</li><li>Select the <strong>Diagrams by Condition</strong> property specification cell and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the <strong>Diagrams by Condition</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Condition</strong> radio button.</li><li>In the Property Test operation specification area, select the <strong>Type</strong> property and enter the <strong>Requirement Diagram</strong> as its value.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_diagrams_by_condition.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Select the <strong>Specific Diagrams</strong> property specification cell and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legends" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Diagram</strong> dialog, select the desired Requirement Table.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps, the Legend adornments are applied to all Requirement Diagrams and the selected Requirement Table. By specifying the <strong>Diagrams by Condition</strong> property, you also ensure that the Legend adornments will be automatically applied to newly created Requirement Diagrams as well because they meet the defined condition. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a48faba-cae9-40f9-9ea6-2ea302ab9902"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">580083820</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Legends" /></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends automatically" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating and managing Legend Items" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Items" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000129 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00096: (2026x Refresh1) Applying military symbols

- page_id: `304000129`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000129/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+military+symbols
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:50.480+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Procedures
- labels: ['applying-military-symbols', 'using-military-symbols', 'mil-std-2525c-symbology', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Military symbols can be applied to resources in t.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

By default, military symbol libraries will be disabled for the UAF projects.

If you will need to use the military symbols:

- When creating a new project select the needed library version.
- In the Project Options dialog, General > UAF options group, change the Use MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library option value from None to the needed library version.

To apply military symbol for resource shape

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Select Symbol dialog' space='']**Select Symbol**dialog:
  - Create resources from MIL-STD-2525 Symbology toolbar (in MODAF only).
  - Select resource and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] on the smart manipulator toolbar (in MODAF only).
  - Open the Specification window of any UAF element, select Image property cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='military_symbols.png'] .
2. Select the military symbol you want to apply, specify affiliation and frame style. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click **OK**. 
The military symbol is applied for the resource shape.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Military symbols can be applied to resources in t.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-1_military.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying military symbols" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9bf41ba-ceda-42d3-b604-37c4050c745e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, military symbol libraries will be disabled for the UAF projects.</p><p>If you will need to use the military symbols:</p><ul><li><span>When creating a new project </span>select the needed library version.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>UAF</strong> options group, change the <strong>Use MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library</strong> option value from <strong>None</strong> to the needed library version.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To apply military symbol for resource shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Select Symbol dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Select Symbol </strong>dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>:<br /></span></span><ul><li>Create resources from MIL-STD-2525 Symbology toolbar (in MODAF only).</li><li>Select resource and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="military_symbols.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying military symbols" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> on the smart manipulator toolbar (in MODAF only).</li><li>Open the <strong>Specification window</strong> of any UAF element, select <strong>Image</strong> property cell and click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="21"><ri:attachment ri:filename="military_symbols.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying military symbols" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ul><span class="confluence-link"> </span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">Select the military symbol you want to apply, specify affiliation and frame style.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Select Symbol dialog" ac:alt="Select Symbol dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_symbol_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying military symbols" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The military symbol is applied for the resource shape.<br /></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000335 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00097: (2026x Refresh1) Applying OMG SysML Style

- page_id: `304000335`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000335/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+OMG+SysML+Style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:02.226+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can apply the visual style of the OMG SysML Specifications (OMG SysML style) to your SysML v1 model.

To apply the OMG SysML style to a SysML v1 project

1. In the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the **Project Options** dialog, choose the **Symbol styles** on the left and select the **OMG SysML style** on the right. If you do not see the **OMG SysML style**a. Click the **Import** button. The **Open** dialog opens.b. Open the ***<md.install.dir>/templates/SysML directory*** and select **OMG SysML style.stl**.c. Click **Open**.
3. Click the Make Default button.
4. Click OK . Your SysML project uses OMG SysML style as a default style.

To apply OMG SysML style to a SysML v1 diagram

1. In the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the **Project Options** dialog, choose the **Symbol styles** on the left and select the **OMG SysML style** on the right.
3. Click the **Apply** button.
4. In the **Select Diagrams** dialog, select a SysML diagram(s) and click **OK**.
5. Click **OK**in the **Project Options** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Applying OMG SysML style to existing SysML diagrams might distort the diagrams. Use the automated layout features that optimize diagram layout for viewing.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can apply the visual style of the OMG SysML Specifications (OMG SysML style) to your SysML v1 model.</p><p><br /></p><p>To apply the OMG SysML style to a SysML v1 project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li><p>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, choose the <strong>Symbol styles</strong> on the left and select the <strong>OMG SysML style</strong> on the right.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67f211d8-71d2-43b2-887d-ba5b1a4bae07"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not see the <strong>OMG SysML style</strong></p><hr /><p>a. Click the <strong>Import</strong> button. The <strong>Open</strong> dialog opens.</p><p>b. Open the <strong><em>&lt;md.install.dir&gt;/templates/SysML directory</em></strong> and select <strong>OMG SysML style.stl</strong>.</p><p>c. Click <strong>Open</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click the <strong>Make Default</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />Your SysML project uses OMG SysML style as a default style.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To apply OMG SysML style to a SysML v1 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li><p>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, choose the <strong>Symbol styles</strong> on the left and select the <strong>OMG SysML style</strong> on the right.</p></li><li><p>Click the <strong>Apply</strong> button.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Select Diagrams</strong> dialog, select a SysML diagram(s) and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK </strong>in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog.</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Project Options dialog" ac:alt="Project Options dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Project Options_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying OMG SysML Style" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e5ea953-dd75-4fc0-bf36-8b09cac3607e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Applying OMG SysML style to existing SysML diagrams might distort the diagrams. Use the automated layout features that optimize diagram layout for viewing.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991095 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00098: (2026x Refresh1) Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams

- page_id: `303991095`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991095/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+or+removing+aspect+for+preexisting+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:20.455+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Diagram aspects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

581426749

581426751

581426750

You can apply custom or predefined aspects for preexisting diagrams. It is possible to apply multiple aspects for one diagram. The aspects can be applied for all diagrams except [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activity diagram' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) State Machine diagram' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sequence diagram' space=''] diagrams. The procedure below describes how to apply/remove an aspect for a diagram.

To apply/remove an aspect for a diagram

- From the diagram shortcut menu, point to Aspect, and choose an appropriate aspect from the list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Applying an aspect for a diagram. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.

581426748

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **Diagram aspects** sample model:

- Download [ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].
- Find in the modeling tool: *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML v1\diagram aspects.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff80cc3a-6b61-4f77-828f-cac932104c23"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581426749</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2e2d61e-e6d7-44f9-b310-52ed8d346d92"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581426751</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c6f2657-bd26-4da7-b19b-883728954a15"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581426750</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can apply custom or predefined aspects for preexisting diagrams. It is possible to apply multiple aspects for one diagram. The aspects can be applied for all diagrams except <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Activity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) State Machine diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[State Machine]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams. The procedure below describes how to apply/remove an aspect for a diagram.</p><p>To apply/remove an aspect for a diagram</p><hr /><ul><li>From the diagram shortcut menu, point to <strong>Aspect,</strong> and choose an appropriate aspect from the list.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="aspect_command_group.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Applying an aspect for a diagram. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="743da1cc-30ee-4816-b7ce-4dd2eedad7d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">581426748</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Diagram aspects</strong> sample model:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Download <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram aspects.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>. </span></span></span></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link">Find in the modeling tool: <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">samples\SysML v1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">\<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">diagram aspects</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></span></span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /></span></span></span></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991516 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00099: (2026x Refresh1) Applying Rollup Pattern Blocks

- page_id: `303991516`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991516/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+Rollup+Pattern+Blocks
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:46.279+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Rollup Pattern Wizard
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When trying to calculate total mass, cost, power, or another system dimension based on individual values of all the parts in the model,you need to apply a pattern Block to any number of[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED']by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Rollup Pattern Wizard' space=''].It automatically applies pattern Blocks (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generalization' space='']) recursively, and creates property values.The procedure below describes how to apply the rollup pattern Block.

To apply the rollup pattern Block

1. Right-click the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] to which you want to apply the rollup pattern Block.
2. S elect **Tools** > **Apply Rollup Pattern**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='']**, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select the pattern Block you want to apply. Rollup pattern BlocksYou can select: 
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space='']: CostRollupPattern, MassRollupPattern, and PowerRollupPattern. They are created to calculate the total cost, mass, and power of the model elements. 
- Your own created rollup pattern Blocks. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating new Rollup Pattern Block' space='']]]>
4. Specify options: 
- **Apply Recursively**to apply the pattern Block recursively. 
- **Set Role Name** to set the role names of Part Properties. 
- **Set Subsetted Properties** to set the Subsetted Properties of the Part Properties. 
- **Create Value Properties and Redefine** to create and redefine Value Properties.
5. Click **OK**. 
The pattern Block is applied to the model elements according to selected options. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='']

**Webinar**

- Total Mass, Cost, and Power Rollups

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figures of this page is the **Laptop Cost Analysis** that comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='MMA']. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\simulation\LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999189 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00100: (2026x Refresh1) Applying validationRule stereotype

- page_id: `303999189`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999189/2026x+Refresh1+Applying+validationRule+stereotype
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:40.114+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Validation > (2026x Refresh1) Custom validation > (2026x Refresh1) Creating new validation rules
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

After the Constraint element is created, you must apply:

- «validationRule» stereotype. It is derived from the «invariant » stereotype. It expands the Constraint properties with the ability to specify the following information: severity level (for sorting/filtering), abbreviation string (a short string, for easy recognition) and error message (complete description of error explanation). [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying the validation rule properties' space='']>]]>

To apply a stereotype, do one of the following:

- In the corresponding element's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specification window' space=''] , click the Applied Stereotype property specification cell, then click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] , and select stereotypes from the open list. After you have selected, click Apply .
- In the element's shortcut menu, select Stereotype and select a stereotype that you wish to apply. Click Apply when you are done.
- On the diagram pane, select an element and in that element's name area, type the stereotype name between brackets « ». The element's name comes after the stereotype. If you want to name the element Books and assign it the «table» stereotype, in the element's name area type the following: «table» Books. The name completion for the stereotypes works in the name editing mode, press Ctrl+Spacebar or Ctrl+Backspace to get a list of available stereotypes.

You can change the order of the stereotypes applied to the element. The symbol style of the last stereotype in the list will be applied to the element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Click <strong>Apply</strong> when you are done.</li><li><p>On the diagram pane, select an element and in that element's name area, type the stereotype name between brackets « ». The element's name comes after the stereotype.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39a0c9ab-6b1a-4036-865c-135b11c0442c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to name the element Books and assign it the «table» stereotype, in the element's name area type the following: «table» Books. The name completion for the stereotypes works in the name editing mode, press Ctrl+Spacebar or Ctrl+Backspace to get a list of available stereotypes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p>You can change the order of the stereotypes applied to the element. The symbol style of the last stereotype in the list will be applied to the element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993969 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00101: (2026x Refresh1) Architectural views

- page_id: `303993969`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993969/2026x+Refresh1+Architectural+views
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:46.011+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) UML diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

UML defines 13 diagrams that describe 4+1 architectural views:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Several kinds of diagrams provide a visual notation for the concepts in each view.

##### Use Case view

The use case view represents the functionality and behavior of a system or subsystem as it is perceived by external users. This view is targeted mainly at customers, designers, developers, and testers.

The use case view usually is presented as a number of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MED'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MED'] in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Use Case diagram' space='']. Occasionally it is used in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activity diagram' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sequence diagram' space=''].

The use case view is central because the contents drive the development of the other views. It is also used for project planning. Every single use case unit is deemed as a manageable unit during the project execution.

##### Structural view

The structural view represents structural elements for implementing a solution for defined requirements. It identifies all of the business entities and how these entities are related to each other. Usually entities are represented as classifiers and their instances in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Object diagram' space=''] in multiple abstraction levels. System decomposition to different layers can be displayed using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Package diagram' space='']. A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram' space=''] can be used to represent the classifier inner structure. The system structural view artifacts are created by software architects and represent the system implementation design solutions.

##### Behavioral view

The dynamic behavior of the system is displayed on the Interaction ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sequence diagram' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Communication diagram' space='']), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) State Machine diagram' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Activity diagram' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Interaction Overview diagram' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Time diagram' space=''] diagrams. It focuses mainly on the interactions that occur between objects inside a system, activities and work performed by the various parts of a system, and state changes within a particular object or collaboration. Rather than defining the participants of the system, it defines how particular use cases are executed, which provides value for the external user. The dynamic view is concerned about what is happening inside the system and how those actions impact other participants.

##### Implementation view

The implementation view describes the implementation artifacts of logical subsystems defined in the structural view. It can include the intermediate artifacts used in a system construction (code files, libraries, data files, etc.) This view defines dependencies between the implementation components and their connections by the required and provided interfaces. Components and their relationships are displayed on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Component diagram' space='']. Inner parts of the component can be represented with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram' space='']. The implementation view helps analyze system parts and their dependencies in a higher component level.

##### Environment view

The environment view represents the physical arrangement of a system, such as computers and devices (nodes) and how they are connected to each other. In contrast to the component view, the deployment view is concerned with the physical structure of the system and the location of the software modules (components) manifested by artifacts within the system.

The environment view is displayed on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Deployment diagram' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>UML defines 13 diagrams that describe 4+1 <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="75fcd8db-4b0f-4bc2-97dc-c72c7c2eb7fc">architectural</ac:inline-comment-marker> views:</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Architectural views" ac:alt="Architectural views"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ArchitecturalViews.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Architectural views" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Several kinds of diagrams provide a visual notation for the concepts in each view.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Use Case view</h3><p>The use case view represents the functionality and behavior of a system or subsystem as it is perceived by external users. This view is targeted mainly at customers, designers, developers, and testers. </p><p>The use case view usually is presented as a number of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Use Case" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[use cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actor" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[actors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Use Case diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use Case diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Occasionally it is used in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Activity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p><p>The use case view is central because the contents drive the development of the other views. It is also used for project planning. Every single use case unit is deemed as a manageable unit during the project execution. </p><h3>Structural view</h3><p>The structural view represents structural elements for implementing a solution for defined requirements. It identifies all of the business entities and how these entities are related to each other. Usually entities are represented as classifiers and their instances in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Class diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Class]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Object diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Object diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in multiple abstraction levels. System decomposition to different layers can be displayed using <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Package diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Package diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can be used to represent the classifier inner structure. The system structural view artifacts are created by software architects and represent the system implementation design solutions.</p><h3>Behavioral view</h3><p>The dynamic behavior of the system is displayed on the Interaction (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sequence diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Sequence]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Communication diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Communication]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) State Machine diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[State]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Activity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Activity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Interaction Overview diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interaction Overview]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Time diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Time]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagrams. It focuses mainly on the interactions that occur between objects inside a system, activities and work performed by the various parts of a system, and state changes within a particular object or collaboration. Rather than defining the participants of the system, it defines how particular use cases are executed, which provides value for the external user. The dynamic view is concerned about what is happening inside the system and how those actions impact other participants.</p><h3>Implementation view</h3><p>The implementation view describes the implementation artifacts of logical subsystems defined in the structural view. It can include the intermediate artifacts used in a system construction (code files, libraries, data files, etc.) This view defines dependencies between the implementation components and their connections by the required and provided interfaces. Components and their relationships are displayed on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Component diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Component diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Inner parts of the component can be represented with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Composite Structure diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The implementation view helps analyze system parts and their dependencies in a higher component level.</p><h3>Environment view</h3><p>The environment view represents the physical arrangement of a system, such as computers and devices (nodes) and how they are connected to each other. In contrast to the component view, the deployment view is concerned with the physical structure of the system and the location of the software modules (components) manifested by artifacts within the system. </p><p>The environment view is displayed on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Deployment diagram" /></ac:link>.</p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991065 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00102: (2026x Refresh1) Architecture Management

- page_id: `303991065`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991065/2026x+Refresh1+Architecture+Management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:18.599+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The **Architecture Management** (Am) domain captures meta-data relevant to the entire architecture. It provides information pertinent to the entire architecture and presents supporting information rather than architectural models.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The <strong>Architecture Management</strong> (Am) domain captures meta-data relevant to the entire architecture. It provides information pertinent to the entire architecture and presents supporting information rather than architectural models.</p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

281843976

281843987

281843977

**Description**

The **Architecture Management Connectivity** (Am-Cn) view depicts and analyzes all relevant dependencies between architectural descriptions, e.g., reference architectures, as-is to to-be architectures, enterprise architecture to system architectures.

**Implementation**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Working with Architecture Management diagram' space=''].

281843975

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303994526 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00105: (2026x Refresh1) Architecture Management Information

- page_id: `303994526`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303994526/2026x+Refresh1+Architecture+Management+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:24:19.503+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 > (2026x Refresh1) Architecture Management
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

832800706

832800708

832800707

**Description**

The **Architecture Management Information**(Am-If) view contains definitions of terms used in the given architecture. It consists of textual definitions in the form of a glossary, their taxonomies, and their metadata (i.e., data about architecture data), including metadata for any custom-tailored views. Architects should use standard terms where possible (i.e., terms from existing, approved dictionaries, glossaries, and lexicons).

**Implementation**

An Architecture Management Information****(Am-If) viewpoint is represented by:

- Dictionary table . It contains the definitions of terms used in the given architecture. The table also consists of textual definitions in the form of a glossary, their taxonomies, and their metadata (i.e., data about architecture data), including metadata for any custom-tailored views. Architects should use standard terms where possible (i.e., terms from existing, approved dictionaries, glossaries, and lexicons). This table provides a central reference for a given architecture’s data and metadata. It enables the set of architecture descriptions to stand alone, with minimal reference to outside resources.
- Reports : - Dictionary (.docx). - Dictionary Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx). - Dictionary Tabular (.docx).

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

832800704

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Definition' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alias' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Same As' space='MED']

It consists of textual definitions in the form of a glossary, their taxonomies, and their metadata (i.e., data about architecture data), including metadata for any custom-tailored views. Architects should use standard terms where possible (i.e., terms from existing, approved dictionaries, glossaries, and lexicons).</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>An Architecture Management Information<strong> </strong>(Am-If) viewpoint is represented by:</p><ul><li><strong>Dictionary table</strong>. It contains the definitions of terms used in the given architecture. The table also consists of textual definitions in the form of a glossary, their taxonomies, and their metadata (i.e., data about architecture data), including metadata for any custom-tailored views. Architects should use standard terms where possible (i.e., terms from existing, approved dictionaries, glossaries, and lexicons). This table provides a central reference for a given architecture’s data and metadata. It enables the set of architecture descriptions to stand alone, with minimal reference to outside resources.</li><li><strong>Reports</strong>:<br />- Dictionary (.docx).<br />- Dictionary Excel Spreadsheet (.xlsx).<br />- Dictionary Tabular (.docx).</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:title="Dictionary" ac:alt="Dictionary"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dictionary.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Architecture Management Information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70a992ae-d97a-449a-bf46-820311b74ab9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">832800704</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Definition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Alias" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Same As" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991098 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00114: (2026x Refresh1) Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint

- page_id: `303991098`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991098/2026x+Refresh1+Architecture+Meta-Data+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:20.670+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0
- labels: ['architecture-meta-data-viewpoint', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

831912465

831912467

831912466

The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint provides information pertinent to the entire architecture but do not represent a distinct view of the architecture. The Architecture Meta-Data viewpoint subviews set the scope and context of the architecture. The scope includes the subject area and timeframe for the architecture. The setting in which the architecture exists comprises the interrelated conditions that compose the context for the architecture. These conditions include doctrine, tactics, techniques and procedures, relevant goals and vision statements, concepts of operations, scenarios, and environmental conditions.

831912464

**Views**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998415 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00115: (2026x Refresh1) Architecture templates

- page_id: `303998415`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998415/2026x+Refresh1+Architecture+templates
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:39.446+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are five architecture report templates that come with the report engine:

#### Behavioral Report

A Behavioral report is a standard report for the Behavioral view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of Sequence, Communication, State Machine, and Activity diagrams. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package consists of the specification of messages in the Sequence diagram, the specification of lifelines in the Communication diagram, the specification of states in the State Machine diagram, and the actions in the Activity diagram. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.

#### Environment Report

An Environment report is a standard report for the Environment view. The report will be generated by a selected package that consists of an Implementation diagram (Deployment diagram). The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of nodes, interfaces, components, and artifacts which are ordered by the element’s name.

#### Implementation Report

An Implementation report is a standard report for the Implementation view. The report will be generated by a selected package that consists of an Implementation diagram (Component diagram). The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package consists of a list of interfaces, components, and artifacts. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.

#### Structural Report

A Structural report is a standard report for the Structural view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of a Class diagram. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of interfaces, classes, and enumerations which are ordered by the element’s name.

#### Use Case Report

A Use Case report is a standard report for the Use Case view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of Use Case diagrams. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of actors and use case. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are five architecture report templates that come with the report engine:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9e3af78-c96a-412c-9208-449c0d7e292d" /></p><h2>Behavioral Report</h2><p>A Behavioral report is a standard report for the Behavioral view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of Sequence, Communication, State Machine, and Activity diagrams. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package consists of the specification of messages in the Sequence diagram, the specification of lifelines in the Communication diagram, the specification of states in the State Machine diagram, and the actions in the Activity diagram. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.</p><h2>Environment Report</h2><p>An Environment report is a standard report for the Environment view. The report will be generated by a selected package that consists of an Implementation diagram (Deployment diagram). The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of nodes, interfaces, components, and artifacts which are ordered by the element’s name.</p><h2>Implementation Report</h2><p>An Implementation report is a standard report for the Implementation view. The report will be generated by a selected package that consists of an Implementation diagram (Component diagram). The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package consists of a list of interfaces, components, and artifacts. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.</p><h2>Structural Report</h2><p>A Structural report is a standard report for the Structural view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of a Class diagram. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of interfaces, classes, and enumerations which are ordered by the element’s name.</p><h2>Use Case Report</h2><p>A Use Case report is a standard report for the Use Case view. This report will be generated by a selected package that consists of Use Case diagrams. The report lists all packages in a project and arranges them hierarchically. Each package describes the specification of actors and use case. All elements in the report are ordered by their names.</p>
````

## PAGE 00116: (2026x Refresh1) Array tool

- page_id: `303998291`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998291/2026x+Refresh1+Array+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:21.843+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050323630

2050323641

2050323628

**On this page**

43

2050323631

Use $array to create an Array or a HashSet instance.

###### **$array.createArray()**

Create an empty ArrayList.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.util.List | An instance of ArrayList. |

****

###### **$array.createArray(collection)**

Construct an ArrayList containing elements of the specified collection, in the order they are returned by the collection's iterator. Return a zero size ArrayList if the given collection is null.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | collection | java.util.Collection | A collection of instances whose elements are to be placed into a list. |
| Return | - | java.util.List | An ArrayList containing elements of the specified collection. |

****

###### **$array.subList(list, size)**

Create an ArrayList of a portion of the list in the given size.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | list | java.util.List | An original list. |
|  | size | int | A view size of a given list. |
| Return | - | java.util.List | An ArrayList of a specified view size within a given list. |

****

###### **$array.addCollection(parent, child)**

Add a child list into a parent collection. It helps the template handle the case where the child is null.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | parent | java.util.Collection | A parent collection. |
|  | child | java.util.Collection | A child collection. |
| Return | - | void |  |

****

###### **$array.createHashSet()**

Create a HashSet instance.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.util.Set | An instance of HashSet. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Artifacts provide modelers with the capability to show additional information about a process.

This information is not directly related to a sequence or message flow of the process.

Types of Artifacts are as follows:

- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:59.986+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) Business Motivation Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Assessment Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1986176031

1986176034

1986176033

**Description**

An Assessment is a judgment of some Influencer that affects the ability of organization to employ its Means or achieve its Ends. In other words, an Assessment expresses a logical connection or fact type between Influencers and the Ends and/or Means of the business plans. In this way, an Assessment indicates which Influencers are relevant to which Ends and/or Means.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1986176029

**Related elements**

- Assessment Concepts
- Assessment Concept Relationships

**Related diagram**

- Business Motivation Diagram

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd69a8ca-6700-4d37-bbb9-ec44bde3fda4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1986176031</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecf05752-f415-44cf-a7ea-076c69488801"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1986176034</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e408bb4-28d4-4d4a-822f-274a72367f41"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1986176033</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>An Assessment is a judgment of some Influencer that affects the ability of organization to employ its Means or achieve its Ends. In other words, an Assessment expresses a logical connection or fact type between Influencers and the Ends and/or Means of the business plans. In this way, an Assessment indicates which Influencers are relevant to which Ends and/or Means.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assesment.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Assessment" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d8dde51-c042-4a2e-8c00-d3ed72db966b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1986176029</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Assessment+Concepts">Assessment Concepts</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Assessment+Concept+Relationships">Assessment Concept Relationships</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Business+Motivation+Diagram">Business Motivation Diagram</a></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991387 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00119: (2026x Refresh1) Assessment Concept Relationships

- page_id: `303991387`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991387/2026x+Refresh1+Assessment+Concept+Relationships
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:59.584+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) Business Motivation Diagram > (2026x Refresh1) Assessment Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1986268114

1986268116

1986268115

**Description**

The following table provides the relationships that exist between element types and their meaning.

| Relationship | Description | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Identifies | This link connects to a or . Meaning that the Assessment points out some possible Potential Reward or Risk that is/are significant to that Assessment. Each possible consequence serves as an appraisal of the worth, value, or quality of some aspect of the Assessment in specific terms, types, or dimensions. |  |
| ProvidesImpetus | This link connects to a or . Meaning that the Assessment is the driving force for the Business Policy or Business Rule. |  |
| AffectsAchievements | This link connects to a , , or . Meaning that the Assessment has an impact on the End outcomes. |  |
| AffectsEmployment | This link connects to a , , , or . Meaning that the Assessment has an impact on the use of Means. |  |
| JudgementFor | This link connects to a Influencer ( or ). Meaning that the Assessment is the Influencer’s judgment that influences the organization’s ability to carry out its Means or achieve its Ends. |  |

1986268113

**Related element**

- Assessment Concepts

**Related diagram**

- Business Motivation Diagram
