- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Assessment element is the Influencer's assessment of the organization ends and means including the activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities.

There are three types of Assessment elements:

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993756 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00121: (2026x Refresh1) Assigning a keyboard shortcut to a Macro

- page_id: `303993756`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993756/2026x+Refresh1+Assigning+a+keyboard+shortcut+to+a+Macro
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:23:30.445+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Macro engine > (2026x Refresh1) Macro keyboard shortcuts
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To add a new keyboard shortcut to a macro

1. Open either the (i) Environment Options or (ii) Macro Information dialog.
2. Type a keyboard shortcut in the [ATTACHMENT filename='press-new-shortcut-key.png'] box.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to assign the keyboard shortcut to a macro.
4. Click OK .

If a keyboard shortcut key entered in the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] box has already been assigned to another macro, the following message will appear under the **Press new shortcut key**box: **Currently assigned to <the command name>**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To add a new keyboard shortcut to a macro</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open either the (i) <strong>Environment Options</strong> or (ii) <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog.</li><li>Type a keyboard shortcut in the<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="press-new-shortcut-key.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Assigning a keyboard shortcut to a Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> box.</li><li>Click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assign-btn.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Assigning a keyboard shortcut to a Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong> to assign the keyboard shortcut to a macro.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8dec5360-bd93-40ea-87e2-08069e3accb0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If a keyboard shortcut key entered in the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="press-new-shortcut-key.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Assigning a keyboard shortcut to a Macro" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> box has already been assigned to another macro, the following message will appear under the <strong>Press new shortcut key </strong>box: <strong>Currently assigned to &lt;the command name&gt;</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995310 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00123: (2026x Refresh1) Assigning resources in NSV-8 chart

- page_id: `303995310`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995310/2026x+Refresh1+Assigning+resources+in+NSV-8+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:26.421+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NSV-8 System Evolution Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before assigning resources, make sure they have Increment and Out of Service milestones specified. If the resource does not have milestones specified, you will not be able to see it in the NSV-8 chart.

To assign a resource

1. Right-click the Version of Configuration and from the shortcut menu, select Resources . The Select Resources dialog opens.
2. Select the desired resource and click OK .

- You can assign a resource in a simple way. Just select a resource in the Containment tree and drag it to the Version of Configuration.
- Double-click the resource bar to see internal diagram of the resource.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before assigning resources, make sure they have Increment and Out of Service milestones specified. If the resource does not have milestones specified, you will not be able to see it in the NSV-8 chart.</p><p><br /></p><p>To assign a resource</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Version of Configuration and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Resources</strong>. The <strong>Select Resources</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the desired resource and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b87682b7-f108-4928-a253-2b77f879af40"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can assign a resource in a simple way. Just select a resource in the Containment tree and drag it to the Version of Configuration.</li><li>Double-click the resource bar to see internal diagram of the resource.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000586 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00124: (2026x Refresh1) Assigning shortcut keys

- page_id: `304000586`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000586/2026x+Refresh1+Assigning+shortcut+keys
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:26.572+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Restoring shortcut keys

1192686262

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Restoring shortcut keys

1192686264

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Restoring shortcut keys

1192686263

#### EXCERPT: Restoring shortcut keys

INLINE

Most commonly used commands in a modeling tool have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Most common shortcut keys' space=''], allowing you to access the commands quicker. However, you can assign custom shortcut keys to modeling tool commands and change or remove already existing shortcut keys.

To assign a new shortcut key to a command

1. From the main menu of a modeling tool, select Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. From the option group list on the left side of the dialog, select Keyboard .
3. Search for the command you need to assign a shortcut key to. You can also search by the shortcut keys.
4. In the Press new shortcut key box, press the shortcut you want to assign. Do not type the shortcut, but press the keys as you would when executing the command.
5. Click the Assign button. The assigned shortcut appears in the Current keys box.
6. Click OK .

The shortcut key is then assigned to the selected command and you can start using it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Assigning a shortcut key.

#### TIP: Usage information

Usage information

- You can assign multiple shortcut keys to one command.
- If you enter a shortcut key that is already assigned to another command, a notification appears under the Press new shortcut key box.
- If you assign a shortcut key that is already assigned to another command, the shortcut key is automatically unassigned from that command and assigned to the new one.

##### Removing shortcut keys

You can remove both default and custom shortcut keys.

To remove a shortcut key

1. From the main menu of the modeling tool, select Options > Environment . The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. From the option group list on the left side of the dialog, select Keyboard .
3. Search for the command you need to assign a shortcut key to. You can also search by the shortcut keys.
4. In the Current keys box, select the shortcut key you want to remove.
5. Click the Remove button. To remove all shortcut keys assigned to the selected command, click the Remove All button.
6. Click OK .

#### NOTE: Restoring shortcut keys

Restoring shortcut keys

To restore all shortcut keys to their default values, open the **Environment Options** dialog, select **Keyboard**, and click **Reset to Defaults**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="39e42f79-fce5-4994-bd45-35b4557aa30f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1192686262</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2175f79-eaa6-4f40-be74-bfa5df01eb5d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1192686264</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dea4738b-2a67-47bb-8a2f-a2a101880d6f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1192686263</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49f2a791-7fa0-4587-88b5-dc7e175149e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Most commonly used commands in a modeling tool have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Most common shortcut keys" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[shortcut keys]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, allowing you to access the commands quicker. However, you can assign custom shortcut keys to modeling tool commands and change or remove already existing shortcut keys.</p><p><br /></p><p>To assign a new shortcut key to a command</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu of a modeling tool, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. Do not type the shortcut, but press the keys as you would when executing the command.</li><li>Click the <strong>Assign</strong> button. The assigned shortcut appears in the <strong>Current keys</strong> box.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>The shortcut key is then assigned to the selected command and you can start using it. </p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assigning_shortcut_keys.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Assigning shortcut keys" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Assigning a shortcut key.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1cadcd7-a909-4463-a26a-780bf59b3de8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Usage information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can assign multiple shortcut keys to one command.</li><li>If you enter a shortcut key that is already assigned to another command, a notification appears under the <strong>Press new shortcut key</strong> box.</li><li>If you assign a shortcut key that is already assigned to another command, the shortcut key is automatically unassigned from that command and assigned to the new one.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target"><br />Removing shortcut keys</h3><p>You can remove both default and custom shortcut keys.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove a shortcut key</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu of the modeling tool, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996552 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 00126: (2026x Refresh1) Assigning value specification as property value

- page_id: `303996552`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996552/2026x+Refresh1+Assigning+value+specification+as+property+value
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:09.894+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Editing property values > (2026x Refresh1) Cases of advanced editing
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are some properties whose values can be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MED'].

Examples of properties whose values are value specifications: *Default Value*, *Value*.

To assign a value specification

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Show Shortcut Menu button (see the highlighted button in the following figure).
3. From the shortcut menu, select **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MED']**.
4. Select a value specification from the list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change an assigned value specification

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Show Shortcut Menu button (see the highlighted button in the preceding figure).
3. From the shortcut menu, select **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MED']**> **Delete**<value specification>.
4. Assign a new value specification.

## PAGE 00127: (2026x Refresh1) Association

- page_id: `303998375`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998375/2026x+Refresh1+Association
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:42.578+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) Common BPMN Elements > (2026x Refresh1) Artifacts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

148680820

148680821

148680821

**Description**

An Association link shows that particular classes are related to each other. Association links may have names defined – names help to read diagrams. You can also specify multiplicity at the end of an association.

**Example**

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

148680819

**Related element**

- Class

**Related diagram**

- Business Data Diagram

****

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992016 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00128: (2026x Refresh1) Attaching, reattaching, and detaching files

- page_id: `303992016`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992016/2026x+Refresh1+Attaching+reattaching+and+detaching+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:24.573+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Projects with file attachments
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

304082935

304082937

304093561

**On this page**

304082936

When a file is attached to a project, such file becomes a model element called Attached File.

Attached Files are displayed in the Model Browser. 
 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Attaching files

You can attach files in one of the following ways:

44

###### Dragging the file icon to the diagram pane

To attach a file by dragging the file icon to the diagram pane

1. In the file system, select the icon of the file you need to attach.
2. Drag it to the diagram pane.

The shape of the newly created Attached File element appears on the diagram pane. The diagram owner becomes the owner of the file as well.

###### Dragging the file icon to the Containment tree

To attach a file by dragging the file icon to the Containment tree

1. Drag the file icon onto an element in the Containment tree .
2. In the shortcut menu, select **Create****Attached File**.

A new Attached File element appears in the Containment tree. The element on which you dragged the file icon becomes the owner of the file.

###### Using a command from the shortcut menu of the element selected in the Containment tree

To attach a file by using a command from the shortcut menu of an element

1. In the Containment tree, right-click a possible owner for the file to be attached to.
2. In the shortcut menu, s elect Create Element > Attached File .
3. In the Select File to Attach dialog, select the file and click Attach .

A new Attached File element with its name in the edit mode appears in the Containment tree. If you do not need to change the file name, press Enter. Otherwise, type a new name and then press Enter.

Regardless of the way used to create an Attached File element, it has the same default properties, such as **Attached****At**, **Modified****At**, **Size**, **Author**, and **File**. You can see these properties, their descriptions, and values in the **Specification** window of the Attached File element. If you need to replace the attached file, you can do this using the **File** property value editor.

##### Reattaching files

To reattach the file

1. In the Containment tree, select the attached file you need to replace.
2. Press Enter to open its **Specification** window.
3. In the**Quick Filter** box, type *file*.
4. Click the cell of the **File** property value, then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
5. In the **Select File to Attach** dialog, select the new file to attach, and click **Attach**.
6. In the **Specification** window, click **Close**.

The file is reattached, and the **Modified At** property value of the appropriate Attached File element is updated. 
 
If you no longer need the attached file, you can simply remove it from the model just like any other element. Select it in the Containment tree and press Delete. If you have its shape on the diagram, select the shape and press Ctrl+D.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999433 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00129: (2026x Refresh1) Attribute

- page_id: `303999433`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999433/2026x+Refresh1+Attribute
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:56.070+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

- XML schema attribute maps to UML Attribute with stereotype XSDattribute.
- default maps to initial UML Attribute or AssociationEnd value.
- annotation – to UML Attribute or AssociationEnd documentation.
- name – to UML Attribute or AssociationEnd name.
- type or content simpleType – to UML Attribute or AssociationEnd type.

#### PANEL: Other attributes or elements maps to corresponding tagged values

Other attributes or elements maps to corresponding tagged values

```text
<attribute
```

```text
default = string
```

```text
fixed = string
```

```text
form = (qualified | unqualified)
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
name = NCName
```

```text
ref = QName
```

```text
type = QName
```

```text
use = (optional | prohibited | required ) optional
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace ...} >
```

```text
Content: (annotation?,(simpleType?))</attribute>
```

Example

```text
]]>
```

Ref value is generated from ref or refString TaggedValue. One of ref or name must be present, but not both.

If ref is present, then all of <simpleType>, form and type must be absent. Type and <simpleType> must not both be present.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Example of attribute UML model (1).

<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "[http://nomagic.com](http://nomagic.com)" xmlns:xs = ["http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema) targetNamespace =["http://nomagic.com"](http://nomagic.com/)

#### PANEL: Example of attribute UML code

Example of attribute UML code

```text
<xs:attribute name = "name" type = "xs:string" default = "minde"
```

```text
fixed = "fixed_value" form = "qualified" use "optional" >
```

```text
<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >name attribute documentation</xs:documentation>
```

```text
</xs:annotation>
```

```text
</xs:attribute>
```

```text
<xs:attribute name = "address" fixed = "fixed_value" form =
```

```text
"qualified" use = "optional" >  		<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >surname attribute
```

```text
documentation‹/xs:documentation>		</xs:annotation> 		<xs:simpleType >
```

```text
<xs:restriction base = "xs:string" />
```

```text
‹/xs:simpleType> 	</xs:attribute>
```

```text
<xs:attribute name = "surname" type    "xs:string" />
```

```text
<xs:attributeGroup name = "attr_group" >
```

```text
<xs:attribute ref    "nm:name" >
```

```text
<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >reference
```

```text
documentation</xs:documentation>
```

```text
</xs:annotation>
```

```text
</xs:attribute>
```

```text
<xs:attribute ref = "nm:surname" />
```

```text
</xs:attributeGroup> </xs:schema>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ul><li>XML schema attribute maps to UML Attribute with stereotype XSDattribute.</li><li>default maps to initial UML Attribute or AssociationEnd value.</li><li>annotation – to UML Attribute or AssociationEnd documentation.</li><li>name – to UML Attribute or AssociationEnd name.</li><li>type or content simpleType – to UML Attribute or AssociationEnd type.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd9dfa8d-0f4f-4757-9b3c-076e88c23b62"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Other attributes or elements maps to corresponding tagged values</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;attribute </pre><pre>	default = string  </pre><pre>	fixed = string  </pre><pre>	form = (qualified | unqualified)</pre><pre>	id = ID </pre><pre>	name = NCName</pre><pre>	ref = QName</pre><pre>	type = QName  </pre><pre>	use = (optional | prohibited | required ) optional </pre><pre>	{any attributes with non-schema namespace ...} &gt;</pre><pre>	Content: (annotation?,(simpleType?))<br />&lt;/attribute&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Example</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a5dcb2fb-e366-44d9-b53b-867fb83784ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<xs:attribute name="age" type="xs:positiveInteger" use="required"/>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Ref value is generated from ref or refString TaggedValue. fixed = &quot;fixed_value&quot; form =</pre><pre>&quot;qualified&quot; use = &quot;optional&quot; &gt; <br /> 		&lt;xs:annotation &gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:documentation &gt;surname attribute</pre><pre>documentation‹/xs:documentation&gt;<br />		&lt;/xs:annotation&gt; <br />		&lt;xs:simpleType &gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:restriction base = &quot;xs:string&quot; /&gt;</pre><pre>		‹/xs:simpleType&gt; <br />	&lt;/xs:attribute&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:attribute name = &quot;surname&quot; type    &quot;xs:string&quot; /&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:attributeGroup name = &quot;attr_group&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:attribute ref    &quot;nm:name&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:annotation &gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;xs:documentation &gt;reference</pre><pre>documentation&lt;/xs:documentation&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;/xs:annotation&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;/xs:attribute&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:attribute ref = &quot;nm:surname&quot; /&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/xs:attributeGroup&gt; <br />&lt;/xs:schema&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999468 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00130: (2026x Refresh1) AttributeGroup

- page_id: `303999468`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999468/2026x+Refresh1+AttributeGroup
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:30:59.243+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) XML schemas > (2026x Refresh1) XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Attribute Group maps to a simple UML Class with the stereotype XSDattributeGroup.

- name – to UML Class name
- annotation – to UML Class documentation
- attribute – to inner UML Attribute or AssociationEnd with XSDattribute
- stereotype.
- attributeGroup - the inner attributeGroup must only be reference. This reference maps to Attribute or AssociationEnd with the type of referenced attributeGroup. The opposite Association End kind must be aggregated and it must be navigable.
- anyAttribute – to inner UML Attribute with stereotype XSDanyAttribute .

If the reference is generated, a name is not generated.

When an **<attributeGroup>**appears as a daughter of **<schema> or <redefine>,**it corresponds to an attribute group definition, shown below. When it appears as a daughter of **<complexType> or <attributeGroup>,**it does not correspond to any component as such.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### attributeGroup UML model example.

#### PANEL: attributeGroup UML model example with associated XML

attributeGroup UML model example with associated XML

```text
<xs:schema xmlns:nm = "http://nomagic.com" xmlns:xs = "http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" targetNamespace = "http://nomagic.com"
```

```text
<xs:attributeGroup name = "global_attr_group">
```

```text
<xs:attribute name = "address" type = "xs:string" />	</xs:attributeGroup>
```

```text
<xs:attributeGroup name = "attr_group_name" >
```

```text
<xs:annotation>
```

```text
<xs:documentation>attribute group documentation</xs:documentation>		</xs:annotation>
```

```text
<xs:attribute name = "surname" type = "xs:string"/>
```

```text
<xs:attribute name = "name" type = "xs:string" >
```

```text
<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >name attribute documentation</xs:documentation>
```

```text
</xs:annotation>		</xs:attribute>
```

```text
<xs:attributeGroup ref = "nm:global_attr_group2" >
```

```text
<xs:annotation >
```

```text
<xs:documentation >reference documentation</xs:documentation>
```

```text
</xs:annotation>		</xs:attributeGroup>		<xs:anyAttribute />
```

```text
</xs:attributeGroup>
```

```text
<xs:attributeGroup name = "global_attr_group2" >		<xs:attribute name = "city" type = "xs:string"/>	</xs:attributeGroup>
```

```text
</xs:schema>
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Attribute Group maps to a simple UML Class with the stereotype XSDattributeGroup.</p><ul><li>name – to UML Class name</li><li>annotation – to UML Class documentation</li><li>attribute – to inner UML Attribute or AssociationEnd with XSDattribute</li><li>stereotype.</li><li>attributeGroup - the inner attributeGroup must only be reference. This reference maps to Attribute or AssociationEnd with the type of referenced attributeGroup. The opposite Association End kind must be aggregated and it must be navigable.</li><li>anyAttribute – to inner UML Attribute with stereotype <em>XSDanyAttribute</em>.</li></ul><p>If the reference is generated, a name is not generated.</p><p>When an <strong><u>&lt;attributeGroup&gt;</u> </strong>appears as a daughter of <strong> <u>&lt;schema&gt;</u> or <u>&lt;redefine&gt;,</u> </strong>it corresponds to an attribute group definition, shown below. When it appears as a daughter of <strong><u>&lt;complexType&gt;</u>  or <u>&lt;attributeGroup&gt;,</u> </strong>it does not correspond to any component  as such.</p><h6><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 61 attributeGroup UML Model Example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) AttributeGroup" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">attributeGroup UML model example.</h6><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b1f1691-7cda-4088-acd4-5f288d2da77f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">attributeGroup UML model example with associated XML</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;xs:schema xmlns:nm = &quot;<a href="http://nomagic.comn">http://nomagic.com&quot;</a> xmlns:xs = &quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLScheman">http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema&quot;</a> targetNamespace = &quot;<a href="http://nomagic.com">http://nomagic.com</a>&quot;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:attributeGroup name = &quot;global_attr_group&quot;&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:attribute name = &quot;address&quot; type = &quot;xs:string&quot; /&gt;<br />	&lt;/xs:attributeGroup&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:attributeGroup name = &quot;attr_group_name&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:annotation&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:documentation&gt;attribute group documentation&lt;/xs:documentation&gt;<br />		&lt;/xs:annotation&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:attribute name = &quot;surname&quot; type = &quot;xs:string&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:attribute name = &quot;name&quot; type = &quot;xs:string&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:annotation &gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;xs:documentation &gt;name attribute documentation&lt;/xs:documentation&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;/xs:annotation&gt;<br />		&lt;/xs:attribute&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:attributeGroup ref = &quot;nm:global_attr_group2&quot; &gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:annotation &gt;</pre><pre>				&lt;xs:documentation &gt;reference documentation&lt;/xs:documentation&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;/xs:annotation&gt;<br />		&lt;/xs:attributeGroup&gt;<br />		&lt;xs:anyAttribute /&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/xs:attributeGroup&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:attributeGroup name = &quot;global_attr_group2&quot; &gt;<br />		&lt;xs:attribute name = &quot;city&quot; type = &quot;xs:string&quot;/&gt;<br />	&lt;/xs:attributeGroup&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;/xs:schema&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000441 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00131: (2026x Refresh1) Authentication with 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `304000441`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000441/2026x+Refresh1+Authentication+with+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:35.073+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

5

The **3D**EXPERIENCE menu groups all available integrations with the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform. After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installation, licensing, and system requirements' space='IL'] supporting the integration with the platform, you can access the related functionalities in the **3D**EXPERIENCE menu.

For some functionalities to work, you need to log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform as described below. Note that you can log in to the platform deployed either on-premises or on the cloud server.

You can manage all secure connections, including self-signed certificates, using the [Server Certificates](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Server+Certificates) tool.

To log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform from the modeling tool

1. In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > Login .
2. In the Server box of the open dialog, enter the server address. If the 3D EXPERIENCE platform is deployed on the cloud, select the The 3DEXPERIENCE platform is hosted on the cloud check box. [ATTACHMENT filename='login_dialog.png']
3. Click OK .
4. In the respective boxes of the open dialog, enter your 3D EXPERIENCE username and password. [ATTACHMENT filename='log_in_to_platform.png']
5. Click Log in .

Once you log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you can use all the functionalities of the installed plugins without canceling the session.

3D

##### Switching credentials

Once you log in to the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you can switch to a different platform, the platform's collaborative space, or a different access role without terminating your session.

To switch to different **3D**EXPERIENCE platform credentials

1. In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > Switch Credentials .
2. In the **Credentials**drop-down list box of the open dialog, select the credentials you want to switch to and click**OK**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Credentials** drop-down list box displays only the platforms, collaborative spaces, and roles that you have access to. They are displayed in the following format: Platform • Collaborative Space• Access Role.

##### Logging out

When you finish working on a project in the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform, you can terminate your session by logging out of the platform.

To log out of the **3D**EXPERIENCE platform

- In the main menu, select 3DEXPERIENCE > Logout .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990567 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00132: (2026x Refresh1) Auto importing template tool

- page_id: `303990567`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990567/2026x+Refresh1+Auto+importing+template+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:48.224+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Appendix A: report extensions
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard can automatically import a Template Tool to a template without having to declare the #import statement in a template code manually (see *[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Importing custom tool to template' space='']*).

To enable Report Wizard to automatically import a Template Tool, you have to use the existing template variable inside “*template.xml*” and “*report.xml*”.

The current template variable syntax:

```text
Variable Name
  Variable Description
  
  Variable Value
]]>
```

Add "com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool" to a type value where the name is a variable name and the value is a full-qualified template tool class, for example:

To automatically import TextTool

1. Edit the built-in.xml file in the ...\data\reports\Tutorial 24 - Text Tool\reports .
2. Add a variable as shown in the following example.

```text
text
  A text tool
  com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool
  com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool
]]>
```

The result is a template code that can access com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool from $text.

| $text.noLineBreak($diagram.name) |
| --- |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once you have edited a template variable, you have to restart the application prior to generating a report through the modeling tool.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard can automatically import a Template Tool to a template without having to declare the #import statement in a template code manually (see <em><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Importing custom tool to template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Importing Custom Tool to Template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></em>).</p><p>To enable Report Wizard to automatically import a Template Tool, you have to use the existing template variable inside “<em>template.xml</em>” and “<em>report.xml</em>”.</p><p>The current template variable syntax:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e62d12b4-085e-45ac-80ff-df405fa3a4ae"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<variable>
  <name>Variable Name</name>
  <description>Variable Description</description>
  <type/>
  <value xml:space="preserve">Variable Value</value>
</variable>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Add &quot;com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool&quot; to a type value where the name is a variable name and the value is a full-qualified template tool class, for example:</p><p><br /></p><p>To automatically import TextTool</p><hr /><ol><li>Edit the built-in.xml file in the <em>...\data\reports\Tutorial 24 - Text Tool\reports</em>.</li><li>Add a variable as shown in the following example.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c01b07d8-75a2-4127-840a-5fcea178f72b"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[<variable>
  <name>text</name>
  <description>A text tool</description>
  <type>com.nomagic.magicreport.engine.Tool</type>
  <value xml:space="preserve">com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool</value>
</variable>]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The result is a template code that can access com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.TextTool from $text.</p><table><tbody><tr><td><pre>$text.noLineBreak($diagram.name)</pre></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="table-4.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Auto importing template tool" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94bc0efd-71ea-4d5a-8742-94117ad9f534"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Once you have edited a template variable, you have to restart the application prior to generating a report through the modeling tool.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target" style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995521 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00133: (2026x Refresh1) Automated Package Structure Creation

- page_id: `303995521`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995521/2026x+Refresh1+Automated+Package+Structure+Creation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:42.657+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of copy-pasting an existing package structure, you can create one automatically. You can do that by creating your own package structure configurations first and then applying them to your model via the **Create Package Structure** action.

##### Creating a package structure configuration

To create a package structure configuration

1. Create a configuration element, which can be any named element (e.g. a Package named 'SubsystemPackages').
2. Right-click the configuration element. In the shortcut menu, click Stereotype > select «package StructureConfiguration » from the menu, and click Apply .
3. Right-click the configuration element and open its Specification window.
4. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
5. Under «package StructureConfiguration » select applyToElements and click the Create Value button.
6. In the **Select Elements** dialog, select the element types for which the **Create Package Structure** action will be available. Use the **Multiple Selection**option to specify multiple element types.
7. Under «package StructureConfiguration » select CreateElements and click the Create Value button.
8. In the **Value** box, create alist of packages that will be created. The packages' nesting structure should be defined using spacing as indents (see the image below). If, in addition to spacing, any indent symbols (e.g., bullet points, numbers, etc.)are used to define the structure, see **Step 9**.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. (Optional) If the structure defined in the **CreateElements** tag is specified using any indent symbols (e.g., bullet points, numbers, etc.)that you do not want to include in the created packages' names, select **RemoveIndentSymbols**, click the **Edit Value**button and set it to *true*. By default, the **RemoveIndentSymbols**tag is set to *false*.
10. Click Close .

##### Creating a package structure automatically

To create a package structure automatically

1. Right-click an element for which you want to create the package structure defined in the configuration. In the shortcut menu, click **Tools**> **Create Package Structure**. The **Select Package Structure**dialog opens. The **Create Package Structure**action is only available for elements specified in the **applyToElements**tag of the package structure configuration.
2. In the Select Package Structure dialog, select the package structure configuration element that defines the package structure you want to create. Click OK . The selected package structure is created for the element.

MagicGrid project templates contain two package structure configurations included by default. To learn more, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using packages' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

ac:macro-id="13a50caf-9206-4cbe-8d80-dc6dc8945084"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The packages' nesting structure should be defined <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8b53de3e-6921-4993-95dd-3cd5a2d574ef">using spacing as indents</ac:inline-comment-marker> (see the image below). If, in addition to spacing, any indent symbols <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">(e.g., bullet points, numbers, etc.) </span>are used to define the structure, see <strong>Step 9</strong>. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating a package structure configuration step 8.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Automated Package Structure Creation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">(Optional) If the structure defined in the <strong style="text-align: left;">CreateElements</strong> tag is specified using any <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">indent symbols (e.g., bullet points, numbers, etc.)<span> that you do not want to include in the created packages' names, select <strong>RemoveIndentSymbols</strong>, click the <strong>Edit Value </strong>button and set it to <em>true</em>.<br /></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb9de8ee-ffd3-4bd9-a475-d652dca648ea"><ac:rich-text-body><p>By default, the <span><strong>RemoveIndentSymbols </strong>tag is set to <em>false</em>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Creating a <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">package structure automatically</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create a package structure automatically</span></p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Right-click an element for which you want to create the package structure defined in the configuration. In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Tools </strong>&gt; <strong>Create Package Structure</strong>. The <strong>Select Package Structure </strong>dialog opens.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e09cd78-c8c0-46fd-8311-67b1480dc671"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Create Package Structure </strong>action is only <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b818aa2c-e8f9-483e-aa44-9a719deab1f4">available</ac:inline-comment-marker> for elements specified in the <strong>applyToElements </strong>tag of the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">package structure configuration.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>In the <strong>Select Package Structure </strong>dialog, select the package structure configuration element that defines the package structure you want to create. Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The selected package structure is created for the element.</li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="53f2e3ec-35f2-4c74-a3c1-81e64aac455d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>MagicGrid project templates contain two package structure configurations included by default. To learn more, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using packages" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992174 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00134: (2026x Refresh1) Automated project usages

- page_id: `303992174`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992174/2026x+Refresh1+Automated+project+usages
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:39.958+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects > (2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418314237

418314240

418314239

**Automated usages of other projects in the main project**are the usages fully managed by our modeling tool. For example, it can create, remove, and rearrange as necessary without user intervention.

##### Version 17.0.3 and later

Version 17.0.3 introduces a new type of project usages, **automated project usages**. Our modeling tool fully manages them. It creates, removes, and rearranges as necessary without user intervention. **Automated project usages** are not shown in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing used projects' space='']**Used Project** dialog as only **user-defined usages** are shown there.

##### Before version 17.0.3

In earlier versions of our modeling tool, there was only one type of project usage. The user created these **user-defined usages** and they carried additional parameters; i.e., whether the used project was used in read-only or read/write mode, the used project loading options, which used project version was required, etc.

When modifying projects with complex composition, always keep the following condition in mind:

*Whenever there are model level references from elements in resource A (either a used project or the main project) to elements in resource B, there is a used project usage A → B.*

In earlier versions of our modeling tool, you could see this condition in action when working with used projects in read/write mode. Whenever you modified the contents of a used project so that it started to refer to another used project, and then you attempted to save it, our modeling tool would add an additional usage. You would then see the messages: “*Used project B was directly attached to A*” or “*Mounts were updated ...*”.

However, in some cases, this system behavior caused undesirable consequences, as shown in the following pages:

418321539

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Managing used projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning' space='']

For example, it can create, remove, and rearrange as necessary without user intervention.</p><h3>Version 17.0.3 and later</h3><p>Version 17.0.3 introduces a new type of project usages, <strong>automated project usages</strong>. Our modeling tool fully manages them. It creates, removes, and rearranges as necessary without user intervention. <strong>Automated project usages</strong> are not shown in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing used projects" /><ac:link-body><strong>Used Project</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> as only <strong>user-defined usages</strong> are shown there.</p><h3>Before version 17.0.3</h3><p>In earlier versions of our modeling tool, there was only one type of project usage. The user created these <strong>user-defined usages</strong> and they carried additional parameters; i.e., whether the used project was used in read-only or read/write mode, the used project loading options, which used project version was required, etc.</p><p>When modifying projects with complex composition, always keep the following condition in mind:</p><p style="text-align: left;margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Whenever there are model level references from elements in resource A (either a used project or the main project) to elements in resource B, there is a used project usage A → B.</em></p><p style="text-align: left;">In earlier versions of our modeling tool, you could see this condition in action when working with used projects in read/write mode. Whenever you modified the contents of a used project so that it started to refer to another used project, and then you attempted to save it, our modeling tool would add an additional usage. You would then see the messages: “<em>Used project B was directly attached to A</em>” or “<em>Mounts were updated ...</em>”.</p><p>However, in some cases, this system behavior caused undesirable consequences, as shown in the following pages:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d7aea0e8-3a0a-4ea3-abac-46cfb553328d" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48be605c-1e90-4dc8-81d5-c339bc12456a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418321539</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Managing used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing Used Projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Project partitioning" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Partitioning]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991331 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00135: (2026x Refresh1) Automatic delegation

- page_id: `303991331`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991331/2026x+Refresh1+Automatic+delegation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:40.578+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''], the automatic delegation functionality:

- Creates a connector from the port to the nearest border.
- Creates connectors with relevant ports between the ports of the nested parts (delegate).

##### Delegating ports and connectors automatically via port

To delegate ports and connectors automatically via port

1. Select a port. To create connectors with relevant ports for ports within a nested ports structure, make sure to invoke the **Delegate Port(s)** action either from the part or the port enclosing a nested ports structure. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. In the diagram toolbar, do one of the following:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools > Delegate Port(s) .
  2. Right-click the port, and in its shortcut menu, select Delegate Port(s) .

The needed port and connector are created to the nearest border:

- If a port is enclosed within a part that is not nested within another part, a connector is created to the nearest border - the diagram frame. [ATTACHMENT filename='Delegating_ports_and_connectors_automatically_1.png']
- If a port is enclosed within a part that is nested within another part, a connector is created to the nearest border - the enclosing part. [ATTACHMENT filename='Delegating_ports_and_connectors_automatically_2.png']

##### Delegating ports and connectors automatically via part

To delegate ports and connectors automatically via part

1. Select a part containing a port.
2. In the diagram toolbar, do one of the following:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools > Delegate Port(s) .
  2. Right-click the part, and in its shortcut menu, select Delegate Port(s) .

The needed port and connector are created automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Delegating ports and connectors automatically via connector

To delegate ports and connectors automatically via connector

1. Select a connector between two nested parts.
2. In the diagram toolbar, do one of the following:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools > Delegate Port(s) .
  2. Right-click the connector, and in its shortcut menu, select Delegate Port(s) .

The needed ports and connectors are created automatically. If the connector has a flow, that flow is also applied to the delegated connectors (as shown in the figure below). Some adjustments might be necessary. They will be indicated by the validation rules.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Autowire Parts' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Item Flow management' space='']

If the connector has a flow, that flow is also applied to the delegated connectors (as shown in the figure below). Some adjustments might be necessary. They will be indicated by the validation rules.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="autodelegation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Automatic delegation" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="ddd81961-b5f9-4b79-80d4-ee38febe3d59"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Autowire Parts" /></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="558c4e9b-87bd-4884-a8f0-2eca33797999"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Item Flow management" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995459 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00136: (2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation Wizard

- page_id: `303995459`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995459/2026x+Refresh1+Automatic+Instantiation+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:49:33.359+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This tool allows you to create instances of various entities with just a few clicks. It is useful for working with complex models, and for assembling large systems from parts.

While creating instances automatically, you can:

- Select properties for which the instances will be created.
- Change types of the instantiated properties.
- Change names of the instantiated properties.
- Apply custom stereotypesto the instantiated properties.
- Assign default values for the instantiated properties.

For properties having a multiplicity of more than 1, you can create as many instances of the same type as you need, since parallel parts can be added while creating instances. Previously, this feature had been supported by the **SysML v1 plugin only**.

The **Automatic Instantiation wizard** analyzes the structure of the selected element and collects all available information about that element from the model (attributes, properties, and so on). All this information is presented in the first step of the wizard.

The **Automatic Instantiation wizard** evaluates collected information and provides a suggestion of all possible instances to be created.

If you are satisfied with the default values provided in the **Automatic Instantiation wizard,** you can finish the wizard in the first step without making any changes. The instances will be created with the default values and in your working package. But if you want to change default values, create several different configurations, see the **Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard** and the **Case study:** sections on how to do that.

The **Automatic Instantiation Wizard** consists of three steps:

- Step #1. Selecting parts to be instantiated
- Step #2. Selecting a package
- Step #3. Creating a diagram (optional)

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select the elements for which you want to create instances.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

**Related pages**

- 
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Link' space='MED']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Value Specification' space='MED']
  - Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard
  - Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study
  - Automatic Instantiation wizard in SysML Plugin

All this information is presented in the first step of the wizard.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The <strong>Automatic Instantiation wizard</strong> evaluates collected information and provides a suggestion of all possible instances to be created.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you are satisfied with the default values provided in the <strong>Automatic Instantiation wizard,</strong> you can finish the wizard in the first step without making any changes. Selecting parts to be instantiated</span></li><li data-uuid="2ed862a6-6de7-4bd4-9c18-7ca8923dbeca"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Step #2. Selecting a package</span></li><li data-uuid="c1007a5c-7b1c-4264-8887-9f900790e952"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Step #3. Creating a diagram (optional)</span></li></ul><p><span> </span></p><p><span>To open the <strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="4ad37548-0df4-4d05-a6ac-3a94e042863f"><span>Select the elements for which you want to create instances.</span></li><li data-uuid="0914d484-3c38-4c8d-b0dc-8a2c6592d4f7">From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Instance</strong>.<br />The <strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard opens. </li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;" data-uuid="ed20410a-006f-416f-ad52-c2c0383a9f14"><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Link" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Value Specification" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Working+with+Automatic+Instantiation+Wizard">Working with Automatic Instantiation Wizard</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Automatic+Instantiation+Wizard+Case+Study">Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study</a></li><li class="with-breadcrumbs"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Automatic+Instantiation+wizard"><span class="current">Automatic Instantiation wizard</span> in SysML Plugin</a></li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997482 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00137: (2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study

- page_id: `303997482`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997482/2026x+Refresh1+Automatic+Instantiation+Wizard+Case+Study
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:13.963+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Consider this example:**

Let’s say we have three classes named **Administrator**, **Librarian**, and **Personnel**. These classes are connected in the following way:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The multiplicity in the association end near the **Librarian** is 4. In this case, the **Automatic Instantiation wizard** suggests four parts.

In the following figure, these values are displayed as **[1]**, **[2]**, **[3]**, and **[4]**. Each of them have a set of properties inherited from the type of the parts. In this case, we cannot add more parts because the multiplicity points to the particular number of available parts. The type of the part can be changed, if there is a sub element in the model.

In this case, class **Librarian** has a sub-class **Personnel**, so the type of **part [1]** can be changed to **Personnel** as you can see in the following figure.

Let’s select parts and properties for instantiation:

1. Select a new package, wherein our created instance specifications will be stored.
2. Create a new package Instances of the Administrator .
3. Create a new diagram, wherein our created instances will be displayed.
4. After selecting the diagram type (in this case, we will select to create a class diagram) and owner, let’s click the Finish button.
5. See the result in the following figure.

These classes are connected in the following way:</span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="example_of_classes.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation Wizard Case Study" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p>The multiplicity in the association end near the <strong>Librarian</strong> is 4. The type of the part can be changed, if there is a sub element in the model.</p><p>In this case, class <strong>Librarian</strong> has a sub-class <strong>Personnel</strong>, so the type of <strong>part [1]</strong> can be changed to <strong>Personnel</strong> as you can see in the following figure.</p><p>Let’s select parts and properties for instantiation:</p><ol><li>Select a new package, wherein our created instance specifications will be stored.</li><li>Create a new package <strong>Instances of the Administrator</strong>.</li><li>Create a new diagram, wherein our created instances will be displayed.</li><li>After selecting the diagram type (in this case, we will select to create a class diagram) and owner, let’s click the <strong>Finish</strong> button. </li><li>See the result in the following figure. </li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991471 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00138: (2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation wizard in SysML v1

- page_id: `303991471`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991471/2026x+Refresh1+Automatic+Instantiation+wizard+in+SysML+v1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:44.280+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard analyzes the structure of a selected element and collects all available information about that element from the model (attributes, properties, and so on). It evaluates collected information and suggests all possible instances to be created.

This tool allows you to create instances of various entities with just a few clicks. It is useful for working with complex models, and for assembling large systems from parts. For properties having a multiplicity of more than 1, you can create as many instances of the same type as you need, since parallel parts can be added while creating instances. While creating instances automatically, you can:

- Select properties for which the instances will be created.
- Change types of the instantiated properties.
- Change names of the instantiated properties.
- Apply custom stereotypes to the instantiated properties.
- Assign default values for the instantiated properties.

The wizard consists of the following areas:

- Steps area: displays all steps of the wizard and shows the current step you are working on.
- Element tree: displays the entire internal structure of the selected element.
- *Selection area*: allows you to select the elements to be instantiated.
- Instantiated properties area: allows you to change the default values and create several different sets of instances .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Automatic Instantiation wizard of the *Vehicle* Block with the main areas highlighted: Steps area, Element tree, Selection area, and Instantiated properties area.

##### Using the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

To create and display instance specifications of Blocks in the diagram by using the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select the Block for which you want to create instances.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > Create Instance (see the following figure). [ATTACHMENT filename='create_instance_command.png']
3. Follow the steps of the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='']How to work in each step of the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard >> If you are satisfied with the default values provided in the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard, you can finish the wizard in the first step without making any changes. The instances will be created with the default values and in your working package. To change default values or create several different configurations, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard' space=''].
4. Click Finish when you are done. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'] and a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] are created for the selected Block (see the figure below). All part instances are grouped under their owners which allows you to visually identify the structure of nested instances in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Containment tab' space=''] .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Block Definition Diagram with instances of the vehicle Block is created by using the Automatic Instantiation wizard.

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the**VehicleStructure**sample model. To open this sample, download*[ATTACHMENT filename='VehicleStructure.mdzip'].*

## PAGE 00139: (2026x Refresh1) Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF

- page_id: `303999994`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999994/2026x+Refresh1+Automatic+Instantiation+wizard+in+UAF
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:34.282+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Dialogs
- labels: ['automatic-instantiation-wizard', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1004562780

1004562782

1004562781

The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard is used to automatically instantiate any typical structure of UAF architecture.

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UAF elements.
2. Right-click them and choose Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

The wizard consists of these three steps:

truenone

##### Selecting parts

Select elements and their internal parts to instantiate.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Selecting a package

Select, create or clone the package to store the created actual structure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating a diagram

Specify diagram name, select diagram type to visualize the actual structure and the package to store the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1004562777

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Instantiating structures' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996662 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00140: (2026x Refresh1) Automatic numbering

- page_id: `303996662`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996662/2026x+Refresh1+Automatic+numbering
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:13.609+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with model elements > (2026x Refresh1) Generic numbering mechanism > (2026x Refresh1) Working with generic numbering mechanism
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Make sure that [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Numbering Customizations' space=''] are already created.

With an automatic numbering feature, you can number the elements of the selected type upon the element creation or modification. You can specify the number display location after the elements are numbered.

To enable automatic element numbering

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog opens.
2. In the options group list, select or expand the General option and click Numbering either in the options list or in the options group list. The numbering properties opens.
3. Set the Use Element Auto-numbering property value to true .
4. Click OK .

To specify the element number display location

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the selected element’s shortcut menu, click Symbol Properties . The Symbol Properties dialog opens.
  - From the main menu, select Options > Project . Then, in the opened [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Setting project options' space='']**Project Options** dialog , select the Symbol styles > Default group.
2. Select one of the following **Element Numbering Display Mode** property value: Property valueVisualization on the element shapeAbove the element name[IMAGE alt='' src='']Before the element name[IMAGE alt='' src='']Do not display on symbol[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click OK after you have selected.

You can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Manual numbering' space=''].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992728 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00141: (2026x Refresh1) Autosave

- page_id: `303992728`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992728/2026x+Refresh1+Autosave
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:11.641+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After you stop working with the modeling tool,an idle time passes and the current project is saved to a special file called the AutoRecovery file. 
If the application is terminated normally, the AutoRecovery file is removed. If the application crashes, the AutoRecovery file is kept. On startup, the modeling tool checks for an AutoRecovery file. If it exists, the modeling tool suggests loading the project from this file.

To save an AutoRecovery file of the open project(s) when a system is not in use

1. Open the Environment Options dialog.
2. In the General pane, select the Save/Load group, and then set the Save Project Recovery Data on Idle option to true .
3. Enter the system idle time (in minutes) in the Idle Time to Activate Recovery Save box. This is the necessary idle time in order to activate the AutoRecovery save.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>After you stop working with the modeling tool,<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> an idle time passes</span> and the current project is saved to a special file called the AutoRecovery file. <br class="atl-forced-newline" />If the application is terminated normally, the AutoRecovery file is removed. If the application crashes, the AutoRecovery file is kept. On startup, the modeling tool checks for an AutoRecovery file. If it exists, the modeling tool suggests loading the project from this file.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To save an AutoRecovery file of the open project(s) when a system is not in use</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> pane, select the <strong>Save/Load</strong> group, and then set the <strong>Save Project Recovery Data on Idle </strong>option to <em>true</em>. </li><li>Enter the system idle time (in minutes) in the <strong>Idle</strong> <strong>Time</strong> <strong>to</strong> <strong>Activate</strong> <strong>Recovery</strong> <strong>Save</strong> box. This is the necessary idle time in order to activate the AutoRecovery save.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991338 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00142: (2026x Refresh1) Autowire Parts

- page_id: `303991338`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991338/2026x+Refresh1+Autowire+Parts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:40.643+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling structure with Blocks > (2026x Refresh1) Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram
- labels: ['automatic-connections', 'automatic-connectors', 'connect-parts', 'autowire', 'mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Autowire Parts** capability analyzes and finds compatible ports/interfaces, and automatically creates [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MED'] between [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED']. The figure below demonstrates the *Thermal View* Internal Block diagram before and after Autowire Parts when the entire diagram is selected. This functionality provides a quick and easy way to create multiple connectors simultaneously.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED']and ports are displayed or created in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''], the parts can be related to one another via [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='']Connectors. You can manually create these connections by using the **Connector** button from the part's/port's shape smart manipulator toolbar or from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface' space='']. You can also do this automatically by using the **Autowire Parts** command from the Tools menu ([IMAGE alt='' src='']) in the diagram toolbar, if the following conditionsconditions are satisfied:

- Parts and ports are displayed on the Internal Block diagram pane.
- The displayed ports are [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='MED'].
- Ports are typed by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='MED'] that have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='MED'] defined.
- Types and directions of these flow properties are compatible.
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space=''] do not exist in the model.
- Only one-to-one connection is supported.
- Multiple interfaces are not supported.

See an example [ATTACHMENT filename='Autowire-Parts.mdzip']

The following procedure describes how to use the **Autowire Parts** function. This functionality works for an entire diagram or individually selected parts and ports.

To connect Part Properties via compatible ports automatically

1. Select any number of part or port shapes, or the diagram pane.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Tools > **Autowire Parts**, or press Ctrl+Alt+W. InformationIf new connectors are not created after clicking the **Autowire Parts**, check if all required are satisfied.
3. In the opened Messages window, you can specify two options: [ATTACHMENT filename='autowiring_message.png']
  - Highlight new connections - leave the check box selected, if you want the newly created Connectors to be highlighted on the diagram pane, and be able to confirm or delete them manually.
  - Show this message next time - leave the check box selected, if you want this message to appear the next time you click the Autowire Parts command.
4. Click OK .
5. The Connector(s) between Part Properties via compatible interfaces are created in the model. If you left the Highlight new connection check box selected in the Messages window (step 3, first bullet), you must review the newly created Connectors in the following way (see the figure below): a. Select the Connector. b. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='confirm_connector.png'] and choose: - Confirm Connector - removes the highlight from the Connector. - Delete Connector - removes the Connector from the model. - Ignore - removes the highlight from the Connector. - Select In Validation Results - opens the Active Validation Results table. [ATTACHMENT filename='confirm_connection.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990461 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00144: (2026x Refresh1) AV-1 Overview and Summary Information

- page_id: `303990461`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990461/2026x+Refresh1+AV-1+Overview+and+Summary+Information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:33.280+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-1-overview-and-summary-information', 'av-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229302728

229302730

229302729

**Description**

The overview and summary information contained within the AV-1 provides executive-level summary information in a consistent form that allows quick reference and comparison between Architectural Descriptions. The written content of the AV-1 content describes the concepts contained in the pictorial representation of the OV-1.

The AV-1 frames the context for the Architectural Description. The AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. It should contain sufficient information to enable a reader to select a single Architectural Description from among many to read in more detail. The AV-1 serves two additional purposes:

- In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide.
- When the architecture is built, the AV-1 provides summary information concerning who, what, when, why, and how of the plan as well as a navigation aid to the models that have been created.

The AV-1 may be used to:

- Scope the architecture effort.
- Provide context to the architecture effort.
- Define the architecture effort.
- Summarize the findings from the architecture effort.
- Assist search within an architecture repository.

**Implementation**

The AV-1 can be represented:

- Using an AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='av_1.png']
- Using the following (.docx) reports: - AV-1 Overview and Summary Information. - AV-1 DARS.

The AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report includes summarizing information about all developed viewpoints and views.

The AV-1 DARS report includes the same information as in the AV-1 Overview & Summary Information report and also some additional data that are required particularly by DoD Architecture Registry System (DARS). The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.

229302727

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architectural Description' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architectural Reference' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Architecture Metadata' space='MED']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generating DARS report' space='']
