The written content of the AV-1 content describes the concepts contained in the pictorial representation of the OV-1.</p><p>The AV-1 frames the context for the Architectural Description. The AV-1 includes assumptions, constraints, and limitations that may affect high-level decisions relating to an architecture-based work program. It should contain sufficient information to enable a reader to select a single Architectural Description from among many to read in more detail. The AV-1 serves two additional purposes:</p><ul><li>In the initial phases of architecture development, it serves as a planning guide.</li><li>When the architecture is built, the AV-1 provides summary information concerning who, what, when, why, and how of the plan as well as a navigation aid to the models that have been created.</li></ul><p>The AV-1 may be used to:</p><ul><li>Scope the architecture effort.</li><li>Provide context to the architecture effort.</li><li>Define the architecture effort.</li><li>Summarize the findings from the architecture effort.</li><li>Assist search within an architecture repository.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The AV-1 can be represented:</p><ul><li>Using an AV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="AV-1 Overview and Summary Information" ac:alt="AV-1 Overview and Summary Information"><ri:attachment ri:filename="av_1.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) AV-1 Overview and Summary Information" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Using the following (.docx) reports:<br /><p>- AV-1 Overview and Summary Information.</p><p>- AV-1 DARS.</p></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The AV-1 Overview &amp; Summary Information report includes summarizing information about all developed viewpoints and views.</p><p>The AV-1 DARS report includes the same information as in the AV-1 Overview &amp; Summary Information report and also some additional data that are required particularly by DoD Architecture Registry System (DARS). The AV-1 DARS report has the DARS compatible structure. Before generating the report you must load the DARS template in AV-1 and provide the template with relevant data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29d928db-eff2-4732-bb69-65fec83182d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229302727</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Architectural Description" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Architectural Reference" /></ac:link></span><br /></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Architecture Metadata" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating DARS report" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating DARS template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990457 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00145: (2026x Refresh1) AV-2 Integrated Dictionary

- page_id: `303990457`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990457/2026x+Refresh1+AV-2+Integrated+Dictionary
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:32.840+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF 2.0 > (2026x Refresh1) DoDAF All Views viewpoint
- labels: ['av-2', 'av-2-integrated-dictionary', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229449780

229449782

229449781

**Description**

The AV-2 presents all the metadata used in an architecture. An AV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element (e.g., DoDAF Meta-model, a published document or policy).

An AV-2 shows elements from the DoDAF Meta-model that have been described in the Architectural Description and new elements that have been introduced by the Architectural Description.

It is essential that organizations within the DoD use the same terms to refer to a thing. Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:

- Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.
- Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.
- Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.
- Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.

**Implementation**

AV-2 can be represented:

- An AV-2 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.

229449779

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Definition' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Alias' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Same As' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Generating document based reports' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e42b2126-cf50-43a4-9cca-ef572e144bb0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229449780</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e012d3f-f426-45af-9b06-6d30696ad936"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229449782</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4d2b797-2cf8-464e-b715-d2a279b81f8e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229449781</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The AV-2 presents all the metadata used in an architecture. An AV-2 presents all the data as a hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element (e.g., DoDAF Meta-model, a published document or policy).</p><p>An AV-2 shows elements from the DoDAF Meta-model that have been described in the Architectural Description and new elements that have been introduced by the Architectural Description.</p><p>It is essential that organizations within the DoD use the same terms to refer to a thing. Because of the interrelationship among models and across architecture efforts, it is useful to define common terminology with common definitions (referred to as taxonomies) in the development of the models within the Architectural Description. These taxonomies can be used as building blocks for DoDAF-described Models and Fit-for-Purpose Views within the Architectural Description. The need for standard taxonomies derives from lessons learned from early DoD Architectural Description development issues as well as from federation pilots conducted within the Department. Federation of Architectural Descriptions were made much more difficult because of the use of different terminology to represent the same architectural data. Use of taxonomies to build models for the architecture has the following benefits over free-text labeling:</p><ul><li>Provides consistency across populated views, based on DoDAF-described Models.</li><li>Provides consistency across Architectural Descriptions.</li><li>Facilitates Architectural Description development, validation, maintenance, and re-use.</li><li>Traces architectural data to authoritative data sources.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>AV-2 can be represented:</p><ul><li>An AV-2 table.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:title="AV-2 Integrated Dictionary" ac:alt="AV-2 Integrated Dictionary"><ri:attachment ri:filename="av_2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) AV-2 Integrated Dictionary" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ul><li>Using a report, which is automatically generated from all data. Since only a partial AV-2 report can be generated, the rest of data must be filled in manually.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fabe6b99-9e45-45b7-8985-b2e5fc71e744"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229449779</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Definition" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Alias" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Same As" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Generating document based reports" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997441 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00146: (2026x Refresh1) Available Transformations

- page_id: `303997441`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997441/2026x+Refresh1+Available+Transformations
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:12.155+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Transformations are usually used for converting models between different modeling domains. Transformations are named by the types of their source models and their destination models. These are the available transformations:

- Any to Any . This transformation copies all your model or part of it to another package without making any changes. You can also remap types in the destination model by applying some type mapping rules.
- Profile Migration . Helper transformation for migrating models using one profile to models using another profile (usually - between different versions of the same profile - old and new).

UML to SQL(Generic/Oracle), SQL to UML, UML to XML Schema, XML Schema to UML transformations are available with the separately-installed Cameo Data Modeler plugin (which comes free of charge with Architect and Enterprise Editions and is separately purchasable for Standard and Professional editions).

Additional custom types of transformations can be defined by plugins.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Transformations are usually used for converting models between different modeling domains. Transformations are named by the types of their source models and their destination models. These are the available transformations:</p><ul><li><strong>Any to Any</strong>. This transformation copies all your model or part of it to another package without making any changes. You can also remap types in the destination model by applying some type mapping rules.</li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Profile Migration</strong>. Helper transformation for migrating models using one profile to models using another profile (usually - between different versions of the same profile - old and new).</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d03b38f1-c9ac-4094-9c27-5adee5b78388"><ac:rich-text-body><p>UML to SQL(Generic/Oracle), SQL to UML, UML to XML Schema, XML Schema to UML transformations are available with the separately-installed Cameo Data Modeler plugin (which comes free of charge with Architect and Enterprise Editions and is separately purchasable for Standard and Professional editions).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83a24d82-c890-4d54-9bba-50ac5c213ddd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Additional custom types of transformations can be defined by plugins.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997645 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00147: (2026x Refresh1) Avoiding Conflicting Legend items

- page_id: `303997645`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997645/2026x+Refresh1+Avoiding+Conflicting+Legend+items
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:31.314+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagramming > (2026x Refresh1) Legends > (2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends > (2026x Refresh1) Specifying Legend Items
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you use multiple Legends in one diagram, you should specify a different set of adornment properties for Legend Items of each Legend. This way, Legend Items of different Legends do not conflict when applied to the same element. For example, try using **Fill Color** in one Legend and **Icon** in another. If you want to use the **Icon** property in multiple Legends, then specify different **Icon Position on Shape**/**Path** for Legend Items of each Legend. In addition, you can define Legend Item adornment by specifying the **Pen Color**, **Line Width,** and **Line Style** properties describing the appearance of a shape border or path.

#### TIP: Legend Item priority

Legend Item priority

If you can not avoid conflicting Legend Items in a diagram or table, specify the **Priority** property for each Legend Item. It defines which Legend Item should be applied in case several Legend Items conflict.

##### Example

Let's say you want to use two Legends in a Requirements Diagram: the *Status* Legend indicating the status of each Requirement and the *Risk* Legend showing the risk level of Requirements. It is likely that Legend Items from both Legends will be applied to the same Requirement. For example, a single Requirement can have both status and risk values specified. Let's analyze this specific use case by creating the Legends mentioned above and defining their Legend Item adornment properties so that Legend Items would not conflict when applied to the same Requirement:

1. Open the desired Requirements Diagram.
2. In the diagram, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Legends' space=''] intended to show the status of Requirements and name the Legend Status .
3. Create and define the Legend Items for the Status Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''] indicating four possible Requirement status values and name them Closed , Approved , Disapproved, and In Progress .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired Fill Color .
  4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Applying Legend Items' space='']Specify the **Elements by Condition** property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the Status property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Repeat the steps from b. to d. with the rest of the Legend Items.
4. In the same diagram, create a Legend to indicate the risk level of Requirements and name the Legend Risk .
5. Create and define the Legend Items for the Risk Legend by completing the following steps:
  1. Create three Legend Items indicating three possible Requirement risk levels and name them High , Medium , and Low .
  2. Open the Specification window of the first Legend Item.
  3. Specify the Legend Item adornment by selecting the desired **Icon** and **Icon Position on Shape**. Specifying IconWhen specifying the **Icon** property:Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select an image from the Image Library of your modeling tool.Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select a custom image from the local file system.Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to specify the URL of an image.
  4. Specify the **Elements by Condition** property by creating a Property Test operation and selecting the **Risk** property value the Legend Item represents.
  5. Repeat the steps from b. to d. with the rest of the Legend Items.

If you followed the above steps, you have a Requirements Diagram with the *Status* and *Risk* Legends applied to it. The Legend Items of both Legends do not conflict when applied to the same shape, as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The figure displays a Requirement shape with two Legend Items applied to it. 
The Legend Items do not conflict due to different adornment properties used to define them.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you use multiple Legends in one diagram, you should specify a different set of adornment properties for Legend Items of each Legend. This way, Legend Items of different Legends do not conflict when applied to the same element. For example, try using <strong>Fill Color</strong> in one Legend and <strong>Icon</strong> in another. If you want to use the <strong>Icon</strong> property in multiple Legends, then specify different <strong>Icon Position on Shape</strong>/<strong>Path</strong> for Legend Items of each Legend. In addition, you can define Legend Item adornment by specifying the <strong>Pen Color</strong>, <strong>Line Width,</strong> and <strong>Line Style</strong> properties describing the appearance of a shape border or path.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ade7b350-1fd3-44cc-9bdc-bf32eb39765d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Legend Item priority</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you can not avoid conflicting Legend Items in a diagram or table, specify the <strong>Priority</strong> property for each Legend Item. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999579 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00148: (2026x Refresh1) Background Task Manager

- page_id: `303999579`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999579/2026x+Refresh1+Background+Task+Manager
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:08.851+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Improving performance
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1427371678

1427371681

1427371679

Low-priority or routine tasks are moved to separate threads and are performed as background tasks. These tasks do not stop a modeling tool from running, and you can work with your model as usual. Background tasks are displayed in the Background Task Manager at the bottom right corner of the modeling tool window. For example, when a search process is running, the progress bar is displayed in the Background Task Manager, shown in the following figure.

If a background task runs for too long, you can cancel it as described below.

To end an ongoing process

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='end_process.png'] in the Background Task Manager status bar to cancel the selected process. If you cancel the last of several tasks (or only one existing process), the Background Task Manager is turned off.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Background Task Manager showing the progress of a searching task.

1427371677

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Improving performance' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Memory monitor' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Memory allocation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Active validation period' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Background tasks are displayed in the Background Task Manager at the bottom right corner of the modeling tool window. For example, when a search process is running, the progress bar is displayed in the Background Task Manager, shown in the following figure.</p><p>If a background task runs for too long, you can cancel it as described below.</p><p><br /></p><p>To end an ongoing process</p><hr /><ul><li>Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="end_process.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Background Task Manager status bar to cancel the selected process. If you cancel the last of several tasks (or only one existing process), the Background Task Manager is turned off.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="progress_bar.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Background Task Manager" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Background Task Manager showing the progress of a searching task.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6114c90-5668-43eb-a1ec-3efcad6b5ae9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1427371677</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Improving performance" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Memory monitor" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Memory allocation" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Active validation period" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00149: (2026x Refresh1) Backward traceability – specification

- page_id: `303995685`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995685/2026x+Refresh1+Backward+traceability+%E2%80%93+specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:57.915+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Model analysis > (2026x Refresh1) Traceability > (2026x Refresh1) Predefined traceability rules
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

| Property Name | Description | Applied For: | Reference Through: | Value elements type | Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Manifested in Artifacts | The property shows which components are manifested in an artifact. | Artifact | Relationships: Manifestation | Component | Example |
| Specifying Class | The property shows the classes representing the component specification from the Design model. | Component | Relationships: Abstraction | Class | Example |
| Specifying Component | The property shows the components that are realized by classifiers through component realization. | Classifier | Relationships: Component Realization, Realization | Component | Example |
| Specifying Use Case | The property shows the use cases (from the Requirements model) representing the class specification. | Class | Relationships: Abstraction | Use Case | Example |
| Specifying Use Case | The property shows the use cases that specify the given use case in the higher level of abstraction. For example, the Business Use Case specifies the Requirements Use Case. | Use Case | Relationships: Abstraction | Use Case | Example |
| Realized Interface | The property shows the interfaces specifying the contract, in which the related classifier conforms to. | Classifier | Relationships: Interface Realization | Interface | Example |
| Specifying Element, All Specifying ElementsAvailable in Enterprise and Architect editions only. | The Specifying Element property gathers specifying elements from the upper abstraction level. The All Specifying Elements property transitively gathers specifying elements from all upper abstraction levels. | Element | Relationships: Abstraction, Component Realization, Interface Realization. | Element | Example |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998571 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00150: (2026x Refresh1) Basic concepts

- page_id: `303998571`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998571/2026x+Refresh1+Basic+concepts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:45.804+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Data-related modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Entity-relationship (ER) modeling and diagrams
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An entity is a thing capable of an existence. An entity usually refers to some aspect of the real world, which can be distinguished from other aspects of the real world (a person, place, customer transaction, order, etc.).

An entity is represented by a box shape on the diagram. An entity has two compartments where properties (columns) of the entity can be specified. The upper compartment holds the primary key properties of the entity; the lower compartment holds other properties.

A relationship between entities describes how entities are associated. Relationships are represented by lines, connecting entities. Relationship end adornments indicate multiplicities of these ends. Multiplicity is the number of entity instances that can be associated with a number of another entity instances. Relationship multiplicity is represented by three symbols, the so-called “crow's foot notation” or “Information Engineering notation”.

The following table shows symbols of the relationship multiplicity.

| Name | Value | Notation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Zero | Zero |  |
| Vertical | One |  |
| Crow's foot | Many |  |

Multiplicity lower bounds and upper bounds are paired into one adornment, as shown in the following table. Note that any lower bound, which is more that 0 is treated as 1 (this also includes lower bounds greater than 1, such as 2.). Also, any upper bound which is greater than 1 is treated as Many (this also includes upper bounds less than unlimited, such as 7.).

| Min | Max | Read as | Figure |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 0 | 1 | One (optional) |  |
| 1 | 1 | One (mandatory) |  |
| 0 | Many | Many (optional) |  |
| 1 | Many | Many (mandatory) |  |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Basic ER diagram example.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- Some authors use Entity Type term to signify the description of a set of entities and Entity Instance term to signify concrete exemplar from that set. Entity term used in this manual corresponds to Entity Type .
- Data modeling world frequently uses term Cardinality to denote the allowable numbers of entity instances, which can be associated. But with the rise of the UML, the more correct term Multiplicity was introduced and term Cardinality is only used to denote concrete numbers of entity instances, which are associated. Hence in the example Person [0..1]------
- -[0..*] Book, the ranges [0..1] and [0..*] are called multiplicities. And if we have person “John Doe” associated with books “Moby Dick” and “Origin of Species”, we have a cardinality of 2 for loaned books role (and 1 on an opposite end - current reader role). Note that cardinality is always concrete number while multiplicity denotes range of possible cardinalities.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The upper compartment holds the primary key properties of the entity; the lower compartment holds other properties.</p><p>A relationship between entities describes how entities are associated. Multiplicity is the number of entity instances that can be associated with a number of another entity instances. ## PAGE 00153: (2026x Refresh1) Basic tasks in tables

- page_id: `303990134`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990134/2026x+Refresh1+Basic+tasks+in+tables
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:16.000+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Diagrams > (2026x Refresh1) Tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Important

218215203

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Important

218215208

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Important

218215207

Tables are diagrams which allow you to manage any amount of model data in a spreadsheet-like form. It is much easier to analyze a group of data in one place, especially when you can create new elements, add existing elements, edit their properties directly from the table, and paste data from the clipboard. This allows you to sort and filter data, show, hide, reorder columns, and clear or edit individual cells. Exporting tables into more commonly used file formats, such as *.html*, *.csv*, or *.xlsx*, makes it simple to share them with stakeholders.

#### NOTE: Important

Important

Each table row represents an element. Each column represents element properties. The column header and row number header are highlighted in grey when selecting single or multiple cells.

The following procedures provide the basic tasks to get you started working with all modeling tool tables:

218215198

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Editing property values' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992125 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00154: (2026x Refresh1) Behavior to structure synchronization

- page_id: `303992125`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992125/2026x+Refresh1+Behavior+to+structure+synchronization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:35.447+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1084440424

1084440437

1084440427

The behavior to structure synchronization checks the consistency of the Internal Block diagram (IBD) with Activities, automatically detects inconsistencies and enables users to handle each inconsistency individually by providing solvers. As a result, the coherence of IBDs with Activities is ensured at any stage of the model development process.

The behavior to structure synchronization validates the Part Properties displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] to check for compatible

- Proxy Ports
- Flow Properties
- Interface Blocks
- Connectors
- Item Flows

To verify whether or not the structural elements (e.g., a Proxy Port and an Interface Block) and behavioral elements (e.g., a flow) are compatible, validation rules are used. If any inconsistencies are found, they are highlighted in yellow in the Internal Block Diagram and displayed in the**Validation Results** panel. Then, they can be individually handled using solvers.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing behavior to structure.

##### Checking behavior to structure synchronization

You need to set the **Allocation Mode**(**Options**>**Project**>**SysML**>**Allocation Mode**)project optionto **Usage** before you can synchronize behavior to structure.

To check the structure model consistency with the behavior model

1. In the toolbar of the Internal Block Diagram, click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools.
2. In the open drop-down menu, select Behavior to Structure Synchronization. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

The behavior to structure synchronization check is initiated.

484219123

**Continue reading**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="841a5f4d-74a0-4fa4-aaed-99dc00301c21"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1084440424</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a176ceb-ea86-431b-a67d-47098a620fcc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1084440437</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c95de57f-624d-4ab8-8f50-e48cce559498"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1084440427</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The behavior to structure synchronization checks the consistency of the Internal Block diagram (IBD) with Activities, automatically detects inconsistencies and enables users to handle each inconsistency individually by providing solvers. As a result, the coherence of IBDs with Activities is ensured at any stage of the model development process.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ea75ada-62b5-429b-ae2d-cb2efce51e89"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-sd8lDPHtlk" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>   <ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3216511a-9150-41ad-9be8-7f35af1e11bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Zuphq7gFL1w" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>The behavior to structure synchronization validates the Part Properties displayed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Internal Block Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to check for compatible</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="784a8dde-e6d8-4776-b689-e7f7eddb8085">Proxy Ports</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="784a8dde-e6d8-4776-b689-e7f7eddb8085">Flow Properties</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="784a8dde-e6d8-4776-b689-e7f7eddb8085">Interface Blocks</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="784a8dde-e6d8-4776-b689-e7f7eddb8085">Connectors</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="784a8dde-e6d8-4776-b689-e7f7eddb8085">Item Flows</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><p>To verify whether or not the structural elements (e.g., a Proxy Port and an Interface Block) and behavioral elements (e.g., a flow) are compatible, validation rules are used. If any inconsistencies are found, they are highlighted in yellow in the Internal Block Diagram and displayed in the<strong> Validation Results</strong> panel. Then, they can be individually handled using solvers.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="structure_and_behavior_models_synchronization.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Behavior to structure synchronization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Synchronizing behavior to structure.</h6><h3>Checking behavior to structure synchronization </h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7e7c0f2-00c7-404d-a569-4855cca3bc1b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to set the <strong>Allocation Mode</strong><span> </span><span>(</span><strong>Options</strong><span> &gt;</span><strong> Project</strong><span> &gt; </span><strong>SysML</strong><span> &gt; </span><strong>Allocation Mode</strong><span>) </span>project option<span> </span>to <strong>Usage</strong> before you can synchronize behavior to structure.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />To check the structure model consistency with the behavior model</p><hr /><ol><li>In the toolbar of the Internal Block Diagram, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Behavior to structure synchronization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Tools.</li><li>In the open drop-down menu, select <strong>Behavior to Structure Synchronization.</strong><br /><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="behavior_to_structure_sync_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Behavior to structure synchronization" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></li></ol><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a4344bbf-9a03-49f3-8337-2857606c756f">The behavior to structure synchronization check is initiated.</ac:inline-comment-marker> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b67cc1f-75c3-42fa-b535-cd642921b424"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">484219123</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Continue reading</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3cb859a5-2ed8-42c2-b553-a77c7e0bf462" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992555 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00155: (2026x Refresh1) Blackbox ICD Table

- page_id: `303992555`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992555/2026x+Refresh1+Blackbox+ICD+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:02.715+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 diagram descriptions
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Blackbox ICD Table represents all external Ports/interfaces of the selected Block. The table is useful when trying to identify what kind of inputs and outputs are available in the selected system.

In the following example, the distiller system has two inputs (*dirty water*and *q in*) and three outputs (*bypass*, *purified* and *sludge*). In this case, the Port *dirty water*, Port *purified*, Port *bypass* support such value properties as: *water temp*, *water press*, etc. Pay attention, that the input to distiller is a *dirty water* (row 1) and the output from distiller is *purified*water (row 4). Both of them contains the same properties but it may have different values defined (they are not defined in the figure). [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating Interface Control Document tables' space='']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Blackbox ICD Table of the Distiller Block.

A Blackbox ICD Table consists of the following columns.

| Column name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| # | A row number. |
| ~ ^ | Displays if Port is conjugated (~) or inherited (^). |
| Port Name | A Port name. |
| Port Type | A Port type. |
| Type Features | Displays all the features (e.g., Flow property, Part property) of the Port type. |
| Direction | Displays a combined direction prefix of the Port. |
| Documentation | Displays a documentation text. If it is not described, click to edit. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In this case, the Port <em style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">dirty water</em>, Port <em style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">purified</em>, Port <em style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">bypass</em> support such value properties as: <em style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">water temp</em>, <em style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">water press</em>, etc. Pay attention, that the input to distiller is a <em style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">dirty water</em> (row 1) and the output from distiller is <em style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">purified</em></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> water (row 4). <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996900 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00156: (2026x Refresh1) Block structure

- page_id: `303996900`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996900/2026x+Refresh1+Block+structure
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:34.307+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Sample models > (2026x Refresh1) Creating a simple model > (2026x Refresh1) Blocks and Parts
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The three components are connected through ports that correspond electrical pins. The source and the resistor each have both a positive and a negative pin, whereas the ground has only one positive pin. Electrical energy (carried by electric charge) is transmitted between components through the pins. The image below contains the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] that illustrates this.

A new abstract block *TwoPinComponent* is created to illustrate abstract components that have two ports (pins). These two ports are named *p*******and*n*, and they are both of the *ChargeFlowElement*type. ChargeFlowElement belongs to the simulation library (*SysPhSLibrary.mdzip*), and it can be selected by unchecking **Apply Filter**in the type list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *p* and*n* ports typed by *ChargeFlow Element*Interface Block.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The three components are connected through ports that correspond electrical pins. The source and the resistor each have both a positive and a negative pin, whereas the ground has only one positive pin. Electrical energy (carried by electric charge) is transmitted between components through the pins. The image below contains the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Block Definition Diagram (BDD)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that illustrates this. </p><p>A new abstract block <em>TwoPinComponent</em> is created to illustrate abstract components that have two ports (pins). These two ports are named <em>p</em><strong><em> </em> </strong>and<em> n</em>, and they are both of the <em>ChargeFlowElement </em>type. ChargeFlowElement belongs to the simulation library (<em>SysPhSLibrary.mdzip</em>), and it can be selected by unchecking <strong>Apply Filter  </strong>in the type list. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="470"><ri:attachment ri:filename="electrical_components.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Block structure" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">The <em>p</em> and<em> n</em> ports typed by <em>ChargeFlow Element </em>Interface Block.</span></h6><p><br /></p><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303992391 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00157: (2026x Refresh1) Blocks and Parts

- page_id: `303992391`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303992391/2026x+Refresh1+Blocks+and+Parts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:21:55.329+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Sample models > (2026x Refresh1) Creating a simple model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1553744160

1553744160

1553744160

The electrical circuit model has the main *block* and the*circuit*. To model the structure of the circuit, create a Circuit [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] and an associated [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']. The circuit has three parts: *a source*, *a ground*, and *a resistor*. These parts are of a different type since they all have a different behavior. It is necessary to create a Block for each of these parts. The picture below shows how the Internal Block Diagram look like.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The *Circuit* IBD consists of three parts: *source*, *ground* and *resistor*.

1553744159

**Related pages:**

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d457e48-5ef7-4c3d-9002-e0774a35cc3e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1553744160</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd9cbac2-d35a-499d-beae-f6d1b35b51cd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1553744160</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8590ad01-0b68-4b53-9825-e6964ebce8c1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1553744160</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The electrical circuit model has the main <em>block</em> and the<em> circuit</em>. To model the structure of the circuit, create a Circuit <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> and an associated <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Internal Block Diagram (IBD)]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The circuit has three parts: <em>a source</em>, <em>a ground</em>, and <em>a resistor</em>. These parts are of a different type since they all have a different behavior. It is necessary to create a Block for each of these parts. The picture below shows how the Internal Block Diagram look like. </p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ibd_sample_model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Blocks and Parts" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The <em>Circuit</em> IBD consists of three parts: <em>source</em>, <em>ground</em> and <em>resistor</em>.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a28dfc3c-f9c4-4c32-b5d8-8f5dd784e13d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1553744159</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages:</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b30fcee2-0b0c-40d0-92aa-37f61062237b" /><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998297 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00158: (2026x Refresh1) Bookmark tool

- page_id: `303998297`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998297/2026x+Refresh1+Bookmark+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:29:23.448+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Report Wizard > (2026x Refresh1) Helper tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

2050367121

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

2050367135

2050367115

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2050367125

$bookmark contains utility functions for bookmarking. The functions of this module are accessible from templates through **$bookmark**.

#### INFO: Information

Information

The default style of bookmarks depends on the editor used. For example, Microsoft Word 2003 displays hyperlinks in blue.

****

###### **$bookmark.openURL(url, content)**

Create a hyperlink to open a URL.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | url | java.lang.String | A URL text. |
| content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The text content with a hyperlink. |

For example:

```text

```

****

###### **$bookmark.openURL(url, content)**

Create a hyperlink to open a URL.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | url | java.net.URL | A URL instance. |
| content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The text content with a hyperlink. |

For example:

```text

```

****

###### **$bookmark.openURL(uri, content)**

Create a hyperlink to open a URI.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | uri | java.net.URI | A URI instance. |
| content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The content with a hyperlink. |

For example:

```text

```

****

###### **$bookmark.open(content)**

Create a hyperlink for a bookmark. The bookmark ID will be automatically generated from the content.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The text content with a hyperlink. |

****

###### **$bookmark.open(bookmarkId, content)**

Create a hyperlink for a bookmark. The bookmark ID value must match the parameter bookmark ID that passes onto the link.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | bookmarkId | java.lang.String | The bookmark ID. |
|  | content | java.lang.Object | The text content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Link | The text content with a hyperlink. |

For example:

```text

```

###### **$bookmark.create(bookmarkObject)**

Create a bookmark. The bookmark ID will be automatically generated from the bookmark object.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | bookmarkObject | java.lang.Object | A bookmark object or content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Bookmark | The content with a bookmark. |

****

###### **$bookmark.create(bookmarkId, bookmarkObject)**

Create a bookmark. The default element type for this function is "label".

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | bookmarkId | java.lang.String | The bookmark ID. |
|  | bookmarkObject | java.lang.Object | A bookmark object or content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Bookmark | The content with a bookmark. |

For example:

```text

```

###### **$bookmark.create(bookmarkId, bookmarkObject, elementType)**

Create a bookmark by specifying an element type.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | bookmarkId | java.lang.String | The bookmark ID. |
|  | bookmarkObject | java.lang.Object | A bookmark object or content. |
|  | elementType | java.lang.String | An element type name, e.g., html tag. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Bookmark | The content with a bookmark. |

****

###### **$bookmark.getBookmarkId(id)**

Return a bookmark ID from a given string.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | id | java.lang.String | An original string value. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Bookmark | The bookmark ID. |

****

###### **$bookmark.createRef(referenceID)**

Create a reference bookmark of any Microsoft Word reference numbered items.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | referenceID | java.lang.String | A reference ID. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.TextReference | The content with a bookmark in the Microsoft Word format. |

****

###### **$bookmark.openRef(referenceID)**

Create a hyperlink for a reference bookmark of any Microsoft Word reference numbered items.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | referenceID | java.lang.String | A reference ID. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.TextReference | The content with a bookmark in the Microsoft Word format. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can press F9 to replace the text content shown as **Refer** with the updated numbered item that it refers to.

****

###### **$bookmark.openRef(referenceID, referenceText)**

Create a hyperlink for a reference bookmark of any Microsoft Word reference numbered items.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | referenceID | java.lang.String | A reference ID. |
|  | referenceText | java.lang.String | The text content. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.TextReference | The content with a bookmark in the Microsoft Word format. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can press F9 to replace the text content shown as **Refer referenceText** with the updated numbered item that it refers to.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7e17993-335b-4695-a82a-ba3614f4f9ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050367121</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a782421-c9bc-4d7b-9eab-cee9d86f167c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050367135</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d853ba0-ad46-4247-adb1-64f7c74396e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050367115</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab4f9a35-a6c1-4036-9b04-feb095c74624"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8c0ba57-57e1-4579-8287-5562f46d022a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050367125</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>$bookmark contains utility functions for bookmarking. The functions of this module are accessible from templates through <strong>$bookmark</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1fb11e59-ea29-4074-9c5b-0b59d7ba3022"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default style of bookmarks depends on the editor used. For example, Microsoft Word 2003 displays hyperlinks in blue.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>$bookmark.openURL(url, content)</strong></h4><p>Create a hyperlink to open a URL.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 59.4634%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 12.9467%;" /><col style="width: 8.79324%;" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990641 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00159: (2026x Refresh1) Boundary Events

- page_id: `303990641`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990641/2026x+Refresh1+Boundary+Events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:50.678+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Boundary Event is an Intermediate event which can be placed on the boundary of any of the following activities:

- SubProcess, Task, or Call Activity
- SubChoreography, Choreography Task, or Call Choreography

Boundary Event indicates that while attached-to Activity is running, event is listening for the trigger signal.

Boundary Event types:

- Interrupting Boundary Event aborts Activity and Process is continued of exceptional flow. This event is drawn with a solid border.
- Non-Interrupting Boundary Event splits process to parallel flows. This event is drawn with a dashed border.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Error Boundary Event attached to SubProcess

Types of Boundary Events are as follows:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Boundary Event is an Intermediate event which can be placed on the boundary of any of the following activities:</p><ul><li>SubProcess, Task, or Call Activity</li><li>SubChoreography, Choreography Task, or Call Choreography</li></ul><p>Boundary Event indicates that while attached-to Activity is running, event is listening for the trigger signal.</p><p>Boundary Event types:</p><ul><li>Interrupting Boundary Event aborts Activity and Process is continued of exceptional flow. This event is drawn with a solid border.</li><li>Non-Interrupting Boundary Event splits process to parallel flows. This event is drawn with a dashed border.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="boundary_error.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Boundary Events" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Error Boundary Event attached to SubProcess</h6><p>Types of Boundary Events are as follows:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="dd5528d2-89f7-4470-8751-3f740afa86c7" /></p>
````

**Description**

A BPMN Association link shows that particular classes are related to each other. BPMN Association links may have names defined – names help to read diagrams. You can also specify multiplicity at the end of a BPMN Association.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Association between two Classes with Multiplicity at the End

**Related diagram**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991269 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00163: (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Choreography Diagram

- page_id: `303991269`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991269/2026x+Refresh1+BPMN+Choreography+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:40.986+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486494018

1486494019

1486494019

**Description**

A Choreography formalizes the way business participants coordinate their interactions. A Choreography is a type of process, but its purpose and behavior are different from a standard BPMN process.

A standard process defines the flow of activities of a specific partner entity or organization. In contrast, a Choreography formalizes the way business participants coordinate their interactions. The focus is not on the work performed within these participants, but rather on the information (messages) exchanged between them.

A Choreography is a definition of expected behavior, basically a procedural business contract between interacting participants. It shows the messages exchanged and their logical relations. This allows business partners to plan their business processes for inter-operation without introducing conflicts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Choreography diagram

A BPMN Choreography diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram and includes restrictions and extensions as defined by BPMN.

Types of Choreography elements are as follows:

1486494017

**Related elements**

- Choreography
- Choreography Activities
- Error
- Intermediate Catch Event
- Intermediate Throwing Event
- Boundary Events
- End Events
- Gateways

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Process Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Using BPMN Choreography Diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d7fcc8e-42db-47fb-8f45-740495ab3b76"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486494018</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a041327-4185-4886-ae2a-d2d0bea387fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486494019</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="78f3a4bb-031e-4ba1-8b01-5d946f36d162"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486494019</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Choreography formalizes the way business participants coordinate their interactions. A Choreography is a type of process, but its purpose and behavior are different from a standard BPMN process.</p><p>A standard process defines the flow of activities of a specific partner entity or organization. In contrast, a Choreography formalizes the way business participants coordinate their interactions. The focus is not on the work performed within these participants, but rather on the information (messages) exchanged between them.</p><p>A Choreography is a definition of expected behavior, basically a procedural business contract between interacting participants. It shows the messages exchanged and their logical relations. This allows business partners to plan their business processes for inter-operation without introducing conflicts.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="choreography_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) BPMN Choreography Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>BPMN Choreography diagram</h6><p>A BPMN Choreography diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram and includes restrictions and extensions as defined by BPMN.</p><p>Types of Choreography elements are as follows:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4d335140-5180-4a64-b441-3516fbfd43f8" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989790 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00164: (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Collaboration Diagram

- page_id: `303989790`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989790/2026x+Refresh1+BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:26.851+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486122303

1486122304

1486122304

**Description**

A Collaboration represents the interactions between two or more business entities. A Collaboration diagram depicts a global point of view. It shows the interactions between participants in general.

A Collaboration contains two or more pools, representing the participants in the collaboration. Messages exchanged between the participants are shown by message flows that connect two pools together (or objects within the pools).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Collaboration diagram with two Black Box Pools

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Collaboration diagram with two Pools showing process

A Collaboration diagram can also show distinct conversations between collaborating participants in a domain. Communications are defined by the conversations, participants, and conversation links between them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Collaboration diagram with Conversations

The elements of a BPMN Process diagram can be displayed on the BPMN Collaboration diagram.

1486122303

**Related element**

- BPMN Process

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Process Diagram

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

A Collaboration diagram depicts a global point of view. It shows the interactions between participants in general.</p><p>A Collaboration contains two or more pools, representing the participants in the collaboration. Messages exchanged between the participants are shown by message flows that connect two pools together (or objects within the pools).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaboration_I.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) BPMN Collaboration Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Collaboration diagram with two Black Box Pools</h6><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaboration_II.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) BPMN Collaboration Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Collaboration diagram with two Pools showing process</h6><p>A Collaboration diagram can also show distinct conversations between collaborating participants in a domain. Communications are defined by the conversations, participants, and conversation links between them.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaboration_III.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) BPMN Collaboration Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>BPMN Collaboration diagram with Conversations</h6><p>The elements of a BPMN Process diagram can be displayed on the BPMN Collaboration diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2bcef008-ec4c-4195-84a8-a37d5e81a3b4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486122303</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related element</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Process">BPMN Process</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00165: (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Data Usage Matrices

- page_id: `303993319`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993319/2026x+Refresh1+BPMN+Data+Usage+Matrices
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:56.528+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Matrices
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A BPMN Data Usage Matrix shows what data classes are used in BPMN Processes and SubProcess. Data Objects represents classes in a BPMN diagram.

A BPMN Data Usage Matrix presents information about all BPMN Processes and SubProcesses in rows and Classes in columns. Each row in the matrix shows if a class is used in a BPMN Process or SubProcess diagram as a Data Object.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Data Usage Matrix

**Related elements**

- BPMN Process
- SubProcesses
- Class
- BPMN Matrices

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Process Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams' space='']
- Dependency Matrix

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Data Objects represents classes in a BPMN diagram.</p><p>A BPMN Data Usage Matrix presents information about all BPMN Processes and SubProcesses in rows and Classes in columns. Each row in the matrix shows if a class is used in a BPMN Process or SubProcess diagram as a Data Object.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="data_usage_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) BPMN Data Usage Matrices" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>BPMN Data Usage Matrix</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="8a678172-4e84-4051-93e8-3ad7905acea2"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Process">BPMN Process</a></li><li data-uuid="3b1e47f1-ae45-4def-8135-3ec2cc6c6c12"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) SubProcesses">SubProcesses</a></li><li data-uuid="adb9546d-6105-42b1-9925-dcc64e6b07fe"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Class">Class</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link" data-uuid="64d5c999-ac19-4c28-9ccc-9b493db1eca3"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Matrices">BPMN Matrices</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagram</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="1c264b88-0dcb-4684-b635-9527890d142d"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="076434e6-dc42-4c53-9a5c-4af2e2c40594"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating a Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="79c6266a-9c9c-44a4-9d26-878d3510dc3a"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Dependency+Matrix">Dependency Matrix</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303989644 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00167: (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling

- page_id: `303989644`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303989644/2026x+Refresh1+BPMN+modeling
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:17:41.296+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Cameo Business Modeler Plugin provides implementation of the Business Process Modeling and Notation (BPMN) standard and the best capability for understanding internal business procedures. The notation includes performance, collaboration, and business transactions in your business between organizations. For service providers and consultants, Cameo Business Modeler Plugin is also the ideal communication tool. Not only do you get the benefits of a standard business notation, but also many others that you and your customer/stakeholder can trust and understand. With collaboration and reporting tools, you can integrate your customer right into your process.

To learn more, see the following topics:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="text-align: left;">The Cameo Business Modeler Plugin provides implementation of the Business Process Modeling and Notation (BPMN) standard and the best capability for understanding internal business procedures. The notation includes performance, collaboration, and business transactions in your business between organizations. For service providers and consultants, Cameo Business Modeler Plugin is also the ideal communication tool. Not only do you get the benefits of a standard business notation, but also many others that you and your customer/stakeholder can trust and understand. With collaboration and reporting tools, you can integrate your customer right into your process.</p><p style="text-align: left;">To learn more, see the following topics:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="d054600b-d5b5-4386-b9be-1f7c6f0d6366" /></p>
````

1485168493

1485168493

**Description**

A BPMN Process element defines a process performed in an organization. This element is a container for the BPMN Process diagram and its elements. Process diagram describes how a process is performed.

**Notation** 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1485168491

**Related elements**

- Tasks
- Process Definition Diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990527 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00169: (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram

- page_id: `303990527`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990527/2026x+Refresh1+BPMN+Process+Diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:28.045+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A BPMN Process Diagram describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business works. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities. A BPMN Process Diagram is similar to the UML Activity diagram with a much richer set of default message types and business process styles of notations.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Process diagram

**Related elements**

- BPMN Process
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A BPMN Process Diagram describes a sequence or flow of activities in an organization that shows how the business works. The diagram shows activities, events, and data that trigger or feed business activities. A BPMN Process Diagram is similar to the UML Activity diagram with a much richer set of default message types and business process styles of notations.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="process_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) BPMN Process Diagram" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">BPMN Process diagram</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="cc08b33f-4bd0-477c-8df8-9cd086810848"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Process">BPMN Process</a></li><li data-uuid="821cbae5-3519-47f0-bbb3-7d94a4a2051c"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating a Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

A BPMN Processes Description table is designed to display or edit processes descriptions in a table form. You can display existing processes or create new processes in this table.

## PAGE 00171: (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Processes Structure Map

- page_id: `303995902`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995902/2026x+Refresh1+BPMN+Processes+Structure+Map
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:58.409+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Supportive Diagrams Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

148567171

148567172

148567172

**Description**

A BPMN Processes Structure Map diagram allows you to represent the structure of processes in a project. The 
processes are grouped into packages on the diagram. And each process may itself contains other processes, 
which in turn may contain other processes. The diagram makes the processes easier to understand and helps you 
analyze how they are organized.

A BPMN Processes Structure Map diagram shows packages, processes, subprocesses, and tasks from a 
selected scope.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Processes Structure Map

148567170

**Related elements**

- Package
- BPMN Process
- Activities

**Related diagram**

- BPMN Process Diagram

**Related external resource**

- Relation Map

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993890 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00173: (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Resources Description Table

- page_id: `303993890`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993890/2026x+Refresh1+BPMN+Resources+Description+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:57.879+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Tables
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A BPMN Resources Description table is designed to display or edit resources description in a table form. You can display existing Resources or create new ones in this table.

BPMN defines four types of Resources as follows:

- Resource
- Organization Unit
- Role
- Person
- Information System

You can display all of them in a BPMN Resources Description table.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Resource Roles Description table

**Related elements**

- Resource
- BPMN Tables

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can display existing Resources or create new ones in this table.</p><p>BPMN defines four types of Resources as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="11ed7d5d-dde7-4511-b77e-4a37222ffdad">Resource</li><li data-uuid="cbadfedd-55c5-4b68-8784-8e6e809782fd">Organization Unit</li><li data-uuid="85db651e-8974-46d5-8528-ad27739b323b">Role</li><li data-uuid="e2aff2e1-d9f6-4e6d-90e6-8596a965c5a3">Person</li><li data-uuid="d1465541-b301-4bd1-8f50-c8ec47954ddc">Information System</li></ul><p>You can display all of them in a BPMN Resources Description table.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_description_table.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) BPMN Resources Description Table" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>BPMN Resource Roles Description table</h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="97cfb87c-bccb-4781-939e-057e40d7afcd"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Resource">Resource</a></li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link" data-uuid="cbe23566-5b8d-45a7-a19c-aeff59a1f6b6"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) BPMN+Tables">BPMN Tables</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="db17430e-d860-40c0-9238-14a9e6030ea7"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating a Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993316 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00174: (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Resources Usage Matrices

- page_id: `303993316`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993316/2026x+Refresh1+BPMN+Resources+Usage+Matrices
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:56.804+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Supportive Diagrams Concepts > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN Matrices
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A BPMN Resources Usage Matrix is designed to review and edit an allocation of Resource Roles for BPMN Activities. The Resource Roles used in an Activity show a Resources property in a BPMN Activity specification dialog.

A BPMN Resources Usage Matrix presents information about BPMN Activities in rows and Resource Roles in columns. Each arrow in the matrix shows if a Resource Role is used by an Activity. 
You can define Resource Roles usage for all BPMN Activities by assigning or removing the Resource Roles to or from the Activities.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### BPMN Resource Usage Matrix

**Related elements**

- BPMN Process
- Activities
- Resource
- Organization Unit
- Role
- Person
- Information system
- BPMN Matrices

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Creating diagrams' space='']
- Dependency Matrix
