<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998349 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00176: (2026x Refresh1) BPMN2 project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud

- page_id: `303998349`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998349/2026x+Refresh1+BPMN2+project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:50.565+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are some issues with the Teamwork Cloud BPMN2 projects migration when those projects contain used projects:

- Used projects are not migrated.
- Used projects are migrated, but not updated to the latest version on Teamwork Cloud.

##### Issues of manual projects migration

Before the migration, you need to make sure you have the necessary permissions. [Learn more about roles and permissions >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Rolesandpermissions)

###### Used projects are not migrated

If you are trying to migrate a project containing not migrated used projects, you will get a notification informing you which used projects are not migrated. To proceed with the migration of the main project, you need to migrate the used projects mentioned in the notification message first.

###### Used projects are migrated, but not updated

If you are trying to migrate a project containing used projects not updated to the latest version, you will get a notification informing you which used projects are not updated. In this case, you can choose from the following options:

- Update and Migrate. Used projects will be updated to the latest version, and the project will be migrated. This option is strongly recommended.
- Migrate. The main project will be migrated without updating the used projects to the latest version. In this case, the used projects will remain outdated, and you will not be able to work with them.
- Open as Read-Only. The migration is canceled, and the project opens as read-only. In this case, the project will not be migrated.

For more information about manual project migration, see [Migrating server projects manually](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/243969919/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsmanually).

##### Issues of automatic project migration

Before the migration, you need to make sure you have the necessary permissions. [Learn more about roles and permissions >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Rolesandpermissions)

If the main project contains used projects, you need to migrate the used projects first. Then you can perform the automatic migration of the main project. After the migration is complete, you need to switch your used project versions to the latest (if the latest versions were used before migration).

When you migrate projects automatically and receive a message that some projects were not migrated, it might be that those projects are using other projects. In this case, do one of the following:

- Migrate the listed projects manually.
- Repeat the automatic migration.

For more information about automatic project migration, see [Migrating server projects automatically](https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/243969919/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsautomatically).

##### Roles and permissions

For the migration of server projects, certain roles and permissions are needed. Before migrating, make sure you have **Resource Locks Administrator** and **Resource Manager roles.**Also, you need to have **Administer Resources** and **Release Resource Locks** permissions.

To learn more, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='MCS'].

For more information about migrating server projects, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Migrating Server Projects' space=''].

##### Special migration options

There are special migration options for the server projects:

- You can suspend the used projects updating by adding **-Dsuspend.modules.update.during.migration\=false** in the properties file and set it to *true*.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are some issues with the Teamwork Cloud BPMN2 projects migration when those projects contain used projects:</p><ul><li data-uuid="a6b4a283-d2b3-46c2-8a83-6236adb2791f">Used projects are not migrated.</li><li data-uuid="70d4a416-1623-42c8-9f9e-d21c38c76caa">Used projects are migrated, but not updated to the latest version on Teamwork Cloud.</li></ul><h3>Issues of manual projects migration</h3><p>Before the migration, you need to make sure you have the necessary permissions. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Rolesandpermissions">Learn more about roles and permissions &gt;&gt;</a></p><h4>Used projects are not migrated</h4><p>If you are trying to migrate a project containing not migrated used projects, you will get a notification informing you which used projects are not migrated. In this case, you can choose from the following options:</p><ul><li data-uuid="2a23389b-79df-4f92-ad2f-b1fa6a848637"><strong>Update and Migrate.</strong> Used projects will be updated to the latest version, and the project will be migrated. This option is <strong>strongly recommended.</strong></li><li data-uuid="08f82289-3578-431c-98c7-929c3c81c115"><strong>Migrate.</strong> The main project will be migrated without updating the used projects to the latest version. In this case, the used projects will remain outdated, and you will not be able to work with them.</li><li data-uuid="b7b7c401-b52f-47d3-aff0-100190ee750d"><strong>Open as Read-Only.</strong> The migration is canceled, and the project opens as read-only. In this case, the project will not be migrated.</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c23af08a-a384-4796-96e7-915a630379f1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about manual project migration, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/243969919/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsmanually">Migrating server projects manually</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Issues of automatic project migration</h3><p>Before the migration, you need to make sure you have the necessary permissions. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Project+migration+issues+on+Teamwork+Cloud#ProjectmigrationissuesonTeamworkCloud-Rolesandpermissions">Learn more about roles and permissions &gt;&gt;</a></p><p>If the main project contains used projects, you need to migrate the used projects first. Then you can perform the automatic migration of the main project. After the migration is complete, you need to switch your used project versions to the latest (if the latest versions were used before migration).</p><p>When you migrate projects automatically and receive a message that some projects were not migrated, it might be that those projects are using other projects. In this case, do one of the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5e0360d8-0ec2-4ff1-b0bf-2668e2b91fed">Migrate the listed projects manually.</li><li data-uuid="2d66794a-0f14-49a0-b605-9ac1885d8161">Repeat the automatic migration. </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0999d47e-f1b2-4015-a180-a5101015706a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about automatic project migration, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/CM/pages/243969919/Migrating+projects#Migratingprojects-Migratingserverprojectsautomatically">Migrating server projects automatically</a>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Roles and permissions </h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">For the migration of server projects, certain roles and permissions are needed. Before migrating, make sure you have <strong>Resource Locks Administrator</strong> and <strong>Resource Manager roles. </strong>Also, you need to have <strong>Administer Resources</strong> and <strong>Release Resource Locks</strong> permissions.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">To learn more, see<span> </span></span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MCS" ri:content-title="Permissions" /></ac:link>.</p><p>For more information about migrating server projects, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Migrating Server Projects" /></ac:link>.</p><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="568112ed-f121-476c-a7db-92a29bf6b046">Special migration options</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>There are special migration options for the server projects:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5023e6e1-9353-4cd6-b020-1476efaabd53">You can suspend the used projects updating by adding <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>-Dsuspend.modules.update.during.migration\=false</strong> in the properties file and set it to <em>true</em>.</span> </li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303998561 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00177: (2026x Refresh1) BPMN2 XML support

- page_id: `303998561`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303998561/2026x+Refresh1+BPMN2+XML+support
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:47:44.512+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) BPMN modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Business Process Model and Notation Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486966503

1486966503

1486966503

Cameo Business Modeler supports exporting BPMN2 models as BPMN2 Diagram Interchange files. The BPMN2 Diagram Interchange is a format for interchanging BPMN2 diagrams between tools. Its reduced ambiguity allows the users to exchange BPMN models between tools more conveniently. You can export a BPMN2 diagram as a BPMN2 Diagram Interchange file (BPMN2 XMI).

1486966502

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Exporting Models to BPMN2

## PAGE 00178: (2026x Refresh1) BPMNMessage

- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486303594

1486303595

1486303595

**Description**

A BPMNMessage represents the content of communications between two participants. It is passed by a message flow and is sent or received by a message event.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1486303593

**Related elements**

- Message Flow
- Choreography Task

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

- Creating and Using Choreography Task

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999898 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00179: (2026x Refresh1) Building AcV-1 matrix

- page_id: `303999898`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999898/2026x+Refresh1+Building+AcV-1+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:21.481+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF Acquisition Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF. AcV-1 Acquisition Clusters
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an OV-4 before creating an AcV-1.

The AcV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person) for Actual Projects.

The rows in the AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person) and the columns represent Actual Projects.

To build an AcV-1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an OV-4 before creating an AcV-1.</p><p>The AcV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person) for Actual Projects.</p><p>The rows in the AcV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person) and the columns represent Actual Projects.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build an AcV-1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Person).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303997359 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00180: (2026x Refresh1) Building metric definitions

- page_id: `303997359`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303997359/2026x+Refresh1+Building+metric+definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:28:05.480+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Metric Suites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1891786832

1891786834

1891786833

Metric definition is the attribute of a Metric Suite with the «MetricDefinition» stereotype applied.

To build a metric definition

1. Select the shape of a Metric Suite.
2. Click the Create Element button on the shape, and then select Metric Definition . An attribute with the «MetricDefinition» stereotype is created. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_metric_definition.png']
3. Type its name directly on the shape and then click a free space on the diagram.
4. Select the attribute on the shape and click [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_type_button.png'] .
5. Select the type of the result values that should be calculated according to the formula specified in a metric definition. This step is mandatory. A metric definition must have a result type, e.g., Real or Integer.
6. Press **Enter** to open the Specification window of the metric definition.
7. Click the cell of the Default Value property value. [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] and [ATTACHMENT filename='show_shortcut_menu.png'] appear on the cell.
8. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='show_shortcut_menu.png'] and select Value Specification > Opaque Expression .
9. Click the cell of the **Default Value** property value again, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Default Value** dialog opens.
10. From the **Language** drop-down list, select a language. To build a structured expression, select **StructuredExpression**.
11. In the **Body** box, specify the formula for the metric definition. For more information about building structured expressions, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''].
12. Click OK and then click Close in the Specification window.

The metric definition is specified.

If your Metric Suite is created using the *BaseMetricSuite*, it already has the metric definition for date tracking.

One metric definition can refer to another metric definition of either the same Metric Suite or a more general one (in case there is the Generalization relationship between these Metric Suites).

1891786831

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metric Suites' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding a new Metric Suite' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specify the target for the Metric Suite' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying parameter definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specific use cases' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Building validation-based metric definitions' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991760 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00181: (2026x Refresh1) Building Modelica libraries in SysML v1

- page_id: `303991760`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991760/2026x+Refresh1+Building+Modelica+libraries+in+SysML+v1
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:16.647+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) SysML v1 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) Exporting to External Simulation Models > (2026x Refresh1) Modelica export
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Introduction

The Modelica library allows quickly to build SysML v1 models for simulation by reusing library elements. It provides model components in many domains that are based on standardized interface definitions. The library components are ready to use and shareable between applications. [Find all modelica libraries >>](https://www.modelica.org/libraries)

##### Importing the Modelica Standard Library

The Modelica Standard Library brings the maincomponentsand standard component interfaces from many engineering domains. It provides theblack-boxes of Modelica blocks that contains only ports and parameters.The Modelica Standard Library components are imported to SysML v1 project as follow:

- All Modelica types have «ModelicaBlock» stereotype applied with a full Modelica type name specified in the Name property.
- Modelica blocks are created as SysML Blocks.
- Modelica parameters are created as SysML Value Properties.
- Modelica connectors are created as SysML Interface Blocks.
- Primitive types are created as Value Types.
- If Modelica connector has property with flow keyword defined, it is created as SysML Flow Property for an Interface Block.
- For Block (defined as Model in Modelica) import and create only ports (no keyword, type is Modelica Connector/SysML InterfaceBlock) and parameters as ValueProperties.
- Units and quantities are mapped to ISO80000 units and quantities.

All modelica libraries depend from the Modelica Standard Library because of references between components.That's why the first thing you nee to do before using Modelica libraries in SysML v1 projects, is to import the Modelica Standard Library.

impTo import Modelica Standard Library

1. [Download](https://github.com/modelica/ModelicaStandardLibrary/releases) the Modelica Standard Library and extract it.
2. Open a project in which you want to use the Modelica library components.
3. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the**File**>**Import From**> **Modelica Library**.
4. Select the *ModelicaStandardLibrary > Modelica* folder from your file system.
5. Click **Import**.The Modelica Standard Library is imported. See the following figure.All packages with their structure is createdin the Containment treeand types are placed in corresponding packages. Note, that file names may do not match with the element names and the file structure may do not correspond to the location in the directory. The package structure is the same as Modelica package structure. In order to to avoid the import of Modelica Standard Library in each project, you can save it as a project and reuse in other projects. For this, do the following:[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Sharing project data' space='']>]]>[https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Sharing+project+data](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Sharing+project+data)[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using other projects in a project' space='']>]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Using Modelica libraries

Let's say we need to use *OpenHydraulics* modelica library components in a project. Both *Modelica Standard Library* and *OpenHydraulics* library**must be imported since some components from OpenHydraulics library have references to standard library.

To use the Modelica libraries in SysML v1 project

1. Create new or open existing SysML v1 project.
2. Import the Modelica Standard Library . >]]>
3. [Download](https://github.com/modelica-3rdparty/OpenHydraulics) the *OpenHydraulics*library and extract it.
4. I n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the File > Import From > Modelica Library .
5. Select the OpenHydraulics folder from your file system.
6. Click Import . 
The *OpenHydraulics* library is imported. The figure below demonstrates the reference between components from *OpenHydraulics* and *Modelica Standard Libraries*: the *RealOuput* type of a Proxy Port *y* which is in *OpenHydraulics* library references to *Modelica Standard Library*. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can create model by dragging Modelica library elements directly on diagram pane. As shown in the figure below, the simple schema is created by dragging *Capacitor* and *Resistor* Blocks on IBD diagram pane and displaying all their ports. To complete schema creation, you need to type names *c* and *r* for a Part Properties and connect ports.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The figure below shows exported model to Modelica filewhich already contains references to the Modelica library components.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Select the <em>ModelicaStandardLibrary &gt; Modelica</em> folder from your file system.</span></span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Click <strong>Import</strong>. <br /></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Modelica Standard Library is imported. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">See the following figure. </span>All packages with their structure is created </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">in the Containment tree </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">and types are placed in corresponding packages. Note, that <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">file names may do not match with the element names and the file structure <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">may do not correspond to the location in the directory. The package structure is the same as Modelica package structure.</span></span> <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a6ad0782-03b1-4106-8069-2635b36aa560"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In order to to avoid the import of Modelica Standard Library in each project, you can save it as a project and reuse in other projects. For this, do the following:</span></p><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Sharing project data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Share project data >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Sharing+project+data"><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Use other projects in a project >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="imported_modelica_library.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Building Modelica libraries in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Using Modelica libraries</h3><p>Let's say we need to use <em>OpenHydraulics</em> modelica library components in a project. Both <em>Modelica Standard Library</em> and <em>OpenHydraulics</em> library<em> </em>must be imported since some components from OpenHydraulics library have references to standard library. </p><p>To use the Modelica libraries in SysML v1 project</p><hr /><ol><li>Create new or open existing SysML v1 project.</li><li>Import the <em>Modelica Standard Library</em>. <ac:link ac:anchor="imp"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Follow the procedure above >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><a href="https://github.com/modelica-3rdparty/OpenHydraulics">D<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">ownload</span></a></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> the <em>OpenHydraulics </em>library and extract it.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">I</span>n the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">File</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Import From</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> &gt; </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Modelica Library</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span></li><li>Select the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">OpenHydraulics</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> folder from your file system.</span></li><li>Click <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Import</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">. <br />The <em>OpenHydraulics</em> library is imported. The figure below demonstrates the reference between components from <em>OpenHydraulics</em> and <em>Modelica Standard Libraries</em>: the <em>RealOuput</em> type of a Proxy Port <em>y</em> which is in <em>OpenHydraulics</em> library references to <em>Modelica Standard Library</em>.</span><br /><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="openhydraulicsLib_component_reference_to_modelicaStandardLib_v2.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Building Modelica libraries in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can create model by dragging Modelica library elements directly on diagram pane. As shown in the figure below, the simple schema is created by dragging <em>Capacitor</em> and <em>Resistor</em> Blocks on IBD diagram pane and displaying all their ports. To complete schema creation, you need to type names <em>c</em> and <em>r</em>  for a Part Properties and connect ports.</p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_element_from_modelica_library.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Building Modelica libraries in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The figure below shows exported model <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">to Modelica file </span>which already contains references to the Modelica library components.</span></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exported_modelica_lib_model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Building Modelica libraries in SysML v1" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000558 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00182: (2026x Refresh1) Building NCV-6 matrix

- page_id: `304000558`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000558/2026x+Refresh1+Building+NCV-6+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:24.734+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NCV-6 Capability to Operational Activity Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity to Capability Mapping (NCV-6) describes the mapping between the Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities supportdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations' space=''].

The rows of the matrix are Standard Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.

To build a NCV-6 matrix

1. Specify the rows scope (Standard Operational Activities).
2. Specify the columns scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.

To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability, click the intersection between the desired elements and click one more time, the relation will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity to Capability Mapping (NCV-6) <span>describes the mapping between the Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities support </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>.</span></p><p>The rows of the matrix are Standard Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NCV-6 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the rows scope (Standard Operational Activities).</li><li>Specify the columns scope (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Standard Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.</p><p>To map Standard Operational Activity to Capability, click the intersection between the desired elements and click one more time, the relation will be deleted.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000520 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00183: (2026x Refresh1) Building NCV-7 matrix

- page_id: `304000520`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000520/2026x+Refresh1+Building+NCV-7+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:22.668+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Capability Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NCV-7 Capability to Service Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Capability to Services Mapping Matrix (NCV-7)****depicts which Servicescontribute to the achievement of a Capabilitydirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations' space=''].

The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Capabilities.

To build a NCV-7 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Services).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Refresh button.

Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To map aServiceto a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Capability to Services Mapping Matrix (NCV-7)<strong> </strong><span>depicts which Services </span><span>contribute to the achievement of a Capability </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>.</span></p><p>The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NCV-7 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the Row Scope (Services).</li><li>Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities). </li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh </strong>button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Service </span>to a Capability</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000524 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00184: (2026x Refresh1) Building NPV-1 matrix

- page_id: `304000524`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000524/2026x+Refresh1+Building+NPV-1+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:32:23.490+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Programme Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

It is recommended to create an NOV-4 before creating an NPV-1.

The NPV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects. The rows in the NPV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post) and the columns represent Actual Projects.

To build an NPV-1 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope ().
2. Specify the Column Scope (Actual Projects).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>It is recommended to create an NOV-4 before creating an NPV-1.</p><p>The NPV-1 is an editable matrix displaying the responsibility of Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organizations or Actual Posts) for Actual Projects. The rows in the NPV-1 Responsibility Matrix represent Actual Organizational resources (Actual Organization or Actual Post) and the columns represent Actual Projects.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build an NPV-1 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> ().</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990760 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00185: (2026x Refresh1) Building NPV-2 matrix

- page_id: `303990760`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990760/2026x+Refresh1+Building+NPV-2+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:56.647+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Programme Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NPV-2 Programme to Capability Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**NPV-2 Programme to Capability Mapping** describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability.

The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.

To build a NPV-2 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Actual Projects).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix.

By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:

- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.
- Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources) . The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.

You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the **Specification** window.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>NPV-2 Programme to Capability Mapping</strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">describes the mapping of programs and projects to capabilities to show how the specific projects and program elements help to achieve a capability. </span></p><p>The rows of this matrix are Actual Projects and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NPV-2 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Actual Projects).</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Actual Project maps to Capability through several transitive relationships which are displayed in the Matrix. </p><p>By default, the traceability is shown according to the following criteria:</p><ul><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources and Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources (milestone property) in which type is exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Resources Performers)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Resources (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Service Specification)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Service Specifications (version releasedAtMilestone and versionWithdrawn properties) which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li><li><strong>Mapping Using Milestone (Actual Resources)</strong>. The Actual Project owns Actual Project Milestones (Owned Milestones property) that are related to Actual Resources which are exhibiting (Exhibits relationship) Capabilities.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b597bf3-c2a4-4b05-bcf3-8fb746561ed2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You may hide some mapping rules from a Matrix by changing the Dependency criteria in the <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="mapping_criteria.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Building NPV-2 matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990849 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00186: (2026x Refresh1) Building NSOV-3 matrix

- page_id: `303990849`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990849/2026x+Refresh1+Building+NSOV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:04.443+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix purpose is to provide traceability by illustrating which Services support which Operational Activities.

The Rows of this matrix are Services and the Columns are Operational Activities.

To build a NSOV-3 matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Services);
2. Specify Column Scope (Operational Activities);
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

Operational Activities map to Services using a Supports relationship.

To map Operational Activitiesto Services

Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">purpose is to provide traceability by illustrating which Services support which Operational Activities.</span></p><p>The Rows of this matrix are Services and the Columns are Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NSOV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify<strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services);</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities);</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Building NSOV-3 matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Operational Activities map to Services using a Supports relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Operational Activities </span>to Services</p><hr /><p>Double-click an empty intersection.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><p>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995164 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00187: (2026x Refresh1) Building NSV-12 Service Provision matrix

- page_id: `303995164`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995164/2026x+Refresh1+Building+NSV-12+Service+Provision+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:17.734+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NSV-12 Service Provision
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows in a NSV-12 matrix represent Resource Performers and the columns represent Service Interfaces.

To build a NSV-12 matrix

1. Specify the Rows scope (Resource Performers).
2. Specify the Columns scope (Service Interfaces).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon will be displayed.

- Click an empty cell to create a new Service Port.
- Click the cell again to delete the Service Port and create a new Request Port will.
- Click the cell again to delete the Request Port and clear the cell.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The rows in a NSV-12 matrix represent Resource Performers and the columns represent Service Interfaces.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NSV-12 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the<strong> Rows scope</strong> (Resource Performers).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Columns scope</strong> (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon will be displayed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a247679b-8dbc-485c-8372-57b68d8fbd26"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Click an empty cell to create a new Service Port.</li><li>Click the cell again to delete the Service Port and create a new Request Port will.</li><li>Click the cell again to delete the Request Port and clear the cell.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995186 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00188: (2026x Refresh1) Building NSV-3 matrix

- page_id: `303995186`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995186/2026x+Refresh1+Building+NSV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:21.280+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NSV-3 System to System Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems to Systems Matrix (NSV-3) shows how resources interact with each other directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations' space=''].NSV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. NSV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To build a NSV-3 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems to Systems Matrix (NSV-3) <span>shows how resources interact with each other directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span>NSV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>Arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. NSV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NSV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303995299 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00189: (2026x Refresh1) Building NSV-5 matrix

- page_id: `303995299`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303995299/2026x+Refresh1+Building+NSV-5+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:25:25.894+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF > (2026x Refresh1) NATO Systems Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) NSV-5 System Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Systems to Systems Matrix (NSV-3) shows how resources interact with each other directly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations' space=''].

The rows of the NSV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

To build a NSV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the **Implements** relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

- Click the intersection between the desired elements.

Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Systems to Systems Matrix (NSV-3) <span>shows how resources interact with each other directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p><p>The rows of the NSV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a NSV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the <strong>Implements</strong> relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f97df36e-81e0-4ddb-b156-7ea0ee72aae4"><ac:rich-text-body>Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999951 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00190: (2026x Refresh1) Building SOV-3 matrix

- page_id: `303999951`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999951/2026x+Refresh1+Building+SOV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:29.221+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF. SOV-3 Capability to Service Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Capability to Service Mapping View (SOV-3) depicts which Service contribute to the achievement of a Capabilitydirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations' space=''].

To build a SOV-3 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Services).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Refresh button.

Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To map aServiceto a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Capability to Service Mapping View (SOV-3) <span>depicts which Service contribute to the achievement of a Capability </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To build a SOV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the Row Scope (Services).</li><li>Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities). </li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh </strong>button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Services exhibit Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Service </span>to a Capability</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303999892 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00191: (2026x Refresh1) Building StV-6 matrix

- page_id: `303999892`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303999892/2026x+Refresh1+Building+StV-6+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:31:20.416+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF. StV-6 Operational Activity to Capability Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Activity to Capability Mapping (StV-6) describes the mapping between the Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities supportdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations' space=''].

The rows of the matrix are Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.

To build a StV-6 matrix

1. Specify the rows scope (Operational Activities).
2. Specify the columns scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.

To map Operational Activity to Capability, click the intersection between the desired elements and click one more time, the relation will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Activity to Capability Mapping (StV-6) <span>describes the mapping between the Capabilities required by an Enterprise and the Operational Activities that those Capabilities support </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>.</span></p><p>The rows of the matrix are Operational Activities and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a StV-6 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the rows scope (Operational Activities).</li><li>Specify the columns scope (Capabilities).</li><li>Click the Rebuild button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps to Capability relationship.</p><p>To map Operational Activity to Capability, click the intersection between the desired elements and click one more time, the relation will be deleted.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303993027 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00192: (2026x Refresh1) Building SV-12 Service Provision matrix

- page_id: `303993027`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303993027/2026x+Refresh1+Building+SV-12+Service+Provision+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:22:43.335+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF. SV-12 Service Provision
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The rows in a SV-12 matrix represent Resource Performers and the columns represent Service Interfaces.

To build a SV-12 matrix

1. Specify the Rows scope (Resource Performers).
2. Specify the Columns scope (Service Interfaces).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon will be displayed.

- Click an empty cell to create a new Service Port.
- Click the cell again to delete the Service Port and create a new Request Port will.
- Click the cell again to delete the Request Port and clear the cell.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The rows in a SV-12 matrix represent Resource Performers and the columns represent Service Interfaces.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a SV-12 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the Rows scope (Resource Performers).</li><li>Specify the Columns scope (Service Interfaces).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The cells in the matrix represent a Service or Request Port. If a resource provides a Service Interface, the Service Port icon will be displayed. If it requests a Service Interface, the Request Port icon will be displayed.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5789cb3-91f1-4040-a57c-9fe74ea5b33d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Click an empty cell to create a new Service Port.</li><li>Click the cell again to delete the Service Port and create a new Request Port will.</li><li>Click the cell again to delete the Request Port and clear the cell.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303990736 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00193: (2026x Refresh1) Building SV-3 matrix

- page_id: `303990736`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303990736/2026x+Refresh1+Building+SV-3+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:19:55.075+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF. SV-3 Resource Interaction Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Resource Interaction Matrix (SV-3) shows how resources interact twith each otherdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations' space=''].SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.

An arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.

To build a SV-3 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (System Resources).
2. Specify the Column Scope (System Resources).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Resource Interaction Matrix (SV-3) <span>shows how resources interact twith each other </span><span>directly or using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span>. </span>SV-3 is an editable matrix where cells of the matrix represent a Resource interactions and their headers represent Resources.</p><p>An arrow can be positioned on one side of the matrix or the other depending on the direction of the Resource interaction between resources. SV-3 matrix consists of as many rows and columns as there are Resources in the matrix data source.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a SV-3 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Specify the<strong> Column Scope</strong> (System Resources).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303991256 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00194: (2026x Refresh1) Building SV-5 matrix

- page_id: `303991256`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303991256/2026x+Refresh1+Building+SV-5+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:20:32.692+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF Systems Viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) MODAF. SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5) addresses thedirect and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations' space=''])linkage between Functions described in SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5.

The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.

To build a SV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Functions).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Functions implements Operational Activities using the **Implements** relationship.

To map a Function to an Operational Activity

- Click the intersection between the desired elements.

Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5) <span>addresses the </span><span>direct and indirect (using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x Refresh1) Using Implied Relations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[implied relations]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) </span><span>linkage between Functions described in SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in OV-5.</span></p><p>The rows of the SV-5 matrix represent Functions and the columns represent Operational Activities.</p><p><br /></p><p>To build a SV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Functions).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Functions implements Operational Activities using the <strong>Implements</strong> relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a Function to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the intersection between the desired elements.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1c59401e-36e3-4dd4-8e69-d6cfa036ddac"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Click the intersection again and the relation will be deleted.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304000726 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 00195: (2026x Refresh1) Building the S4 Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `304000726`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/304000726/2026x+Refresh1+Building+the+S4+Services+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:48:23.054+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) UAF 1.3 modeling > (2026x Refresh1) NAF 4.0 > (2026x Refresh1) Service viewpoint > (2026x Refresh1) S4 Service Functions
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix describes the directly or using the [implied relations](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Using+Implied+Relations) mapping between the Services and Operational Activities. The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Operational Activities****

To create S4 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Services).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click the Rebuild button.

Services maps to Operational Activities using the Supports relationship.

To map Services to Operational Activities

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix describes the directly or using the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/(2026x Refresh1) Using+Implied+Relations">implied relations</a> mapping between the Services and Operational Activities. The rows of this matrix represent Services and the columns represent Operational Activities<strong> </strong></p><p><br /></p><p>To create S4 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Services).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click the <strong>Rebuild</strong> button.</li></ol><p>Services maps to Operational Activities using the Supports relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map Services to Operational Activities</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=303996737 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 00196: (2026x Refresh1) Building validation-based metric definitions

- page_id: `303996737`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/303996737/2026x+Refresh1+Building+validation-based+metric+definitions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-28T10:27:17.733+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Modeling Tools Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) General modeling features > (2026x Refresh1) Tools > (2026x Refresh1) Metric Suites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Displaying failing elements

1905924857

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Displaying failing elements

1905924859

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Displaying failing elements

1905924858

A validation-based metric definition is the attribute of a Metric Suite with the «ValidationBasedMetricDefinition» stereotype applied. In this attribute, you can specify a predefined validation rule for calculating metrics according to the parameter values.

800450

To build a validation-based metric definition

1. Select the shape of a Metric Suite, click the smart manipulator on the shape, and select Validation-Based Metric Definition . An attribute with the «ValidationBasedMetricDefinition» stereotype is created. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_validation_based_metric_definition.png']
2. Type the name of the attribute directly on the shape and then click a free space on the diagram.
3. Select the newly created validation-based metric definition on the Metric Suite shape and click [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_type_button.png'] .
4. Select the type of the result values that should be calculated according to the validation rule specified in the validation-based metric definition. Specifying a result typeThis step is mandatory. A validation-based metric definition must have a result type, e.g., Real or Integer.
5. Open the Specification window of the validation-based metric definition.
6. Select the specification cell of the Validation Rule property and click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_short.png'] .
7. In the **Select Constraint** dialog, select the validation rule that will be used to calculate metrics. Click **OK**.
8. Select the specification cell of the Metric Type property. In the drop down list select the type of the metric you want to calculate.
9. Optionally, select the specification cell of the **Failing Elements Definition** property and enter the name of the menu item that displays failing elements according to the validation rule selected in step 7. Displaying failing elementsTo find the menu item that displays failing elements, right-click the Metric Table cell displaying the metric according to the selected validation rule. By default the menu item is **Display Failing Elements**, unless you give it a different name as described in step 9.

The validation-based metric definition for a Metric Suite is now specified and you can start calculating Metrics by using a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metric table' space=''].

1905924856

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Metric Suites' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Adding a new Metric Suite' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specify the target for the Metric Suite' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specifying parameter definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Building metric definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='(2026x Refresh1) Specific use cases' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
